source.subject: Onsite demo — Brookfield itinerary clash / alternate options
messages:
From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed onsite demo week of June 8 — agenda attached

Hi Emily,

Thanks again for the time on Friday. Per our conversation we penciled an onsite walkthrough for Brookfield the week of June 8 — I wanted to confirm whether Thursday, June 10 (10:00–11:30 PT) still works for your team.

Attached is the draft agenda (BROOKFIELD_agenda_v2.pdf) — we’ll do a short product walkthrough, 30-minute technical deep-dive, and a 20-minute ops/implementation discussion. If the timing is tight we can move the deep-dive to a follow-up remote session.

Key asks: can you confirm headcount and whether Brookfield IT needs to be on the call for the demo environment?

If June 10 is no-go, we can push to the following week or record the walkthrough for asynchronous review — happy to be flexible.

Best,
Chloe Martin
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0194

Attachments: BROOKFIELD_agenda_v2.pdf
From: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed onsite demo week of June 8 — agenda attached

Hi Chloe — thanks for the agenda and for lining this up. Quick heads up: I’ll be out of office on a pre-planned trip from June 8–13 (personal travel) and won’t be available for an onsite the week you proposed. We also have a field event on June 9 that pulled two of my leads away.

Options that would work on our side:
- Move the onsite to week of June 15 (preferably June 16)
- Or, record the walkthrough the week of June 8 and schedule a 45-minute live Q&A the week of June 15

If you need an alternate on-site day earlier, our Senior Clinical Ops manager Sarah Li can cover on June 10 but she’s limited to the ops/discuss portion — she won’t be able to join the technical deep-dive.

Also confirm whether the recording will include the technical segment — Ben (SE) will need to see the model latency numbers live if possible.

Thanks,
Emily Zhao
Director, Clinical Operations | Brookfield Health
emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com
From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 27 May 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed onsite demo week of June 8 — agenda attached

Thanks for the quick reply, Emily — totally understood, hope the trip is good.

Ben and Priya — looping you both for availability and a quick read on the two options Emily proposed. Short thoughts from our side:
- We can record the full walkthrough the week of June 8 and produce a 20–25 minute technical-cut that highlights performance and latency numbers. We typically include the performance dashboard exports (screenshot attached).
- For the live Q&A week of June 15, we can book a 45-minute slot that focuses on architecture and rollout (Ben would do the tech portion).

If Brookfield is comfortable with a recorded technical segment, that’ll let us keep momentum while Emily is out. Ben — are you able to join a live Q&A on June 16 (10:00–10:45 PT)? If not, what’s your soonest after June 15?

Emily — happy to confirm a hybrid plan: recorded deep-dive June 8–9, live Q&A June 16. We can also hold a short ops-only on-site with Sarah for any compliance or facility questions if your team prefers some in-person time while you’re away.

-Chloe

On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 09:18 AM Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Chloe — thanks for the agenda and for lining this up. Quick heads up: I’ll be out of office on a pre-planned trip from June 8–13 (personal travel) and won’t be available for an onsite the week you proposed. We also have a field event on June 9 that pulled two of my leads away.
> 
> Options that would work on our side:
> - Move the onsite to week of June 15 (preferably June 16)
> - Or, record the walkthrough the week of June 8 and schedule a 45-minute live Q&A the week of June 15
> 
> If you need an alternate on-site day earlier, our Senior Clinical Ops manager Sarah Li can cover on June 10 but she’s limited to the ops/discuss portion — she won’t be able to join the technical deep-dive.
> 
> Also confirm whether the recording will include the technical segment — Ben (SE) will need to see the model latency numbers live if possible.
> 
> Thanks,
> Emily
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed onsite demo week of June 8 — agenda attached

Quick note — I can cover the live technical Q&A on June 16 at 10:00 PT. I have travel June 14–15 but will be back in time for the 16th morning. If you’d prefer June 15 afternoon instead, I’m booked until 4pm PT.

Recording is fine for the deep-dive; I’ll prepare an edited cut that surfaces latency and throughput charts and include a short narrated section on our batching/caching approach. I’ll upload the snippet and a PDF tech appendix to the shared folder.

Ben
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

On Wed, May 27, 2026 at 10:05 AM Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the quick reply, Emily — totally understood, hope the trip is good.
From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@brookfieldhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Jun 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed onsite demo week of June 8 — agenda attached

Emily — confirming plan per your preference:

1) We will record the full product walkthrough the week of June 8 and deliver the edited technical segment by Tuesday, June 9 (Ben will include latency/throughput visuals).
2) Live Q&A with Ben (technical) + Priya (implementation) on Tuesday, June 16 at 10:00–10:45 PT.
3) If Sarah (ops) wants a short on-site ops only session while you’re away, we can float a 30-minute slot on June 10 — let us know and we’ll coordinate.

Emily — thanks again. I’ll send a calendar invite for June 16 and a link to the recorded video once Ben uploads it.

Best,
Chloe

Attachments referenced: availability-screenshot.png

source.subject: Sapphire Systems — PO issuance audit, spend cap and SKU mapping
messages:
From: Samantha Lee <samantha@sapphiresystems.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sapphire onboarding — PO needed and line item mismatch

Hi Grace / Priya —

Thanks again for the kickoff yesterday. We're excited to get Sapphire set up in your vendor portal. A couple of things we noticed while completing the onboarding checklist and draft invoice: 

- The onboarding form (attached) shows the requested monthly spend cap at $75,000 to cover forecasted usage for June–Sept.
- Your procurement export shows a provisional spend cap of $50,000, which will cause our billing to split across two POs and create two invoice lines.
- On the draft invoice we sent, the SKU mapping for "RU-Compute-XL" and "RU-Storage-Standard" were merged under a single line (item code 1003). Sapphire needs them split to match our ledger.

Can you confirm the PO number that will be issued so we can route invoices correctly? If procurement needs anything else from us to approve the higher spend band, I can provide an updated forecasting workbook and customer references.

Attached: Sapphire_Onboard_Form.pdf (vendor-complete)

Thanks,
Samantha Lee
Vendor Enablement Manager
Sapphire Systems
+1 415 555 0198

From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@sapphiresystems.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sapphire onboarding — PO needed and line item mismatch

Samantha —

Thanks for the clear summary and for attaching the completed form. I'm looping in Ben from Vendor Ops and Priya on procurement intake so we can reconcile the spend cap and SKU split. Preliminary answers below: 

- I see the request for $75k/month; our default vendor cap for new partners is $50k until credit/usage history is established. We can raise that to $75k provisionally with Finance approval.
- PO numbers are assigned by Finance once Vendor Ops submits the intake — Ben, can you confirm whether we should request a single PO for $75k or two POs to match current system limits?
- From a processing perspective, please resend the invoice with separate lines for RU-Compute-XL and RU-Storage-Standard (separate SKU codes). That will speed matching on our side.

I'll attach the internal onboarding checklist and the vendor intake ticket ID in my follow-up. For now, Samantha, please confirm whether Sapphire can accept a provisional PO with the higher spend band marked as "provisional — subject to credit review".

Best,
Grace O'Connor
Procurement Lead
Redwood Inference
grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@sapphiresystems.com>
Date: Wed, 27 May 2026 14:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sapphire onboarding — PO needed and line item mismatch

Grace / Priya —

I submitted a PO request yesterday at 11:00 to Finance with the intake packet (ticket #SUP-2147). Quick notes from Vendor Ops: 

- I included the requested $75k/month and a note that Finance approval is required to override the default $50k cap.
- The vendor-provided invoice had the two SKUs merged; I asked Sapphire to reissue with separate lines (Samantha — thanks for agreeing to resend).
- DocuSign link for the standard vendor terms was included in the request (see link below).

Attachments: PO_request_sapphire.docx (internal summary)
Links: https://app.docusign.com/preview/ABC123-sapphire

Next steps for internal owners: 
1) Finance (Marcus) to confirm provisional issuance or require two POs.
2) Once Marcus issues the provisional PO number, Vendor Ops will push the PO into the vendor portal and notify Sapphire.

Ben Carter
Vendor Ops — Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwoodinference.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@sapphiresystems.com>
Date: Fri, 29 May 2026 15:46:00 -0700
Subject: Final: Provisional PO issued — 2026-PO-3491 (Sapphire Systems)

On Thu, May 26, 2026 at 10:03 AM Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Samantha —
> 
> Thanks for the clear summary and for attaching the completed form. I'm looping in Ben from Vendor Ops and Priya on procurement intake so we can reconcile the spend cap and SKU split. Preliminary answers below: 
> 
> - I see the request for $75k/month; our default vendor cap for new partners is $50k until credit/usage history is established. We can raise that to $75k provisionally with Finance approval.

Colleagues — per Ben's submission and Sapphire's request, Finance has approved a provisional single PO at the requested band. Details below: 

- PO number: 2026-PO-3491 (provisional)
- Spend cap: $75,000/month — marked in the PO header as "provisional; subject to monthly reconciliation and credit review"
- Effective: 2026-06-01 — until 2026-09-30 unless revised earlier
- Line item corrections: Vendor Ops requested SKU split for RU-Compute-XL (SKU 7001) and RU-Storage-Standard (SKU 7002). Vendor invoices must list these as separate lines matching those SKU codes to align with our ledger. If invoices arrive combined we will temporarily route them to manual match which delays payment.

Attached is the issued PO stub (2026-PO-3491-stub.pdf). Ben — please push this into the vendor portal and confirm when Sapphire has accepted.

Samantha — since this PO is provisional, please sign the DocuSign terms and re-upload the corrected invoice. Once the portal shows acceptance we will convert the provisional tag to standard and confirm the spend band.

Marcus Lin
Director, Finance — Redwood Inference
marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: 2026-PO-3491-stub.pdf

source.subject: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems
messages:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bradley <alex.bradley@mayasystems.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Thanks again for your time earlier — great conversation. Quick recap + a few follow-ups so we can scope a short POC for private connectivity: 

1) Objectives we heard on the call
- Low-latency inference for in-product recommendations (95th p99 SLO target ~120ms for 1kB prompts)
- Full control of egress and DNS due to data residency and partner webhooks
- mTLS for internal service-to-service calls and an ability to pin certs

2) Items Maya asked about (we promised answers)
- Options for private connectivity: VPC endpoint / PrivateLink vs. Dedicated / Private deployment model
- Static egress IPs / IP allowlist for third-party API calls from Redwood
- DNS handling: private hosted zones vs. split-horizon for tool-calling endpoints
- mTLS support for control-plane and runtime RPCs
- SDK auth and token rotation for server-side SDKs
- How embeddings/reranker calls can be routed/isolated (if needed)

3) Immediate asks from Maya
- Danielle (netops) asked for a list of recommended network flows and a sample network diagram
- Alex asked whether we can provide a static egress IP range for their allowlist for hosted API during the POC
- Michael requested detail on mTLS options and whether customer-managed certs are supported

Next steps (owning parties):
- Priya: technical write-up addressing VPC options, static egress, DNS, and mTLS (ETA: tomorrow EOD)
- Connor: send draft network diagram & HubSpot deal update (today)
- Maya: confirm availability for a technical deep-dive with Danielle + Rishi (2-3 options for next week)

Attachments: I attached the sketch we worked on during the call (network_diagram_v1.png) and the requirements doc you shared (maya_requirements_20260521.pdf).

—Connor
Connor O'Brien | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
connor.obrien@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0134
[attachment: network_diagram_v1.png (image/png)]
[attachment: maya_requirements_20260521.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bradley <alex.bradley@mayasystems.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T17:10:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Alex — thanks for the call and the clear requirements. Below is a concise technical rundown of options and our recommended POC approach. Happy to expand any point in the deep-dive.

Summary of connectivity options (pros/cons & when we recommend them)
1) Hosted API + IP allowlist (fastest onboarding)
- What: You call Redwood hosted endpoints over the public internet; we can provide static egress IP ranges that you can allowlist on your partners/webhooks.
- Pros: Fastest to start, no infra change on your side besides allowlist.
- Cons: Data traverses internet, requires careful egress/DNS rules and TLS (we provide TLS).
- When to use: short POC where you cannot open VPC peering yet but need controlled egress.

2) Dedicated (reserved capacity) in a shared cloud tenancy with private VPC peering / VPC endpoint
- What: Redwood runs Dedicated GPU pools in your cloud region; we set up VPC peering or PrivateLink endpoints so traffic stays on the cloud provider network.
- Pros: Lower latency, private routing, supports private hosted zones and internal tool-calling.
- Cons: Requires network changes & minimal cloud-side config; longer lead time than hosted.

3) Private deployment (VPC/on-prem) — full isolation
- What: We deploy a customer-managed control plane or an on-prem package (air-gapped option available) and you keep full control of egress and DNS.
- Pros: Maximum control for compliance-sensitive workloads (mTLS with customer certs, KMS integration).
- Cons: More setup, entangled with your infra ops.

Key network constructs and recommendations for the POC
- Static egress IPs: For the hosted path, we can publish a small set of static egress IPs for outbound calls made by Redwood on your behalf (e.g., when tool calling external webhooks). We will provide a file with the exact CIDRs and update cadence.
- PrivateLink / VPC endpoint: For Dedicated, we recommend a PrivateLink endpoint so no public egress is needed; DNS can be routed via a private hosted zone (Route 53 private hosted zone example included).
- Split-horizon DNS: Use split-horizon to point api.redwood.internal -> PrivateLink endpoint for Dedicated, while api.redwood.com continues to resolve public IPs for other teams.
- mTLS: We support mTLS for runtime RPCs and can accept customer-provisioned certs via our onboarding flow (we support certificate pinning and rotation). For control-plane operations (billing/rollouts) we support customer-managed KMS for secrets.
- SDK auth: Server-side SDKs use short-lived tokens (STS) plus refresh tokens; we support API keys and OIDC-aligned rotation workflows. We recommend using our server SDK with rotated credentials stored in your secret manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager).

Embeddings & rerankers (networking notes)
- Request/response sizes: batching best practice — send 10-50 items per request for embeddings to reduce egress chatter.
- Isolation: We can route embeddings/reranker calls through your Dedicated/private deployment so embeddings vectors never cross public internet.

Tool calling / structured output
- If your tool endpoints are inside your VPC, we recommend Dedicated + PrivateLink; for hosted, you can whitelist our static egress IPs so function-calling webhook calls reach your internal endpoints via your firewall.

PoC proposal (two-week POC):
- Week 1: Hosted API with static egress allowlist + sample embedding/reranker integration (quick win).
- Week 2: Spin up Dedicated test pool with PrivateLink (if you want private routing) and validate DNS & mTLS.

Deliverables I will send tomorrow:
- Detailed network flow doc with AWS diagram and sample security group/route table rules
- CSV of static egress IPs for the hosted path
- mTLS onboarding checklist for customer-provided certs

Let me know preferred times for a technical session with Danielle and Rishi. I can do Wed/Thu mornings PST next week.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan | Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0172
[attachment: redwood-static-egress-ip-range.txt (text/plain)]

From: Alex Bradley <alex.bradley@mayasystems.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Danielle Cruz <danielle.cruz@mayasystems.com>, Michael Tan <michael.tan@mayasystems.com>
Date: 2026-05-22T09:30:00-04:00
Subject: RE: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Thanks Priya — this is super helpful and lines up with what we discussed. A few clarifying questions before the deep-dive: 

1) Static egress list: Can you confirm update cadence? We need SLA on changes so our firewall rules don't block mid-POC.
2) mTLS: We prefer to bring our own certs and want automated rotation. Do you support ACME/Let’s Encrypt style rotation or only manual cert uploads?
3) DNS: For PrivateLink flow, will you provide guidance for setting up Route 53 private hosted zones and sample Terraform? That would speed our infra team's work.

Danielle is available next Wed 2026-05-27 at 10:00am PST for the deep-dive — can you confirm Rishi? Also, would it be possible to get the network flow doc before then? It will help our netops prepare.

On Wed, May 21, 2026 at 5:10 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Alex — thanks for the call and the clear requirements. Below is a concise technical rundown...
> (message clipped)

—Alex
Alex Bradley | Head of Product Infrastructure, Maya Systems
alex.bradley@mayasystems.com | +1 (617) 555-2099

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bradley <alex.bradley@mayasystems.com>, Danielle Cruz <danielle.cruz@mayasystems.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-24T11:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Hi Alex / Danielle — Rishi from Redwood infra here. Quick answers to the clarifying points and what we'll bring to the deep-dive: 

1) Static egress update cadence
- We publish static egress CIDRs with a weekly cadence and a 14-day freeze window for changes tied to POC contracts. For POC customers we can extend to a 30-day notice if needed. I attached the current file (redwood-static-egress-ip-range.txt) and will include change-notice processes in the doc.

2) mTLS cert rotation
- We support customer-provided certs and automated rotation via an API we expose for cert uploads. We do not currently integrate directly with ACME endpoints for fully automated issuance, but we support automated rotation once we receive the cert bundle (you can script ACME locally and POST rotated certs to our API). We also support cert pinning and validation TTLs.

3) Route 53 / Terraform examples
- We'll send a Terraform module and a Route 53 private hosted zone example. For PrivateLink we recommend creating an interface endpoint in your VPC and a private hosted zone that resolves api.redwood.internal -> interface endpoint IPs. We'll share both a CloudFormation and Terraform snippet in the deep-dive.

4) Action items for the deep-dive
- Redwood (Rishi/Priya): full network flow doc, Terraform snippets, sample security group rules, and an example mTLS onboarding playbook.
- Maya (Danielle): share your VPC CIDR(s), firewall vendor (Palo Alto / Check Point / native cloud), and any SOC/pen-test constraints we should be aware of prior to setup.

5) Timeline notes
- If we start the POC next week and you give us VPC info within 3 business days, we can have a Dedicated test connection within ~7 business days. Hosted + static-egress path can be validated within 24–48 hours once your allowlist is configured.

I'll be on the Wed 2026-05-27 10:00am PST deep-dive. Priya will lead the network diagram walkthrough. Connor / Marissa are on standby for commercial questions.

Thanks — looking forward to the session.

—Rishi
Rishi Malhotra | Infrastructure Lead, Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0199
[attachment: redwood-static-egress-ip-range.txt (text/plain)]

1) Static egress update cadence

Subject: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Thanks again for your time earlier — great conversation. Quick recap + a few follow-ups so we can scope a short POC for private connectivity: 

1) Objectives we heard on the call
- Low-latency inference for in-product recommendations (95th p99 SLO target ~120ms for 1kB prompts)
- Full control of egress and DNS due to data residency and partner webhooks
- mTLS for internal service-to-service calls and an ability to pin certs

2) Items Maya asked about (we promised answers)
- Options for private connectivity: VPC endpoint / PrivateLink vs. Dedicated / Private deployment model
- Static egress IPs / IP allowlist for third-party API calls from Redwood
- DNS handling: private hosted zones vs. split-horizon for tool-calling endpoints
- mTLS support for control-plane and runtime RPCs
- SDK auth and token rotation for server-side SDKs
- How embeddings/reranker calls can be routed/isolated (if needed)

3) Immediate asks from Maya
- Danielle (netops) asked for a list of recommended network flows and a sample network diagram
- Alex asked whether we can provide a static egress IP range for their allowlist for hosted API during the POC
- Michael requested detail on mTLS options and whether customer-managed certs are supported

Next steps (owning parties):
- Priya: technical write-up addressing VPC options, static egress, DNS, and mTLS (ETA: tomorrow EOD)
- Connor: send draft network diagram & HubSpot deal update (today)
- Maya: confirm availability for a technical deep-dive with Danielle + Rishi (2-3 options for next week)

Attachments: I attached the sketch we worked on during the call (network_diagram_v1.png) and the requirements doc you shared (maya_requirements_20260521.pdf).

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bradley <alex.bradley@mayasystems.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T17:10:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Post-call: Networking & private connectivity questions for Maya Systems

Alex — thanks for the call and the clear requirements. Below is a concise technical rundown of options and our recommended POC approach. Happy to expand any point in the deep-dive.

Summary of connectivity options (pros/cons & when we recommend them)
1) Hosted API + IP allowlist (fastest onboarding)
- What: You call Redwood hosted endpoints over the public internet; we can provide static egress IP ranges that you can allowlist on your partners/webhooks.
- Pros: Fastest to start, no infra change on your side besides allowlist.
- Cons: Data traverses internet, requires careful egress/DNS rules and TLS (we provide TLS).
- When to use: short POC where you cannot open VPC peering yet but need controlled egress.

2) Dedicated (reserved capacity) in a shared cloud tenancy with private VPC peering / VPC endpoint
- What: Redwood runs Dedicated GPU pools in your cloud region; we set up VPC peering or PrivateLink endpoints so traffic stays on the cloud provider network.
- Pros: Lower latency, private routing, supports private hosted zones and internal tool-calling.
- Cons: Requires network changes & minimal cloud-side config; longer lead time than hosted.

3) Private deployment (VPC/on-prem) — full isolation
- What: We deploy a customer-managed control plane or an on-prem package (air-gapped option available) and you keep full control of egress and DNS.
- Pros: Maximum control for compliance-sensitive workloads (mTLS with customer certs, KMS integration).
- Cons: More setup, entangled with your infra ops.

Key network constructs and recommendations for the POC
- Static egress IPs: For the hosted path, we can publish a small set of static egress IPs for outbound calls made by Redwood on your behalf (e.g., when tool calling external webhooks). We will provide a file with the exact CIDRs and update cadence.
- PrivateLink / VPC endpoint: For Dedicated, we recommend a PrivateLink endpoint so no public egress is needed; DNS can be routed via a private hosted zone (Route 53 private hosted zone example included).
- Split-horizon DNS: Use split-horizon to point api.redwood.internal -> PrivateLink endpoint for Dedicated, while api.redwood.com continues to resolve public IPs for other teams.
- mTLS: We support mTLS for runtime RPCs and can accept customer-provisioned certs via our onboarding flow (we support certificate pinning and rotation). For control-plane operations (billing/rollouts) we support customer-managed KMS for secrets.
- SDK auth: Server-side SDKs use short-lived tokens (STS) plus refresh tokens; we support API keys and OIDC-aligned rotation workflows. We recommend using our server SDK with rotated credentials stored in your secret manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager).

Embeddings & rerankers (networking notes)
- Request/response sizes: batching best practice — send 10-50 items per request for embeddings to reduce egress chatter.
- Isolation: We can route embeddings/reranker calls through your Dedicated/private deployment so embeddings vectors never cross public internet.

Tool calling / structured output
- If your tool endpoints are inside your VPC, we recommend Dedicated + PrivateLink; for hosted, you can whitelist our static egress IPs so function-calling webhook calls reach your internal endpoints via your firewall.

PoC proposal (two-week POC):
- Week 1: Hosted API with static egress allowlist + sample embedding/reranker integration (quick win).
- Week 2: Spin up Dedicated test pool with PrivateLink (if you want private routing) and validate DNS & mTLS.

Deliverables I will send tomorrow:
- Detailed network flow doc with AWS diagram and sample security group/route table rules
- CSV of static egress IPs for the hosted path
- mTLS onboarding checklist for customer-provided certs

Let me know preferred times for a technical session with Danielle and Rishi. I can do Wed/Thu mornings PST next week.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan | Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0172
[attachment: redwood-static-egress-ip-range.txt (text/plain)]

- Company legal name and registered address (inc. tax jurisdiction)
- Remit-to address and preferred invoice email
- W-9 (if US) or equivalent tax form
- Primary billing contact (name / email / phone)
- Bank details for ACH (optional; can submit later)
- Preferred PO acknowledgement flow (API / portal / email)

We also typically send a Coupa invite from vendor-portal@coupa.com — please watch for that and let us know if it lands in spam. If you run into any invite/SSO issues, reply with a screenshot and we’ll escalate to vendor-ops.

3) If Coupa needs us to re-send an invite from a different environment let me know.

Sanjay — thanks for the details. If easier, paste a DocuSign link for the W-9 and I’ll get procurement to accept it.

Thanks,
Jonas

source.subject: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing
messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for the conversation last week — great to hear Luma is on track for the new patient messaging rollout. Attached is a fresh proposal (Luma_Proposal_v3.pdf) and a preliminary SOW spreadsheet that outlines run-rates and onboarding milestones. High-level summary: 

- Term: 18 months (hybrid model: committed baseline + usage-pegged burst pool)
- Billing: co-term with fiscal Q3 2026 invoice run; synchronized billing with quarterly true-ups
- Ramp: conservative first 3 months (50% baseline), then linear ramp to committed baseline by month 6, burst pool available for spikes
- Auto-renew: opt-in by default for subsequent 12-month renewals but with 60-day opt-out notice

Can you confirm procurement contact and whether the burst pool should be capped as a percent of committed tokens or an absolute token bucket? If helpful, I can route a one-page amendment template to legal.

Thanks,
Jonas
Jonas Weber
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-8192

Attachments referenced: Luma_Proposal_v3.pdf, SOW_Redwood_Luma_v1.xlsx
From: Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Luma Procurement <luma-purchasing@lumahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing

Jonas — thanks for the fast turn. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Procurement: please loop in our purchaser email (included above). We handle SOWs through Hub and DocuSign.
2) Burst model: prefer a capped percent of committed tokens (20% of monthly committed) rather than open absolute buckets. That helps forecasting for us.
3) Auto-renew: we usually negotiate an opt-in rather than opt-out — can Redwood flip that so renewal requires explicit opt-in?

Also linking the security checklist contact so your team can expect a questionnaire soon.

-Sarah
Sarah Cole
Head of Product Operations, Luma Health

> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 09:12 AM Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Attached is a fresh proposal (Luma_Proposal_v3.pdf) and a preliminary SOW spreadsheet...
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com>
Cc: Luma Procurement <luma-purchasing@lumahealth.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing

Thanks Sarah — good call on the procurement flow. Looping in Karthik for pricing flexibility and Priya for onboarding/success scope. Responses inline below: 

- Burst cap: Redwood can set burst as 20% of monthly committed by default; can make an elevated cap for the first 90 days for launch traffic if you want a temporary safety band.
- Auto-renew: we can switch to opt-in, though operationally that changes how credits/discounts are tiered. Karthik, what would an opt-in renewal do to the effective price?

Priya — think Luma will want a tighter 90-day launch plan with milestone-driven enablement credits. Can you confirm SOW milestones we should wire into the spreadsheet?

Jonas

> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 11:03 AM Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com> wrote:
> > Procurement: please loop in our purchaser email...
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 10:41:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Luma renewal structure suggestion (notes for counters)

Team — internal notes to shape the counter we send to Luma. Posting bullets so the external message stays concise.

Key proposal adjustments I recommend: 
- Term: keep 18 months, but include a 6-month early-stabilization review that can trigger a rebaseline if actual consumption > 125% of forecasted committed tokens.
- Ramp schedule: months 1-3 onboarding (50% baseline, dedicated onboarding credits), months 4-6 linear ramp to baseline, month 7 onwards steady-state. Add a one-time excess buffer of +10% for month 4 to account for dev testing.
- Burst model: capped at 20% monthly committed; allow a temporary 50% emergency cap for first 45 days after GA with pre-approved escalation.
- Auto-renew: change to explicit opt-in. Offer a 5% renewal discount if they commit to opt-in 60 days before term end (to preserve predictable revenue).
- Co-terming/billing: propose co-term to Luma fiscal Q3 but allow a one-time proration to align invoices.

Attachments to include in counter: SOW_Redwood_Luma_v1.xlsx (updated milestones), one-page amendment template (will attach).

Priya N.
Director, Customer Onboarding | Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com>
Cc: Luma Procurement <luma-purchasing@lumahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 8 May 2026 09:16:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing

Hi all — thanks for the context. Pricing update and quick examples below.

Counter proposal (high level):
- Committed baseline: 120M tokens / month
- Baseline unit price: $0.00185 per token (volume discount baked into baseline)
- Burst cap: 20% of monthly committed (24M tokens) priced at $0.0025 per token for any consumption within the cap; emergency 45-day elevated cap priced at $0.0030 if used
- Onboarding credits: $25k credit applied against first two invoices for milestone completion (months 1 and 3)
- Auto-renew: explicit opt-in required; offer a 5% renewal discount if they opt-in 60+ days before end of term
- Early-stabilization clause: if consumption > 125% for rolling 30 days, trigger a rebaseline conversation; pricing holds until new term negotiation

Impact examples: month at baseline + 10% burst = (120M * baseline price) + (12M * burst price) -> illustrative invoice attached in SOW spreadsheet.

Sarah — if that structure aligns, Jonas and I will prepare the one-page amendment and DocuSign route.

Thanks,
Karthik
VP, Revenue Ops | Redwood Inference
From: Sarah Cole <sarah.cole@lumahealth.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Luma Procurement <luma-purchasing@lumahealth.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 16:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal: 18-month hybrid renewal with usage-pegged bursts & synchronized billing

Karthik / Jonas — this all looks reasonable and inline with what we need for launch predictability. A couple of small asks before we sign: 

1) Please add a clear example invoice line in the amendment showing baseline + 10% burst calculation for transparency.
2) Can the emergency elevated cap require an internal Luma approval email chain (we can supply a named approver) to prevent accidental usage spikes billing?

If you add those two items to the one-page amendment I will route to procurement and get DocuSign started. Thanks — please send the DocuSign link when the amendment is ready.

Best,
Sarah

> On Fri, May 8, 2026 at 09:16 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Counter proposal (high level): Committed baseline: 120M tokens / month...

source.subject: Mutual action plan onboarding — coordination and calendar options
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Mike Chen <mike@evergrove.com>, Laura Simmons <laura.simmons@evergrove.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Mutual action plan onboarding — coordination and calendar options

Hi Mike / Laura —

Nice to e-meet you both. Per our conversation last week I’m opening a short thread to get a Mutual Action Plan (MAP) kickoff scheduled so we can align stakeholders, agree on the evaluation plan, and lock the target close timeline. I’ve attached a draft MAP and a quick agenda so you can skim ahead.

Proposed kickoff: 60 minutes. Goals: confirm stakeholders & roles, agree evaluation success criteria and timeline, and identify any procurement/legal blockers.

Two provisional calendar holds (I put both on your calendar as a placeholder; please confirm which works):
- Option A: Tue May 11, 2026 10:00–11:00 PT — Zoom (initial hold)
- Option B: Tue May 18, 2026 10:30–11:30 PT — Zoom (backup)

Zoom (provisional): https://zoom.us/j/0000000000 (updated link will follow once we confirm)

Suggested attendees and owners (please flag additions/removals):
- Customer PM / Evaluation lead: Mike Chen (EverGrove)
- Procurement: Laura Simmons (EverGrove) — please loop procurement contact if needed
- Redwood Sales / MAP owner: Aisha Rahman
- Redwood CS (evaluation plan & success criteria): Ben Carter
- Solutions / technical runbook: Marcus Lin
- Onboarding / data ops: Priya Natarajan

Attachments: EverGrove_MAP-draft-v1.pdf (draft MAP), MAP-agenda-draft-2026-05-04.md (short agenda).

Ask: Please reply with the preferred date and any additional stakeholders (especially procurement or legal contacts). If you have an eval dataset or acceptance tests we should review ahead, attach or share a link.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this moving quickly.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mailto:aisha_rahman@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0124

---
Attachment stubs:
- EverGrove_MAP-draft-v1.pdf (18 KB, PDF)
- MAP-agenda-draft-2026-05-04.md (2 KB, Markdown)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Mike Chen <mike@evergrove.com>

source.subject: DR Control Objectives & Failover Constraints — follow-up
messages:
From: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Alcyon Security Team <security@alcyon.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: DR control objectives and operational constraints

Hi Chloe,

Thanks for agreeing to take our DR / continuity questionnaire. We're evaluating Redwood as part of a multi-vendor procurement and need to validate concrete commitments and architectural constraints for our vendor risk team. A few things we need answered so we can advance to the technical review: 

- Confirm demonstrable RTO and RPO targets for the hosted API (per region) and for Dedicated customers.
- Describe what triggers an automated cross-region failover versus a manual switchover.
- Explain any data replication lag windows we should assume for async replication and how that affects session affinity and KV cache state during failover.
- List any operational limitations (e.g., maximum concurrent failovers, maintenance windows that block failover) and how Redwood sequences traffic during a regional outage.

Can you provide an architecture-level summary (diagrams optional) and any runbook extracts, plus a short list of artifacts we can present to our risk committee? Ideally we'd have this by Wed EOD so we can include it in our scheduled review.

Best,
Michael
Michael Ruiz
Senior Infrastructure Risk Manager
Alcyon Financial
[attachment: alcyon_dr_q1.docx (docx, 44KB)]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 12:34:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DR control objectives and operational constraints

Michael —

Appreciate the clear list. I've pulled together an initial summary and the RTO/RPO matrix for each offering (hosted, dedicated, private) — see attached: redwood_dr_summary.pdf and rto_rpo_matrix.csv. High level answers below; Ben and Priya are looped for service-level and contract-level confirmations.

Quick answers: 
- RTO/RPO: Hosted API: typical RTO <= 3 minutes for control-plane re-route, token-generation sessions may have higher tail latency while KV caches rehydrate (detailed per-model numbers in matrix). Dedicated: RTO <= 5 minutes with configured warm capacity; RPO for both is best-effort near-zero for control-plane metadata, up to 60s for live KV caches in async replication modes. Private (VPC/on-prem) depends on customer replication configuration — we provide replication tooling and recommended patterns.

- Failover triggers: Smart routing policy triggers automatic routing to healthy region when regional health-checks cross configured thresholds (loss of 50% capacity or sustained p95 latency SLA breach for >2 minutes). Customers may opt into automatic failover; otherwise we execute a manual-canary switchover per the agreed runbook.

- Replication and cache state: Persistent model artifacts and control-plane metadata are synchronously replicated across control-plane nodes. KV cache is asynchronously replicated — we recommend design patterns that treat KV as cache (replayable or rebuildable). Expect transient session affinity loss during hot failover; we document rehydration steps and provide per-route sticky routing as an interim mitigation.

- Operational limits: Concurrent cross-region failovers are limited by regional capacity reservations and autoscale policies; by default we allow 2 concurrent DR activations for a tenant with automatic fallback to degraded-service mode if capacity is constrained. Maintenance windows do not block failovers but customers can specify blackout windows.

I'll send a slightly expanded runbook extract in the next message (Ben to confirm the contract SLAs).

Regards,
Chloe Martin
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood_dr_summary.pdf (pdf, 120KB)]
[attachment: rto_rpo_matrix.csv (csv, 7KB)]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 15:09:00 -04:00
Subject: Internal: Alcyon DR specifics & SLA ask — need confirmation

Ben / Priya —

Looping you in on Alcyon's questionnaire. I provided a short summary but they want artifacts for their risk committee and concrete SLA language around RTO/RPO and concurrency limits. Items to confirm: 

1) Hosted API SLA language we can commit to in an SOW for RTO (control-plane re-route) and any explicit RPO bounds for model state/control metadata.
2) Dedicated tenant: confirm that with reserved warm capacity we can guarantee RTO <= 5 minutes and outline what happens if the warm pool is saturated.
3) The standard runbook extract to give them — we should redact any internal-only operational notes.

Target is to consolidate and send a final package to Michael by Wed 5/6 EOD.

Thanks —
Chloe

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 21:03:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: DR Control Objectives & Failover Constraints — follow-up

Hi Michael — Chloe, thanks for the summary. Apologies for the delayed follow-up; I wanted to confirm language we can sign to. Below is a concise package you can present to your risk committee.

Executive commitments (can be put into SOW / Annex):
- Hosted API (Standard): Control-plane failover re-route RTO (time to route traffic to healthy region): 3 minutes (p95).
- Hosted API (Standard): Service-level RPO for control-plane metadata: near-zero (synchronous replication). For in-flight KV cache state RPO is not guaranteed — operational guidance: design for cache rehydration; typical cache window for multi-region active/passive is 30–120s depending on model token throughput.
- Dedicated (Reserved Warm Pool): RTO guarantee 5 minutes (p95) when warm-pool reserved at contractual capacity. If warm pool capacity is exceeded, failover will be sequenced with traffic shaping and we provide crediting for impact periods per the SLA.

Failover mechanics:
- Automatic routing: Enabled via Smart Routing policy. Condition thresholds we use: region health < 50% available capacity OR p95 latency > SLA for >120s. Customers can opt-out.
- Manual canary: For customers who prefer manual escalation we provide a documented canary procedure with rollback gates; expected time-to-route for manual canary: 6–20 minutes (depends on traffic volume and customer route configuration).

Data consistency & KV cache note:
- Persistent artifacts and model binaries are stored in replicated object stores with cross-region replication (synchronous for control-plane metadata, async for large artifacts with consistency checks).
- KV cache is a best-effort, asynchronously replicated cache. We provide tooling to export/restore critical session state to a persistent store prior to maintenance windows.

Operational constraints & sequencing:
- Default concurrency: Two simultaneous tenant-level cross-region activations. Requests beyond this will be queued with priority scheduling; degrade-to-read-only is an available fallback.
- Maintenance windows: Customers may define blackout periods where scheduled maintenance avoids automated failover. Emergency failovers still proceed per policy.

Artifacts attached: redwood_dr_summary.pdf (technical summary), rto_rpo_matrix.csv (per-offering numbers), runbook-extract.md (sanitized excerpt suitable for external distribution). I've redacted internal-only runbook steps.

Next steps: if this language works we can produce a short SOW annex and a signed statement of capability. Priya — can you confirm the crediting language and contract clause references (I think it's Annex B-DR-1)?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Reliability & Field Engineering
Redwood Inference
[attachment: runbook-extract.md (text, 18KB)]

- Operational limits: Concurrent cross-region failovers are limited by regional capacity reservations and autoscale policies; by default we allow 2 concurrent DR activations for a tenant with automatic fallback to degraded-service mode if capacity is constrained. Maintenance windows do not block failovers but customers can specify blackout windows.

I'll send a slightly expanded runbook extract in the next message (Ben to confirm the contract SLAs).

Regards,
Chloe Martin
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 15:09:00 -04:00
Subject: Internal: Alcyon DR specifics & SLA ask — need confirmation

Ben / Priya —

Looping you in on Alcyon's questionnaire. I provided a short summary but they want artifacts for their risk committee and concrete SLA language around RTO/RPO and concurrency limits. Items to confirm: 

1) Hosted API SLA language we can commit to in an SOW for RTO (control-plane re-route) and any explicit RPO bounds for model state/control metadata.
2) Dedicated tenant: confirm that with reserved warm capacity we can guarantee RTO <= 5 minutes and outline what happens if the warm pool is saturated.
3) The standard runbook extract to give them — we should redact any internal-only operational notes.

Target is to consolidate and send a final package to Michael by Wed 5/6 EOD.

Thanks —
Chloe

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 21:03:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: DR Control Objectives & Failover Constraints — follow-up

Hi Michael — Chloe, thanks for the summary. Apologies for the delayed follow-up; I wanted to confirm language we can sign to. Below is a concise package you can present to your risk committee.

Executive commitments (can be put into SOW / Annex):
- Hosted API (Standard): Control-plane failover re-route RTO (time to route traffic to healthy region): 3 minutes (p95).
- Hosted API (Standard): Service-level RPO for control-plane metadata: near-zero (synchronous replication). For in-flight KV cache state RPO is not guaranteed — operational guidance: design for cache rehydration; typical cache window for multi-region active/passive is 30–120s depending on model token throughput.
- Dedicated (Reserved Warm Pool): RTO guarantee 5 minutes (p95) when warm-pool reserved at contractual capacity. If warm pool capacity is exceeded, failover will be sequenced with traffic shaping and we provide crediting for impact periods per the SLA.

Failover mechanics:
- Automatic routing: Enabled via Smart Routing policy. Condition thresholds we use: region health < 50% available capacity OR p95 latency > SLA for >120s. Customers can opt-out.
- Manual canary: For customers who prefer manual escalation we provide a documented canary procedure with rollback gates; expected time-to-route for manual canary: 6–20 minutes (depends on traffic volume and customer route configuration).

Data consistency & KV cache note:
- Persistent artifacts and model binaries are stored in replicated object stores with cross-region replication (synchronous for control-plane metadata, async for large artifacts with consistency checks).
- KV cache is a best-effort, asynchronously replicated cache. We provide tooling to export/restore critical session state to a persistent store prior to maintenance windows.

Operational constraints & sequencing:
- Default concurrency: Two simultaneous tenant-level cross-region activations. Requests beyond this will be queued with priority scheduling; degrade-to-read-only is an available fallback.
- Maintenance windows: Customers may define blackout periods where scheduled maintenance avoids automated failover. Emergency failovers still proceed per policy.

Artifacts attached: redwood_dr_summary.pdf (technical summary), rto_rpo_matrix.csv (per-offering numbers), runbook-extract.md (sanitized excerpt suitable for external distribution). I've redacted internal-only runbook steps.

Next steps: if this language works we can produce a short SOW annex and a signed statement of capability. Priya — can you confirm the crediting language and contract clause references (I think it's Annex B-DR-1)?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Reliability & Field Engineering
Redwood Inference
[attachment: runbook-extract.md (text, 18KB)]
text:
From: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Alcyon Security Team <security@alcyon.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: DR control objectives and operational constraints

Hi Chloe,

Thanks for agreeing to take our DR / continuity questionnaire. We're evaluating Redwood as part of a multi-vendor procurement and need to validate concrete commitments and architectural constraints for our vendor risk team. A few things we need answered so we can advance to the technical review: 

- Confirm demonstrable RTO and RPO targets for the hosted API (per region) and for Dedicated customers.
- Describe what triggers an automated cross-region failover versus a manual switchover.
- Explain any data replication lag windows we should assume for async replication and how that affects session affinity and KV cache state during failover.
- List any operational limitations (e.g., maximum concurrent failovers, maintenance windows that block failover) and how Redwood sequences traffic during a regional outage.

Can you provide an architecture-level summary (diagrams optional) and any runbook extracts, plus a short list of artifacts we can present to our risk committee? Ideally we'd have this by Wed EOD so we can include it in our scheduled review.

Best,
Michael
Michael Ruiz
Senior Infrastructure Risk Manager
Alcyon Financial
[attachment: alcyon_dr_q1.docx (docx, 44KB)]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Michael Ruiz <michael.ruiz@alcyon.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 12:34:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DR control objectives and operational constraints

Michael —

Appreciate the clear list. I've pulled together an initial summary and the RTO/RPO matrix for each offering (hosted, dedicated, private) — see attached: redwood_dr_summary.pdf and rto_rpo_matrix.csv. High level answers below; Ben and Priya are looped for service-level and contract-level confirmations.

Quick answers: 
- RTO/RPO: Hosted API: typical RTO <= 3 minutes for control-plane re-route, token-generation sessions may have higher tail latency while KV caches rehydrate (detailed per-model numbers in matrix). Dedicated: RTO <= 5 minutes with configured warm capacity; RPO for both is best-effort near-zero for control-plane metadata, up to 60s for live KV caches in async replication modes. Private (VPC/on-prem) depends on customer replication configuration — we provide replication tooling and recommended patterns.

- Failover triggers: Smart routing policy triggers automatic routing to healthy region when regional health-checks cross configured thresholds (loss of 50% capacity or sustained p95 latency SLA breach for >2 minutes). Customers may opt into automatic failover; otherwise we execute a manual-canary switchover per the agreed runbook.

- Replication and cache state: Persistent model artifacts and control-plane metadata are synchronously replicated across control-plane nodes. KV cache is asynchronously replicated — we recommend design patterns that treat KV as cache (replayable or rebuildable). Expect transient session affinity loss during hot failover; we document rehydration steps and provide per-route sticky routing as an interim mitigation.

- Operational limits: Concurrent cross-region failovers are limited by regional capacity reservations and autoscale policies; by default we allow 2 concurrent DR activations for a tenant with automatic fallback to degraded-service mode if capacity is constrained. Maintenance windows do not block failovers but customers can specify blackout windows.

I'll send a slightly expanded runbook extract in the next message (Ben to confirm the contract SLAs).

Regards,
Chloe Martin
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood_dr_summary.pdf (pdf, 120KB)]
[attachment: rto_rpo_matrix.csv (csv, 7KB)]

source.subject: Advance commitment schedule & Net terms negotiation for multi-year deal
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@cloudfin.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Marco Bianchi <marco.bianchi@cloudfin.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Multi-year commitment — payment schedule and Net terms question

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We ran the numbers with Finance and Procurement and want to discuss a multi-year advance commitment (3 years) with an annual billing cadence but need flexibility on payment timing. High-level ask:

- Can Redwood accommodate a 3-year, annual prepay with a larger discount if we agree to an initial partial upfront (25% at contract signature) and the remainder billed annually?
- Our procurement policy prefers Net 90 for invoice settlements. Is Net 90 possible for the annual invoices if we do partial upfront?
- Alternatively, would Redwood consider a milestone schedule (25% upfront, 50% at production launch, 25% at year 1 anniversary) for the first year and then annual prepay thereafter?

We can share a PO for the upfront amount as soon as we have a signed SOW/contract. Also attaching our internal purchase guidelines for reference (see attachment). Can you propose a few payment schedule options and the corresponding term discounts?

Appreciate a quick outline so we can get Legal/Finance aligned.

Best,
Elena
Director, Procurement — CloudFin Analytics

Attachment: cloudfin_purchase_policy.pdf (referenced)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.inference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@cloudfin.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Multi-year commitment — payment schedule and Net terms question

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear summary and the policy doc. Quick responses and three proposed options below (we tested economics for each with Finance):

Option A — 3yr annual prepay (single invoice per year)- Discount: 18% on list for 3-year commit- Upfront: 25% at signature (applied as credit to Y1)- Invoicing: annual invoices for the outstanding year amount- Terms: Net 60 on annual invoices (we can extend to Net 75 with exec approval; Net 90 requires a finance exception)

Option B — Milestone-first schedule (hybrid)- Discount: 20% on list (higher due to split risk)- Payment: 25% upfront, 50% at production launch milestone, 25% at Y1 anniversary- Terms: Net 60 for milestone invoices post-signature; remaining future annual payments paid upfront or Net 60 depending on corporate PO

Option C — Heavier upfront with deeper discount- Discount: 22% if CloudFin prepays full first year + 25% upfront at signature for commitment (applies to first invoice only)- Payment: 50% upfront credited toward Y1 and Y2 ramp schedule; remaining annual prepayments due at start of each year- Terms: Net 60 on any subsequent invoice; upfront amount via PO at signature

Notes:
- Net 90 is uncommon for prepay deals at Redwood; we can pursue a Net 90 finance exception if CloudFin can provide a corporate credit evaluation and a PO for the upfront portion.
- Milestone schedules introduce collection risk; Finance prefers a larger upfront or a confirmed PO for the upfront tranche.
- I attached a draft payment schedule spreadsheet (redwood_payment_schedule.xlsx) and a short SOW outline (SOW_v2.pdf) for your review.

If any of these look promising I can loop in Priya to request the formal finance exception and get an initial DS (DocuSign) ready. Which option should I prioritize?

Thanks,
Alex
Account Exec — Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_payment_schedule.xlsx, SOW_v2.pdf

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.inference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Apr 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Multi-year commitment — payment schedule and Net terms question

Alex — looping in Finance input and formal exception guidance. High level Finance stance: we can entertain exceptions for Net 90 on billed amounts when there is sufficient upfront or an irrevocable PO. Detailed recommendation: 

- Preferred structure (Finance-approved): 25% upfront at signature (PO accepted), 50% at production launch (invoice Net 60), remainder (25%) invoiced at year-end with Net 60. This is close to Option B and balances risk.
- For a deeper discount (20%+), require the customer to prepay at least the first year (annual upfront) or provide a fully executed corporate PO that covers the upfront tranche.
- If CloudFin insists on Net 90 across annual invoices, require a formal credit review and approval by Head of Finance (Rishi). That review typically adds 5–7 business days.

Action items for Alex: please confirm which option Elena prefers and attach (1) proposed PO for upfront tranche or (2) corporate credit references if they need Net 90. If they sign for the upfront PO, we can approve a 20% discount for the milestone hybrid or 22% for heavier upfront.

Priya
Director, Finance Operations — Redwood Inference

Attachment: finance_exception_template.docx (referenced)

From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@cloudfin.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Marco Bianchi <marco.bianchi@cloudfin.com>
Date: Sat, 2 May 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Multi-year commitment — payment schedule and Net terms question

Alex / Priya — thanks for the clear options and the spreadsheet. We discussed internally and are inclined to go with the milestone-first schedule you and Finance recommended (Option B variant):

- Agreement: 3-year commitment, 20% discount- Payment: 25% upfront at signature (we will issue PO for this immediately), 50% at production launch, 25% at Y1 anniversary- Terms: Net 60 on milestone invoices; for the remaining annual prepayments after Y1 we will aim for upfront annual payment but may request Net 60 on those invoices

Next steps from our side: Elena to send PO for the 25% upfront today. We will also share any corporate credit references only if Redwood requires Net 90 later. Please send the revised SOW and contract with those payment terms and the DocuSign link and we'll route for signature.

As a note for legal: we expect a standard liability clause and a typical SLA commitment; no unusual redlines anticipated but Legal may request a short review window.

Thanks — looking forward to closing.
Elena
Director, Procurement — CloudFin Analytics

Links included: DocuSign (https://www.docusign.com/link/abc123-redwood-cloudfin)
Attachments referenced by Redwood: SOW_v2.pdf, redwood_payment_schedule.xlsx

Cc: Marco Bianchi <marco.bianchi@cloudfin.com>
Date: Sat, 2 May 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Multi-year commitment — payment schedule and Net terms question

Alex / Priya — thanks for the clear options and the spreadsheet. We discussed internally and are inclined to go with the milestone-first schedule you and Finance recommended (Option B variant):

- Agreement: 3-year commitment, 20% discount- Payment: 25% upfront at signature (we will issue PO for this immediately), 50% at production launch, 25% at Y1 anniversary- Terms: Net 60 on milestone invoices; for the remaining annual prepayments after Y1 we will aim for upfront annual payment but may request Net 60 on those invoices

Next steps from our side: Elena to send PO for the 25% upfront today. We will also share any corporate credit references only if Redwood requires Net 90 later. Please send the revised SOW and contract with those payment terms and the DocuSign link and we'll route for signature.

As a note for legal: we expect a standard liability clause and a typical SLA commitment; no unusual redlines anticipated but Legal may request a short review window.

Thanks — looking forward to closing.
Elena
Director, Procurement — CloudFin Analytics

Links included: DocuSign (https://www.docusign.com/link/abc123-redwood-cloudfin)
Attachments referenced by Redwood: SOW_v2.pdf, redwood_payment_schedule.xlsx

source.subject: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Hi Alex —

Hope you had a good week. As Cloudscale's annual renewal is approaching (term ends 2026-06-30), I wanted to kick off a short renewal QBR to align on capacity, the proposed uplift, and a potential 12-month term extension with a committed capacity tier. High-level goals for the meeting: 

- Review usage trends and token burn (last 6 months)
- Discuss the +30% capacity uplift proposal to reduce queueing for peak workloads
- Walk through a 12-mo term amendment and associated pricing/discount tiers
- Agree on next steps and a mutual action plan (MAP) with owners

Proposed duration: 45 minutes. Can you confirm a couple of 45-min windows next week? I can take 4/20 morning (9–11 PT) or 4/21 afternoon (1–4 PT). If those don't work, send 2–3 alternatives and we'll follow up with a calendar invite.

I attached a draft QBR agenda and a preliminary pricing worksheet for the proposed uplift — these are internal-first but I can share a cleaned version after we confirm timing.

Key ask: if there are procurement or security folks who should join (legal, procurement, data-security), please loop them in. For context, the uplift is primarily to support Cloudscale's new ingestion pipeline (batch inference spikes) and would come with a brief onboarding slot from our Dedicated Ops team.

Thanks — looking forward to the sync.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0126

Attachment: QBR_agenda_v1.pdf (agenda)
Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Jamie Torres <jamie.torres@cloudscale.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Ben —

Thanks for the heads up and for the draft materials. We want to get ahead of the renewal discussion. 4/20 at 10:00 PT works for me; Jamie can confirm in a follow-up.

Two quick items for the call: 
1) Can you include a short pricing comparison for a 6‑month vs 12‑month commit (I know you sent a worksheet, but a one‑slide comparison will help our procurement).
2) We have an internal security reviewer (Lina Ramos) who will likely ask for details on data retention/audit logs — can Priya be prepared to speak to retention and Private deployment options?

Also — we may be asking for a short credit to offset some overage from a prior quarter while negotiating the uplift. Not a formal ask yet, but want to flag it so it's on the table.

Alex
VP, Platform Engineering
Cloudscale Dynamics

On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alex —
> 
> Hope you had a good week. As Cloudscale's annual renewal is approaching (term ends 2026-06-30), I wanted to kick off a short renewal QBR...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>, Jamie Torres <jamie.torres@cloudscale.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Working notes / prep items before the 4/20 meeting (I’ll drop the finalized slide deck to Alex after the call):

- Pricing: I prepared a one‑slide comparison (6m vs 12m) and an uplift schedule tied to committed TPS and token tiers. See attached. For the credit ask Alex mentioned, we can propose a one‑time goodwill credit equal to the overage delta for Q1, contingent on signing the 12‑month uplift by 5/15.
- Security/retention: Priya — please be ready to summarize: default retention windows, audit log granularity, and Private deployment + KMS options. If Lina wants SOC2 artifacts, we can surface the SOC2 report link during the call.
- Ops/onboarding: Recommend scheduling a 2-hour onboarding slot with Dedicated Ops within 2 weeks of amendment signing — covers KV cache warmup, burst policy, and autoscale guardrails.
- MAP: I’ll own creating the mutual action plan doc in HubSpot and assign owners post-call.

I've attached the pricing slide and a draft MAP template.

Thanks team — I’ll join the 4/20 10:00 PT slot (Ben, please send the invite).

Marissa Cole
Account Director, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx (pricing slide)
Attachment: MAP_template_v1.docx (hubspot friendly checklist)
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Alex —

Great — 4/20 10:00 PT is locked and calendar invite is out (you should have it). Agenda attached will be the working doc; we'll capture action items and publish the MAP in HubSpot within 24 hours of the meeting.

To summarize next steps before the call: 
- Marissa: bring the 6m vs 12m slide and proposed uplift pricing. (Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx)
- Priya: prepare SOC2/retention talking points and a short note on Private deployment options.
- Alex/Jamie: bring procurement constraints and Lina (security) if available.
- Ben: draft MAP in HubSpot, confirm DocuSign envelope for the amendment (tentative) and check on a credit proposal floor.

If Alex requests the pricing slide ahead of 4/20 for procurement review, Marissa will share a redacted PDF by EOD 4/21.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0126

Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/CS-2026-Q2-0819
Attachment: QBR_agenda_v1.pdf (final will be updated post-call)

source.subject: Backfill approval: Internal transfer to Senior SRE (comp band uplift & offer)
messages:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T09:12:00-07:00
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Subject: Backfill request: Senior SRE for Infra Reliability team

Hi Priya and Evelyn,

Quick heads-up: Jamal (Senior SRE, Infra Reliability) resigned effective 3/19 to pursue an internal role elsewhere. That leaves a senior slot open on my team that we need to backfill to maintain oncall coverage and our SLO commitments.

I would like to propose an internal transfer of Rohan Mehta (currently Infrastructure Engineer II) into the Senior SRE role with a one-band uplift (IC3 -> IC4). Rohan has led incident retros for the last 6 months, owns our KV cache rollout, and has been acting as the primary oncall for two rotations. Performance notes attached.

Requested approvals:
- Confirmation that we can repurpose the vacated Senior SRE headcount for this internal transfer
- Compensation uplift to Senior band for Rohan (see comp-band-grid.pdf)
- One-time sign-on / retention payment to align with market: proposing $20,000 sign-on conditional on acceptance

Attachments: comp-band-grid.pdf, org-chart-sre.png, offer-letter-draft.docx (draft).

Timeline: hoping to have an offer out to Rohan by 3/29 and start date ~4/12. Please let me know what additional info you need.

Thanks,
David
David Kim
Engineering Manager, Infra Reliability
Redwood Inference
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T11:03:00-07:00
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Backfill request: Senior SRE for Infra Reliability team

On Sun, Mar 22, 2026 at 9:12 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick heads-up: Jamal (Senior SRE, Infra Reliability) resigned effective 3/19 to pursue an internal role elsewhere. That leaves a senior slot open on my team that we need to backfill to maintain oncall coverage and our SLO commitments.
>
> I would like to propose an internal transfer of Rohan Mehta (currently Infrastructure Engineer II) into the Senior SRE role with a one-band uplift (IC3 -> IC4).

Thanks David — this is helpful context. A few asks from People before we can move to a formal offer: 
- Please send a 1-paragraph promotion rationale that maps Rohan's work to the Senior SRE promotion rubric (impact, scope, technical breadth, leadership).
- Confirm whether the headcount being reused is the exact slot left by Jamal or whether we need a new headcount request (paperwork differs).
- If you propose a $20k sign-on for an internal transfer we need a strong business justification (retention risk / market delta). Our default cap for internal lateral-ups is $10k unless exec signs off.

I can draft the offer once we have the rationale and Marcus confirms budget. Also will run compensation benchmarking against market data.

- Priya
Priya Natarajan
People Partner, Redwood Inference
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-23T08:45:00-07:00
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Backfill request: Senior SRE for Infra Reliability team — details + draft offer

Priya — attaching the 1-paragraph promo rationale and a draft offer (offer-letter-draft.docx). Short bullets below to summarize: 

Promotion rationale (high level):
- Impact: Rohan led the KV cache rollout that reduced tail latency by ~18% and authored the postmortem for the March outage that reduced recurrence risk.
- Scope: owns infra for two critical services and mentors two junior engineers.
- Technical breadth: strong across Linux systems, Rust tools we use, and incident automation.
- Leadership: running monthly oncall debriefs and led triage for a cross-team initiative.

Headcount: This is a direct reuse of Jamal's vacated Senior SRE slot (no new headcount).

Comp proposal (initial):
- Title: Senior SRE (IC4)
- Base: $180,000
- Sign-on: $20,000 one-time (to offset internal market delta and retention risk)
- Equity: unchanged from current GC grant schedule (we'll follow People ops guidance)

Please advise on whether Marcus can approve the dollars or if we need Evelyn/exec sign-off. If Marcus or Priya want me to pull lower numbers I can adjust quickly. Target timeline: offer out by 3/29.

Thanks,
David
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-24T13:20:00-07:00
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Backfill request: Senior SRE for Infra Reliability team — budget check

David / Priya — thanks for the detail. A few notes from Finance: 
- We can reallocate Jamal's vacated salary bucket to cover base for the internal move. That said, sign-on / retention payments require a separate one-time approval.
- Recommend capping the sign-on at $15k for internal promotions (we reserve $20k+ for external hires / critical roles).
- If you want $20k we need Evelyn to explicitly approve the exception and document the retention risk.
- Given comp bands and our current spend plan, I can approve a base up to $170k immediately. Anything above that will require a headcount carryover or Exec sign-off.

Action items: Evelyn — can you confirm whether you approve a $20k sign-on and the proposed IC4 placement? If not, happy to proceed with base $170k + $15k sign-on.

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Finance Planning & Analysis
Redwood Inference
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-25T09:54:00-07:00
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Backfill request: Senior SRE for Infra Reliability team — approval conditions

Thanks team. I support approving the internal transfer to Senior SRE (IC4) provided the following conditions are met: 
- Rohan must meet at least 3/4 of the promotion rubric criteria; Priya please record the calibration rationale in the HR file.
- Approve compensation of Base $170,000 + $15,000 one-time sign-on. The $20k ask is not approved without additional justification (we need explicit evidence of counteroffer risk).
- Offer should include a 30-day start target and clear expectations for the first 90 days (oncall ownership + incident playbook updates).

Priya — please prepare the formal offer reflecting Base $170k / Sign-on $15k and the promotion memo. David — please confirm Rohan is willing to move under these terms and target start date.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-27T15:42:00-07:00
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, TalentPartners LLC <recruiter@talentpartners.com>
Cc: Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Subject: Final: Senior SRE internal offer — please prepare

Thanks all — final confirmation based on feedback:
- Title: Senior SRE (IC4)
- Base: $170,000
- One-time sign-on: $15,000
- Start target: 2026-04-12 (aiming for within 30 days)
- Conditions: promotion memo to be filed by People (Priya) and 90-day expectations documented.

Priya — can you please prepare the formal offer (use offer-letter-draft.docx as template) and route through DocuSign (link in related_links). Rohan is cc'd here for awareness.

TalentPartners — FYI; no external hiring needed now but keeping you in the loop in case we need to re-open externally.

Attachments: comp-band-grid.pdf, org-chart-sre.png, offer-letter-draft.docx
Links: Greenhouse job 1345 (internal transfer), HR backfill request doc, DocuSign prep link.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
David
David Kim
Engineering Manager, Infra Reliability
Redwood Inference
