Quick heads-up: Jamal (Senior SRE, Infra Reliability) resigned effective 3/19 to pursue an internal role elsewhere. That leaves a senior slot open on my team that we need to backfill to maintain oncall coverage and our SLO commitments.

I would like to propose an internal transfer of Rohan Mehta (currently Infrastructure Engineer II) into the Senior SRE role with a one-band uplift (IC3 -> IC4). Rohan has led incident retros for the last 6 months, owns our KV cache rollout, and has been acting as the primary oncall for two rotations. Performance notes attached.

Requested approvals:
- Confirmation that we can repurpose the vacated Senior SRE headcount for this internal transfer
- Compensation uplift to Senior band for Rohan (see comp-band-grid.pdf)
- One-time sign-on / retention payment to align with market: proposing $20,000 sign-on conditional on acceptance

Attachments: comp-band-grid.pdf, org-chart-sre.png, offer-letter-draft.docx (draft).

Timeline: hoping to have an offer out to Rohan by 3/29 and start date ~4/12. Please let me know what additional info you need.

Thanks,
David
David Kim
Engineering Manager, Infra Reliability
Redwood Inference

David

source.subject: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates
source.thread_content:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Helen Morris <helen@greenway-people.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

Hi leadership —

Thanks for carving out time. The Q1 engagement pulse completed last week and the external analysis from Greenway highlights a small group of employees who are showing multiple risk signals (manager relationship, unclear growth path, burnout indicators). I’ve attached the anonymized high-risk export and a suggested manager action template.

Top asks for this thread:
- Please review the people on the high-risk list who report into your org (column B in the spreadsheet).
- For anyone you own, confirm whether you want People Ops to take proactive outreach, or whether you’ll handle a 1:1 within 48 hours.
- If you opt for manager-led outreach, use the attached template and copy People Ops on follow-ups.

We flagged 14 individuals company-wide; 4 are in engineering reporting up through Markus, 3 in product (Laura), 2 in infra/ops (Evelyn), and the rest distributed. Greenway will join for a short calibration session tomorrow at 2pm to walk through the methodology.

Attachments: 20260318_engagement_high_risk_list.xlsx (redacted), manager_action_template_v2.docx

Thanks —
Priya
Priya Natarajan
Head of People Operations
Redwood Inference

------------------------------
From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

Thanks Priya — I looked through the spreadsheet. I can take the four engineering folks: two on my direct IC ladder (eng managers should handle), and two individual contributors on the infra team that I prefer to coordinate with Evelyn on.

Action items from me:
- I’ll schedule 1:1 touchpoints for the two ICs within 48 hours and use the manager template.
- I want People Ops on standby for any immediate re-recruit risks (counteroffer help, relocation triggers).

I’ll join the Greenway call tomorrow.

—Markus
Markus Klein
VP, Engineering

------------------------------
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

Markus — I’ll take the two infra folks with you. I reviewed their notes: both show workload spikes and recent role ambiguity after the Feb org changes. I’ll reach out with a clear expectation map and short-term block to reduce context switching.

One sensitive note: one of the infra folks (row 17 in the sheet) referenced experiments with an external recruiter in their comment. We should treat that as high priority and let People Ops lead the outreach (to avoid any surprise escalation).

Assigning owners:
- Evelyn: infra ICs (coord with Markus) — manager-led touch + People Ops alerted for row 17
- Markus: two eng ICs — manager-led touch
- Laura: product list — please confirm

Evelyn Hart
Director, Infrastructure

------------------------------
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

I reviewed the three product flags. Two are straightforward — career conversation + clearer roadmap alignment — I’ll handle those manager-led. One is more nuanced: commented about unclear promotion criteria and a failed upward move. I want Priya to run the outreach for that case so we can coordinate calibration (possible interview feedback loop).

I’ll upload brief notes into the HR case file after the 1:1s.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Head of Product

------------------------------
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Helen Morris <helen@greenway-people.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

Appreciate the quick read-through and owners — that model works. Two requests before we proceed:
1) Please confirm privately if you intend to have a hiring conversation (i.e., discuss external offers) in the 1:1. If yes, copy People Ops immediately after the 1:1 so we can coordinate retention options.
2) Use the enclosed escalation_playbook.pdf for any out-of-scope signals (recruiter outreach, clear intent to leave, or safety concerns).

Greenway will run a 20-minute calibration at 14:00 PT tomorrow to walk through the scoring and redaction approach — link in Drive. I’ll run a brief follow-up summarizing owner confirmations.

Best,
Priya

------------------------------
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Mar 2026 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Survey flags: high-risk attrition list + manager action templates

Quick update after manager outreach this week (summary to attach to HR file):
- Infra IC A (row 12): had a constructive 1:1, accepted a 30% short-term workload reduction and clearer success criteria from manager. No active recruiter activity. Manager to follow weekly for two months.
- Infra IC B (row 17): confirmed outreach from an external recruiter. People Ops to lead retention outreach (Priya, please proceed); manager will stop compensation discussions for now.
- Eng ICs (rows 4 & 7): both would like clearer career ladders; Markus is drafting development plans and connecting them to hiring roadmap.
- Product case (row 23): Priya to lead calibration on promotion feedback and run a synchronous check with the interviewing panel.

Next steps: Priya — please update HR-Case-#4521 with these notes and upload the manager 1:1 summaries. I’ll check back in two weeks for progress.

Evelyn
Director, Infrastructure

[Quoted text hidden]

source.subject: Next steps & AP info: Redwood proposal for NorthSky
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@northsky.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T09:17:00-07:00
Subject: Next steps & AP info: Redwood proposal for NorthSky

Hi Olivia — really appreciate you and the team taking time today. Quick recap from our call and the items we need to keep the procurement path moving: 

- Commercial path: you indicated preference for a purchase order (PO) over card, and possible interest in a marketplace route if it simplifies procurement. Can you confirm whether AWS Marketplace is something procurement would prefer?
- Invoice requirements: please confirm billing address, tax/VAT ID, and whether your AP needs a W-9 or VAT certificate. Also confirm your net terms preference (Net 30/Net 45/etc).
- Quote formatting: Procurement asked for a line-item quote with yearly totals and service descriptions. I’ll attach a draft quote and our standard W-9 for your AP team to review.
- PO template / timing: If you can share your PO template it will speed up things — I can get legal to pre-populate and return.

Next steps: if you can send the AP contact + PO template (or confirm card vs PO and marketplace), we’ll send an updated quote and a DocuSign-ready agreement for signature. If PO, we’ll mark invoice lines so they map cleanly to your PO fields.

Attaching: Redwood_Quote_20260322.pdf, Redwood_W9.pdf

Thanks — talk soon.

Evelyn Hart
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
E: evelyn.hart@redwood.com | M: (415) 555-0192

From: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@northsky.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Next steps & AP info: Redwood proposal for NorthSky

Hi Evelyn — thanks for the summary and the draft quote. Quick answers from AP/procurement: 

- Procurement path: We generally prefer a PO. AWS Marketplace would only be considered if the pricing/terms match and the billing cadence aligns — it’s not our default.
- Invoice format / requirements: Our AP needs the vendor W-9 on file, invoice must include our PO number, line-item breakdown, and total net amount. We are VAT-registered in the EU but for US purchases we need the W-9.
- Net terms: Standard Net 30.
- Billing address: NorthSky Analytics
  Attn: Accounts Payable
  600 Harbor Drive, Suite 400
  San Francisco, CA 94111
- Tax/VAT ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we will provide VAT number if EU entity billed).

I attached the AP PO template for your legal/finance team to review: NorthSky_PO_Template.pdf.

If you can provide a version of the quote with line items that map to the PO (unit price, quantity, service description, total, and tax line) we can route internally and get a PO issued. Also cc accounts@northsky.com on the final invoice.

Thanks,
Olivia Barnes
Director, Finance Operations | NorthSky Analytics
O: (415) 555-2244
olivia.barnes@northsky.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T12:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Next steps & AP info: Redwood proposal for NorthSky

Evelyn / Olivia — thanks for the clarity. Two quick notes from our side: 

1) Quote details: I’ll produce a revised quote that includes per-feature line items (Hosted API, Dedicated reserved capacity, Support add-on) and suggested accounting codes so it maps to a PO cleanly. Expect: unit, qty (annual), unit price, subtotal, tax, total.
2) W-9 and tax: attaching our standard Redwood_W9.pdf here. Olivia — do you need a PDF of our COI (certificate of insurance) as well for procurement?

Priya — can you confirm whether we need to pre-fill any vendor enrollment forms for NorthSky? I’ll include a short note in the quote about invoicing email (accounts@northsky.com) and that Redwood supports Net 30 on PO-backed agreements.

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@northsky.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-22T14:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Next steps & AP info: Redwood proposal for NorthSky

Thanks Marcus — COI is not required at this stage. Confirming a couple quick details so we can move to PO issuance: 

- NorthSky will proceed with a direct vendor invoice (not marketplace).
- Please send final quote PDF with the PO fields filled as discussed.
- Invoice email: accounts@northsky.com (please cc olivia.barnes@northsky.com).
- Net terms: Net 30.
- VAT number for our EU entity (for future cross-border contracts): EU-VAT-GB999999999.

If you can get the populated quote + DocuSign-ready agreement to me by Thursday EOD, we’ll aim to return a PO within 5 business days.

Olivia

source.subject: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health
messages:
From: Sara Patel <sara.patel@luminahealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health
Attachments: Lumina_Security_RFI_v1.pdf (pdf)

Hi David —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We pulled the security checklist together and have three items that are blockers for procurement:

1) Audit logs: we need per-tenant, per-request fields (caller_id, model_id, prompt hash, response id) stored for 365 days and exportable in JSONL.
2) BYOK/KMS: for the pilot we require the ability to rotate keys without disrupting short-term inference for in-flight sessions. If customer keys rotate, Lumina will accept a temporary fallback to provider-managed keys for up to 14 days, but we need an auditable map and a revocation plan.
3) SSO evidence: request logs that show SAML assertion timestamps and token lifetimes for all admin actions.

Can you confirm what of this is supported today, what would be a short-term exception, and roughly how long work would take? We are on a tight timeline for their board review (end of month).

Thanks,
Sara
Security Program Manager
Lumina Health
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Forwarding Lumina's RFI. They listed audit log granularity, a temporary BYOK fallback window, and SSO evidence as procurement blockers.

High level asks:
- Can we map each ask to existing product features / telemetry?
- For items we can't meet in time, what is a safe contractual exception (example: 14-day fallback to Redwood-managed KMS) and what controls/visibility do we need to provide?
- Owners & rough ETA for a short-term patch vs longer roadmap work.

Please triage and reply with owners + next steps. I attached the original RFI to the thread.

—David
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Thanks David — quick triage from Security/Compliance perspective:

Audit logs
- What we have: per-tenant request/response ID, timestamps, and token usage is already logged to our audit stream. Current exports are CSV/Parquet; JSONL export is not yet on the short-term path.
- Short-term option: build a JSONL export shim on top of existing parquet pipeline (low dev risk). Owner: Priya. ETA: 3 weeks for an export endpoint + docs.

BYOK/KMS fallback
- Risk: if we accept a temporary fallback to Redwood-managed keys, we must provide an immutability record mapping customer key id -> provider key id, and a revocation sequence. That mapping must be exportable and time-stamped.
- Technical approach: allow keystore aliasing for a 14-day window; persist mapping in audit ledger and mark all tokens issued under provider-key as temporary. Owner: Ben (eng) + Marissa (product). ETA: 4–6 weeks for prod-grade path, but possible to offer a documented exception (manual process + signed attestation) within 5 business days as a stopgap.

SSO evidence
- We capture SAML assertions at auth time but don't currently retain assertion payloads past troubleshooting TTL. We can add an evidence bucket for admin actions to include assertion metadata (not full assertion) within 2 weeks. Need product sign-off on retention period and PII filtering. Owner: Marissa.

Risks & controls we need if we do a short-term exception:
- Legal needs a template exception that limits fallback duration, requires customer acknowledgement, and includes audit export requirements.
- Post-exception rotation playbook to replace any temporary provider keys and revoke tokens.

I can draft a short technical runbook if you want; tagging Priya for export work and Ben for keystore feasibility.

—Irene
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Mar 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Great breakdown Irene. From Product/PM perspective — proposed action matrix and owners (sketch):

1) JSONL audit export endpoint (surface per-tenant, per-request fields)
   - Owner: Priya (Data infra)
   - Work: small API + export job, add schema docs, add sample JSONL export to support procurement.
   - Short ETA: 3 weeks.

2) BYOK temporary fallback (contract exception + manual off-ramp)
   - Owner: Marissa (PM) + Ben (Eng)
   - Work: document manual exception flow, create a signed attestation template for legal, and build a minimal keystore alias mapping and audit ledger for later automation.
   - Short ETA for manual exception: 5 business days (requires legal + sales sign-off). Prod automation ETA: 6 weeks.

3) SSO assertion evidence for admin actions
   - Owner: Marissa + Security (Irene)
   - Work: retain assertion metadata (not raw assertions), update admin audit schema, add export.
   - ETA: 2 weeks for metadata capture + 1 week for export docs.

Next steps (immediate):
- Ben: confirm that a keystore alias table + timestamped ledger can be implemented behind a feature flag without breaking existing cache/inference flow. Also outline rollback steps.
- Priya: prioritize the JSONL export and provide a minimal sample output we can give Lumina for procurement review.
- Irene: draft the exception template + required audit fields (mapping and revocation stamps).
- David: coordinate with Sales/Legal for the signed exception; we should price the operational lift (short-term manual) as a professional services line item or discount.

I attached a working doc link with the matrix and the quick sample CSV we discussed: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A2bFallbackMatrix

—Marissa
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Quick engineering feasibility notes: 

- Keystore alias table: trivial to add (new DB table + admin API). Main work is wiring alias resolution into the key-lookup path at inference time. That adds a single hop but we should gate behind a flag. No KV-cache incompatibility if we tag KV entries created under provider-key as temporary.
- Rollback: when customer rotates back to their key, we run a batch job to re-associate tokens generated during the fallback window and revoke provider-issued tokens. We'll need a token-revocation queue and an idempotent revocation script.
- Testing: add integration tests for alias resolution, and e2e with prefix caching to ensure latency regression < 3ms per token.

Estimate: manual exception (admin alias creation + ledger entry + attestation) = 2 engineer-days to automate the admin flow; platform & tests to put behind flag = 2 weeks sprint.

I'll put a quick draft of the fallback_matrix.csv in the shared Drive and will follow up with a minimal script for creating alias mappings.

—Ben
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 11:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Thanks team — this is exactly the level of detail I needed. Quick summary and asks for Sales/Legal:

- Offer Lumina a documented manual exception: 14-day fallback to Redwood-managed key with a timestamped mapping ledger and signed attestation. Priya to deliver a sample JSONL export. Ben to provide the admin alias script. Irene to draft the legal exception template.
- Action owners: Priya (export), Ben (alias script + test plan), Marissa (contract exception + pricing), Irene (exception template).
- Timeline: manual exception deliverables within 5 business days; automation and productization target 4–6 weeks.
- Please add costs to HubSpot deal DEAL-7391 and flag for Sales leadership approval if they want to proceed.

I'll loop in Legal & Sales after we have the draft attestation and sample export.

—David

Please triage and reply with owners + next steps. I attached the original RFI to the thread.

—David


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Thanks David — quick triage from Security/Compliance perspective:

Audit logs
- What we have: per-tenant request/response ID, timestamps, and token usage is already logged to our audit stream. Current exports are CSV/Parquet; JSONL export is not yet on the short-term path.
- Short-term option: build a JSONL export shim on top of existing parquet pipeline (low dev risk). Owner: Priya. ETA: 3 weeks for an export endpoint + docs.

BYOK/KMS fallback
- Risk: if we accept a temporary fallback to Redwood-managed keys, we must provide an immutability record mapping customer key id -> provider key id, and a revocation sequence. That mapping must be exportable and time-stamped.
- Technical approach: allow keystore aliasing for a 14-day window; persist mapping in audit ledger and mark all tokens issued under provider-key as temporary. Owner: Ben (eng) + Marissa (product). ETA: 4–6 weeks for prod-grade path, but possible to offer a documented exception (manual process + signed attestation) within 5 business days as a stopgap.

SSO evidence
- We capture SAML assertions at auth time but don't currently retain assertion payloads past troubleshooting TTL. We can add an evidence bucket for admin actions to include assertion metadata (not full assertion) within 2 weeks. Need product sign-off on retention period and PII filtering. Owner: Marissa.

Risks & controls we need if we do a short-term exception:
- Legal needs a template exception that limits fallback duration, requires customer acknowledgement, and includes audit export requirements.
- Post-exception rotation playbook to replace any temporary provider keys and revoke tokens.

I can draft a short technical runbook if you want; tagging Priya for export work and Ben for keystore feasibility.

—Irene


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Mar 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Great breakdown Irene. From Product/PM perspective — proposed action matrix and owners (sketch):

1) JSONL audit export endpoint (surface per-tenant, per-request fields)
   - Owner: Priya (Data infra)
   - Work: small API + export job, add schema docs, add sample JSONL export to support procurement.
   - Short ETA: 3 weeks.

2) BYOK temporary fallback (contract exception + manual off-ramp)
   - Owner: Marissa (PM) + Ben (Eng)
   - Work: document manual exception flow, create a signed attestation template for legal, and build a minimal keystore alias mapping and audit ledger for later automation.
   - Short ETA for manual exception: 5 business days (requires legal + sales sign-off). Prod automation ETA: 6 weeks.

3) SSO assertion evidence for admin actions
   - Owner: Marissa + Security (Irene)
   - Work: retain assertion metadata (not raw assertions), update admin audit schema, add export.
   - ETA: 2 weeks for metadata capture + 1 week for export docs.

Next steps (immediate):
- Ben: confirm that a keystore alias table + timestamped ledger can be implemented behind a feature flag without breaking existing cache/inference flow. Also outline rollback steps.
- Priya: prioritize the JSONL export and provide a minimal sample output we can give Lumina for procurement review.
- Irene: draft the exception template + required audit fields (mapping and revocation stamps).
- David: coordinate with Sales/Legal for the signed exception; we should price the operational lift (short-term manual) as a professional services line item or discount.

I attached a working doc link with the matrix and the quick sample CSV we discussed: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A2bFallbackMatrix

—Marissa


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Quick engineering feasibility notes: 

- Keystore alias table: trivial to add (new DB table + admin API). Main work is wiring alias resolution into the key-lookup path at inference time. That adds a single hop but we should gate behind a flag. No KV-cache incompatibility if we tag KV entries created under provider-key as temporary.
- Rollback: when customer rotates back to their key, we run a batch job to re-associate tokens generated during the fallback window and revoke provider-issued tokens. We'll need a token-revocation queue and an idempotent revocation script.
- Testing: add integration tests for alias resolution, and e2e with prefix caching to ensure latency regression < 3ms per token.

Estimate: manual exception (admin alias creation + ledger entry + attestation) = 2 engineer-days to automate the admin flow; platform & tests to put behind flag = 2 weeks sprint.

I'll put a quick draft of the fallback_matrix.csv in the shared Drive and will follow up with a minimal script for creating alias mappings.

—Ben


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 11:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit log granularity and temporary BYOK window - Lumina Health

Thanks team — this is exactly the level of detail I needed. Quick summary and asks for Sales/Legal:

- Offer Lumina a documented manual exception: 14-day fallback to Redwood-managed key with a timestamped mapping ledger and signed attestation. Priya to deliver a sample JSONL export. Ben to provide the admin alias script. Irene to draft the legal exception template.
- Action owners: Priya (export), Ben (alias script + test plan), Marissa (contract exception + pricing), Irene (exception template).
- Timeline: manual exception deliverables within 5 business days; automation and productization target 4–6 weeks.
- Please add costs to HubSpot deal DEAL-7391 and flag for Sales leadership approval if they want to proceed.

I'll loop in Legal & Sales after we have the draft attestation and sample export.

—David

source.subject: Strategic Tier Switch & Bundle Mapping — HelioBridge Pilot
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@heliobridge.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliobridge.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Packaging question: pilot tier choices and downgrade safety nets

Hi Jonas,

Quick one as we prep the internal review for the HelioBridge pilot next week. We want to lock the pilot terms (90 days) but still keep a clean path to move between the Compact and Throughput tiers without heavy penalties. Two questions:

1) If we start on Compact+Quant (to stay under budget) and need to pivot to Throughput mid-pilot, what is the upgrade process and lead time? Any quota/proration we should expect?

2) If we then decide to scale back at the end of the pilot, do you offer a grace period or credits for unused committed capacity? 

Also, Alicia asked whether we can bundle LLM fine-tuning credits and additional eval runs into the pilot (so we can iterate on prompts). Can you send a one-page summary of the recommended path and the tradeoffs?

Thanks — Samir
Samir Patel
Head of Automation, HelioBridge

From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@heliobridge.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliobridge.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Packaging question: pilot tier choices and downgrade safety nets

Samir — thanks for the clear questions. I’ve attached a short proposal (proposal-v3.pdf) and a feature matrix that maps Compact vs Throughput behaviors. High-level answers up front:

1) Upgrade path (Compact -> Throughput mid-pilot):
- We support immediate upgrades within the same region; the technical switch is typically <1 hour for routing and capacity reassignment.
- Billing/proration: we pro-rate for calendar-day usage. If you upgrade on day 15 of a 30-day cycle, you'll be billed the daily difference between tiers for the remainder of that cycle.
- Capacity lead time: if your upgrade needs more reserved GPUs than current pool affords, we may need a 24–72 hour window to ensure Dedicated capacity; hosted bursts are immediate but have throughput variability.

2) Downgrade & grace policy:
- For pilots we offer a 30-day grace window post-upgrade where we apply unused Dedicated capacity as credits toward future consumption (applies to reserved purchase commitments only).
- If you explicitly commit to a 3- or 6-month reserved block, the standard commitment refund rules apply; I’ve summarized the exceptions in the attached doc.

3) Bundling fine-tuning credits & eval runs:
- We can include a small fine-tuning credit bundle (up to X compute-hours) and 3 additional eval sets as a pilot add-on. This is accounted separately from tier hours (it does not count against Compact throughput).

Next steps I recommend: 1) Decide whether you want the immediate upgrade option with short reserved buffer (recommended) or strict hosted-burst only. 2) If you want fine-tuning credits, confirm target model and number of eval sets and I’ll route to Ben for pricing concession.

Attaching: proposal-v3.pdf (summary + upgrade/downgrade flows) and feature-matrix-HelioBridge.xlsx.

Best,
Jonas Weber
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference

> On Fri, 14 Feb 2026 at 09:12 Samir Patel wrote:
> > Quick one as we prep the internal review for the HelioBridge pilot next week.
> > ...

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@heliobridge.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliobridge.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 17 Feb 2026 11:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strategic Tier Switch & Bundle Mapping — HelioBridge Pilot

Samir / team — putting numbers behind Jonas’ options. Short version: we can offer a pilot-friendly concession if HelioBridge commits to a 6-month runway post-pilot or a minimum $40k committed spend. Options:

A) Flexible pilot (no reserved commit) — 15% off hosted overage rates + 3 eval sets included. No credits on downgrade.
B) Pilot + 3-month reserved buffer (month-to-month billing with reserved overlay) — 22% off tier price, 30-day downgrade grace with credits for unused reserved capacity (applied as account credit).
C) Pilot + 6-month committed block — 30% off tier price, includes fine-tuning credit bundle (up to 50 GPU-hours) and priority capacity routing. 30-day downgrade grace + rollover credits for the first two months.

Notes:
- Upgrade latency guarantees apply to B and C when reserved buffer is selected; hosted-burst only for A.
- We can do a one-time execution credit in the purchase order to cover setup and extra eval runs.

If you want, I’ll draft exact line-item pricing and produce a one-page comparison table for procurement.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Director, Solutions Pricing
Redwood Inference

> On Fri, 14 Feb 2026 at 10:08 Jonas Weber wrote:
> > Samir — thanks for the clear questions. I’ve attached a short proposal...

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: procurement@heliobridge.com, Samir Patel <samir@heliobridge.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliobridge.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Feb 2026 09:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strategic Tier Switch & Bundle Mapping — HelioBridge Pilot

Hi Samir / procurement team — a couple of legal/ops items to surface before you circulate a PO:

- PO language: please include the deal id HB-2026-042 and reference the attached proposal-v3.pdf. That ensures the pilot concession and crediting terms are enforceable.
- SLOs: for option C (6-month commit) we provide an SLA addendum; for A/B you get best-effort routing and incident credits per the standard TOS.
- Data residency & logs: confirm whether you need EU-only routing — that will affect pricing and capacity options; currently this quote assumes US region.

Procurement — if you share the PO draft I’ll review redlines and fast-track our DocuSign path.

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliobridge.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@heliobridge.com>, procurement@heliobridge.com
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 15:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strategic Tier Switch & Bundle Mapping — HelioBridge Pilot

Thanks all — we reviewed options internally and want to move forward with option B (Pilot + 3-month reserved buffer). Two asks before we sign:

1) Please send a one-page pricing table with the exact line items (monthly reserved amount, per-token overage, fine-tuning credit line, and included eval runs).
2) Confirm the 30-day downgrade credit mechanics in writing (how credits appear on invoices).

We’ll aim to get a PO to you by next Wednesday assuming the quote looks like your summary.

Attaching: PO-draft request thread (we’ll send formal PO once we have the quote).

Best,
Alicia Gomez
Director, Platform Engineering
HelioBridge

> On Mon, 17 Feb 2026 at 11:34 Ben Carter wrote:
> > Samir / team — putting numbers behind Jonas’ options...

source.subject: Brightlane — budget owner & procurement intro / next steps
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@brightlanehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Brightlane — follow-up and next steps from discovery

Emily — thanks again for taking the discovery call today. Quick recap so we have alignment and clear next steps.

1) What we heard
- Primary use case: improve triage and clinician workflow summaries for post-acute care teams.
- Key success criteria: 15–20% reduction in time-to-document, accuracy on clinical entity extraction comparable to current tool (~95% on core entities), and no PHI leaving Brightlane's VPC for regulated workflows.
- Timeline: pilot decision targeted in Q3 (budget window confirmed by Emily on call).

2) Stakeholders you mentioned (as we noted on the call)
- Technical champion: Emily Zhang (Data Platform) — owns evaluation and integration scope.
- Potential economic buyer / budget owner: (we asked to confirm) — we discussed looping in a VP of Business Ops or Head of Product.
- Procurement / legal: procurement@brightlanehealth.com (to be introduced).

3) Our ask / proposed next steps

source.subject: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
source.body:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 15, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups

Team — quick leadership update on the CS/Support “known-issues sprint” (planning + execution + first adoption cleanup). We’ve finished publishing the initial batch and wired the cross-links into Support workflows.

1) What shipped (public/internal docs)
- Known Issues landing/index (Support/CS entry point)
  - Confluence landing: /customer-success-and-support/known-issues-and-workarounds/known-issues-index
  - Tagging + “when to escalate” callouts + last-updated discipline
- 5 workaround articles published (curated from top Q1 themes)
  - Streaming timeouts/disconnects + reconnect guidance (links from SDK troubleshooting)
  - 429 quota mismatch / rate-limit headers interpretation
  - Dedicated autoscaling oscillation (how to spot + config knobs)
  - Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations (rollback + what artifacts to collect)
  - Audit log delivery latency (expected SLO + interim mitigations)
- Escalation playbook refresh completed + linked from Support workflows
  - SLA policy by tier + severity mapping + Slack bridge conventions

2) Early metrics (directional, first week)
- Ticket deflection signals (Support):
  - 27 tickets in the last 5 business days where a macro included a known-issues link.
  - Of those, 11 were resolved without engineering escalation (customer confirmed workaround/mitigation in-thread).
  - 6 “repeat tickets” (same symptom, same customer) where the second ticket time-to-triage dropped materially (median first meaningful response down ~35–45% vs prior baseline on the same issue category).
- Time-to-mitigation (subset, escalations matching the published issues):
  - Streaming disconnects and audit log lag cases saw faster alignment on “next action” (workaround vs. incident bridge vs. SRE page). This is largely from having copy/paste-ready diagnostics + clearer escalation criteria.

Caveat: this is a small sample. Chris/Allison are setting up a cleaner ongoing dashboard so we’re not hand-counting.

3) Engineering follow-ups (asks)
To keep this from being a “docs-only” win, we need the associated product/infra follow-through. Top asks for the next 1–2 sprints:
- ENG-4825 (quota enforcement edge case + header consistency): please confirm rollout timeline + any risks of partial behavior by region.
- ENG-4824 (audit log lag metric/alert): align alert thresholds with enterprise expectations and document the runbook link that Support can use.
- ENG-4823 (support-safe dashboard links): canonical “quick view” dashboards for streaming health, latency, error rate, and audit log pipeline.
- ENG-4828 (private installer diagnostics bundle): ensure the diagnostics bundle collects exactly what Support requests in the KB article.
- ENG-4829 (autoscaling guardrails): confirm proposed defaults + whether there’s a safe backport path for existing Dedicated customers.

4) Next steps (ops)
- Dev + Elliot are rolling the macros refresh into the Support queue this week.
- Rachel + Jasmine to confirm we’re aligned on the SLA wording (esp. update cadence language) before we treat the doc as “policy.”

Attachments:
- Known-Issues-Sprint_W07_Results-and-Metrics.pdf
- known-issues_sprint_metrics_snapshot_2026-02-15.csv

If helpful, I can do a 15-min walk-through in staff this week focusing on the “what changed for on-call” angle.

— Jordan
Jordan Blake
Program Ops (CS/Support)
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 15, 2026 at 10:01 AM PST
Subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups

This is great progress.

Two notes from SRE side:
1) Please make sure the “when to escalate” section stays strict on paging thresholds vs. “customer unhappy.” The new wording is much better; we should hold the line so we don’t create an alternate incident process.
2) For ENG-4824 / audit log lag: we can commit to a first pass alert + dashboard this week, but we need Rachel to confirm the expectation we’re anchoring to (what we will and won’t message as an SLA breach).

Happy to review the final “support-safe dashboards” list once Allison shares it.

— Sean


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 15, 2026 at 10:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups

Thanks Jordan.

Eng follow-ups:
- ENG-4825: patch is in final validation now; we’re targeting staged rollout starting Wed (canary -> 25% -> 100%) assuming no regressions.
- ENG-4828: diagnostics bundle PR is ready for review; I’ll ask Ethan/Mina to prioritize so we can match the KB steps exactly.
- ENG-4829: we can land the guardrails, but defaults need SRE sign-off. Sean/Allison — can you propose threshold values that won’t spam pages?

Also: if we can capture “ticket resolved w/ KB link” as a structured field in Jira, we’ll have a much cleaner deflection metric. Dylan/Dev — is that feasible with the current automation work?

— Priya


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>

title: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 15, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups

Team — quick leadership update on the CS/Support “known-issues sprint” (planning + execution + first adoption cleanup). We’ve finished publishing the initial batch and wired the cross-links into Support workflows.

1) What shipped (public/internal docs)
- Known Issues landing/index (Support/CS entry point)
  - Confluence landing: /customer-success-and-support/known-issues-and-workarounds/known-issues-index
  - Tagging + “when to escalate” callouts + last-updated discipline
- 5 workaround articles published (curated from top Q1 themes)
  - Streaming timeouts/disconnects + reconnect guidance (links from SDK troubleshooting)
  - 429 quota mismatch / rate-limit headers interpretation
  - Dedicated autoscaling oscillation (how to spot + config knobs)
  - Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations (rollback + what artifacts to collect)
  - Audit log delivery latency (expected SLO + interim mitigations)
- Escalation playbook refresh completed + linked from Support workflows
  - SLA policy by tier + severity mapping + Slack bridge conventions

2) Early metrics (directional, first week)
- Ticket deflection signals (Support):
  - 27 tickets in the last 5 business days where a macro included a known-issues link.
  - Of those, 11 were resolved without engineering escalation (customer confirmed workaround/mitigation in-thread).
  - 6 “repeat tickets” (same symptom, same customer) where the second ticket time-to-triage dropped materially (median first meaningful response down ~35–45% vs prior baseline on the same issue category).
- Time-to-mitigation (subset, escalations matching the published issues):
  - Streaming disconnects and audit log lag cases saw faster alignment on “next action” (workaround vs. incident bridge vs. SRE page). This is largely from having copy/paste-ready diagnostics + clearer escalation criteria.

Caveat: this is a small sample. Chris/Allison are setting up a cleaner ongoing dashboard so we’re not hand-counting.

3) Engineering follow-ups (asks)
To keep this from being a “docs-only” win, we need the associated product/infra follow-through. Top asks for the next 1–2 sprints:
- ENG-4825 (quota enforcement edge case + header consistency): please confirm rollout timeline + any risks of partial behavior by region.
- ENG-4824 (audit log lag metric/alert): align alert thresholds with enterprise expectations and document the runbook link that Support can use.
- ENG-4823 (support-safe dashboard links): canonical “quick view” dashboards for streaming health, latency, error rate, and audit log pipeline.
- ENG-4828 (private installer diagnostics bundle): ensure the diagnostics bundle collects exactly what Support requests in the KB article.
- ENG-4829 (autoscaling guardrails): confirm proposed defaults + whether there’s a safe backport path for existing Dedicated customers.

4) Next steps (ops)
- Dev + Elliot are rolling the macros refresh into the Support queue this week.
- Rachel + Jasmine to confirm we’re aligned on the SLA wording (esp. update cadence language) before we treat the doc as “policy.”

Attachments:
- Known-Issues-Sprint_W07_Results-and-Metrics.pdf
- known-issues_sprint_metrics_snapshot_2026-02-15.csv

If helpful, I can do a 15-min walk-through in staff this week focusing on the “what changed for on-call” angle.

— Jordan
Jordan Blake
Program Ops (CS/Support)
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Fwd: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)
source.body:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:15 AM
Subject: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Root cause (RRB-17): when CRD migration partially applies, the rollback cleanup hook exits non-zero before clearing `installer-rollback-lock`. The controller treats rollback as failed and schedules another rollback attempt, which sees the lock and repeats. Net effect: "stuck rollback" / infinite rollback retries.

Patch plan:
- In rollback cleanup: clear lock in a `defer`-style best-effort step even if migration revert fails.
- Add lock TTL (annotation with timestamp) and refuse to retry rollback if lock older than N minutes; surface actionable status.
- Add metric `installer.rollback.loop_detected` + alert when >3 retries in 10m.

Workaround (current): delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` and restart installer-controller.

ETA: PR today, release in 2.9.1 if we can get a quick validation run.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:03 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

+1. Please link the PR in INT-330 once up. Also can we update the upgrade runbook today with the safe command sequence + explicit warnings?

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:37 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

I can add loop detection + the metric/alert while you do the cleanup/TTL logic. If 2.9.1 is tight, we can land the metric behind a feature flag.

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 9:02 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Please make sure CS has a customer-safe version of the workaround text (no scary words). Also tracking customer impact notes in the incident thread.

To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 9:02 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Please make sure CS has a customer-safe version of the workaround text (no scary words). Also tracking customer impact notes in the incident thread.
text:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:15 AM
Subject: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Root cause (RRB-17): when CRD migration partially applies, the rollback cleanup hook exits non-zero before clearing `installer-rollback-lock`. The controller treats rollback as failed and schedules another rollback attempt, which sees the lock and repeats. Net effect: "stuck rollback" / infinite rollback retries.

Patch plan:
- In rollback cleanup: clear lock in a `defer`-style best-effort step even if migration revert fails.
- Add lock TTL (annotation with timestamp) and refuse to retry rollback if lock older than N minutes; surface actionable status.
- Add metric `installer.rollback.loop_detected` + alert when >3 retries in 10m.

Workaround (current): delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` and restart installer-controller.

ETA: PR today, release in 2.9.1 if we can get a quick validation run.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:03 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

+1. Please link the PR in INT-330 once up. Also can we update the upgrade runbook today with the safe command sequence + explicit warnings?

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:37 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

I can add loop detection + the metric/alert while you do the cleanup/TTL logic. If 2.9.1 is tight, we can land the metric behind a feature flag.

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 9:02 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Please make sure CS has a customer-safe version of the workaround text (no scary words). Also tracking customer impact notes in the incident thread.

source.subject: Request: short-term credit boost for POC load validation
messages:
From: Sasha Reed <sasha.reed@acmeiot.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@acmeiot.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Short-term credits for stress/load validation

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the Redwood stack performed well in our functional checks. We're lining up a POC stress run next Tuesday to validate latency and throughput at 2–3x expected production concurrency. Our internal test plan (attached) outlines 48 hours of continuous traffic with intermittent spike windows.

We're currently near our trial credit cap and will exhaust tokens if we drive the load we need. Would Redwood be able to provide a promotional credit top-up (one-time) to cover the load test? We're estimating ~100k token-equivalent credits based on the attached concurrency matrix, but we can be flexible if you prefer a smaller uplift and incremental top-ups.

If approved, we also need a brief note for procurement confirming this is a promotional, non-billable top-up tied to DL-00492.

Test artifacts: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3c-acme-poc-plan (acme_poc_testplan.pdf attached)

Thanks — Sasha
Sasha Reed
Head of Product
Acme IoT
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
To: Sasha Reed <sasha.reed@acmeiot.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 12:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Short-term credits for stress/load validation

Hi Sasha —

Appreciate the detailed plan and drive. I reviewed the test matrix and have a few quick clarifying Qs before I escalate for a promotional top-up:

- Are you planning continuous streaming generation or batched requests during the 48h window? (affects token burn model)
- Do you require sustained concurrency at 2–3x, or are the spikes the primary concern?
- Is the traffic fingerprinting (IPs/regions) something we should whitelist ahead of time?

Priya: can you review the attached concurrency matrix and confirm whether our automatic batching/prefix cache will be effective for their workload or if we should recommend a temporary config for the run?

Ethan: if we approve a 100k credit uplift, we'll need a quick action plan for quota application and a backstop rollback. Can you prepare the operational steps (apply credit, monitor burn rate, throttle policy) so we can present both to Acme and to Marissa for approvals?

Sasha — if you can answer the above 1–2 bullets, I'll put this in front of Marissa for a fast-track decision. We aim to have credits in place 24 hours before your run.

Thanks,
Connor
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Short-term credits for stress/load validation — approval request

Marissa — quick approval request: Acme IoT (DL-00492) needs a one-time promotional credit top-up to execute a 48h POC load/latency validation next week. Details below (original note from Sasha and attached plan).

Ask: approve a 100,000 token-equivalent promotional credit (non-billable) to be applied to their trial project for a 7-day window, to ensure they can run the stress tests without space constraints. We estimate this will materially speed decisioning and increase likelihood of a mid-Q2 pilot commitment.

Operational plan (Ethan):
- Apply credit bundle to Acme trial project ID (will share ID once provisioned)
- Monitor token burn and enforce throttling policy if consumption exceeds forecast by >30%
- Revoke remaining promo credits after 7 days, doc both parties

Risks: slight consumption outside estimate (we have throttle policy), and potential accounting audit question (we will label as promotional credit tied to DL-00492).

Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/987654321/DL-00492
Attachments: acme_poc_testplan.pdf, concurrency_matrix.csv

If you can greenlight, I'll confirm with Acme and execute the action plan.

—Connor
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Short-term credits for stress/load validation — approval request

Connor — approved.

Approve 100,000 promotional credit top-up for Acme IoT (DL-00492). Key points to include when you notify the customer: promotional credit applied for load validation only, will be non-billable, expires in 7 days, and will be revoked after expiry. Please ensure Ethan documents the operational steps and that finance is cc'd on the final note.

Priya/Ethan — please coordinate to apply credits tomorrow morning and send me a summary (amount applied, project id, cutover time).

Marissa Cole
Sr. Manager, Sales — Redwood Inference
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
To: Sasha Reed <sasha.reed@acmeiot.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@acmeiot.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Short-term credits for stress/load validation — promo applied

Hi Sasha — good news: Marissa approved the one-time promotional uplift. We've applied 100,000 token-equivalent credits to your Acme POC project (promo_agreement.pdf attached). Summary below:

- Amount: 100,000 token-equivalent promotional credits (non-billable)
- Applied: 2026-02-12 09:00 PT to Acme POC project ID: acme-trial-3421
- Expiry: 7 days from application (2026-02-19 09:00 PT)
- Monitoring: Ethan will monitor burn rate and enforce throttles if consumption exceeds forecast by 30%
- Audit note: label on billing record: "promo: DL-00492 — load validation"

Ethan: please take point on real-time burn monitoring and notify both teams if we need to pause. Priya: any recommended temporary config to reduce token cost (batching/prefix cache) feel free to post here so Acme can implement ahead of the run.

Sasha — best of luck with the stress run. Let us know if you need an on-call from our SREs for the first spike window.

Connor O'Brien
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: promo_agreement.pdf

source.subject: Scheduling: technical architecture deep‑dive (exec sponsor attendance)
messages:
From: Maya Lopez <maya.lopez@crescenthealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Jacob Stern <jacob.stern@crescenthealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Request: architecture deep‑dive with exec sponsor

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the quick demo last week — our infra and product teams want a longer technical deep‑dive to validate integration approaches and run through expected latency/cost tradeoffs for our workload. Our VP of Eng (Evan Harris) and Head of Product want to attend; they prefer a short executive overview followed by a detailed architecture session with your engineering leads.

Can we schedule a 60–75 minute session in the next two weeks that includes:
- A 10–15 minute executive overview for Evan + stakeholders
- 45–60 minute architecture review (deployment options, networking, data handling, observability)

Availability windows that work for us (PST): Tue 2/17 10–12, Wed 2/18 13–16, or Fri 2/20 09–12. If possible, please include Redwood leadership (product/CTO or senior engineering manager) and your solutions engineer.

Also we’ll need a short meeting packet in advance (architecture diagram + list of attendees).

Thanks,
Maya Lopez
Senior Manager, Platform Integrations
Crescent Health

---

Attachments: none
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference.com>
To: Maya Lopez <maya.lopez@crescenthealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.inference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: architecture deep‑dive with exec sponsor

Hi Maya — great to hear Crescent is moving quickly here. Happy to set this up and we’ll make sure the right people attend. Tentative plan from our side: 15min exec overview (Priya Natarajan, VP Product), then full architecture review with engineering (Vivek + me).

Of the windows you suggested, we can do:
- Tue 2/17 10:30–11:45 PST (best)
- Fri 2/20 09:00–10:15 PST (backup)

If Tuesday works for you I’ll send a calendar invite and an agenda. We can attach a short architecture packet (high level) ahead of time.

Quick ask: can you confirm who from Crescent will join besides Evan and Jacob (names/titles) so we include them on the agenda?

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference.com
+1 415‑555‑0124

Attachments: none
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.inference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 08:44:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: architecture deep‑dive with exec sponsor

Forwarding — quick coordination note.

Aditya / Lauren — I can cover deep technical parts (runtime, batching, KV cache, quantization profiles). Priya, can you join for the 15min exec overview? If yes, I’ll draft the agenda and a 2‑page architecture overview.

We should produce:
- 1-page architecture diagram (PDF)
- 2‑slide exec summary (for Evan)
- 1 technical appendix with expected latency numbers and deployment variants

I’ll attach the draft architecture doc to the invite.

Vivek
Sr. Eng Manager, Serving Runtime
Vivek Kulkarni

Attachments (draft): redwood_architecture_overview.pdf (stub)
From: Maya Lopez <maya.lopez@crescenthealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Evan Harris <evan.harris@crescenthealth.com>, Jacob Stern <jacob.stern@crescenthealth.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 07:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: architecture deep‑dive with exec sponsor

Thanks — Tue 2/17 10:30–11:45 PST works for Evan and the team. Evan (VP Eng) will join for the full session and we’ll have Jacob (infra lead) in the architecture portion. Evan asked that the executive overview include a short summary of SLA/availability options and fallbacks.

Attendees from Crescent:
- Evan Harris, VP Eng <evan.harris@crescenthealth.com>
- Maya Lopez, Sr Manager, Platform Integrations <maya.lopez@crescenthealth.com>
- Jacob Stern, Infrastructure Lead <jacob.stern@crescenthealth.com>

Please send calendar invite and agenda + the 1‑page architecture diagram ahead of the meeting.

Maya

> Hi Aditya,
> 
> Thanks again for the quick demo last week — our infra and product teams want a longer technical deep‑dive...

To: Maya Lopez <maya.lopez@crescenthealth.com>, Evan Harris <evan.harris@crescenthealth.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.inference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Confirmed: Tue 2/17 10:30–11:45 PST — Crescent x Redwood technical deep‑dive (calendar invite)

Thanks all — calendar invite sent: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=redwood-crescent-deepdive-20260212

Agenda (60–75m):
- 10:30–10:40 (10m) — Executive overview (Priya Natarajan) — business objectives, SLAs, routing/fallbacks
- 10:40–11:30 (50m) — Architecture deep‑dive (Vivek + Aditya) — serving runtime, network topology, KV cache, quantization options, deployment variants
- 11:30–11:45 (15m) — Q&A and next steps (action items / mutual action plan)

source.subject: Runtime data carveout / rollback clause — product & security sign-off needed
source.messages:
From: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 15:22:00 -0500
Subject: DPA redline + runtime carveout request

Hi Rishi — thanks again for the call today. As discussed, Legal at Helios attached a clean redline of our DPA with two asks that are blocking procurement: 1) a carveout for ephemeral runtime telemetry used solely for model quality debugging (retention <= 7 days), and 2) an explicit rollback/rollback-notice clause if a subprocessor change introduces a data handling change.

We're flexible on language but need Product & Security to confirm it won't prevent your roadmap (esp. dedicated/private customers), or whether we need an exception path. Attaching our redline.

Sarah
Sr. Contracts Manager, Helios Health

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 16:03:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (routing to Product & Security)

Team — forwarding Helios' DPA redline. Two asks highlighted: 1) telemetry carveout (7d retention max) and 2) rollback-notice clause for subprocessor/model changes. Legal is okay with our counter if Product/Security confirm technical feasibility and acceptable risk.

Immediate questions for Product: does a 7-day retention window for runtime telemetry (KV cache snapshots + token-level traces used only for debugging) fit current pipelines? What would be the impact on observability and our Evaluate/Optimize features?

For Security: can we accept the proposed rollback-notice text (they want 30 days notice plus a rollback right) or do we need to require a staged canary with automatic fallback?

Please review the attached Helios_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf and Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx and reply with the concise decision (Accept / Accept with mitigations / Reject + reason). This is blocking the PO.

Thanks — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:10:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (security assessment)

Rishi / team — quick assessment from Security: the telemetry carveout for debugging (7-day retention) is acceptable if we can ensure three controls:

- Scope restriction: telemetry must be pseudonymized and limited to runtime diagnostics (no full request payloads or PII).
- Access controls: telemetry must be accessible only to a named small group (SRE/ML-ops) with time-limited access tokens and audited access logs.
- Auto-expiry: retention enforcement must be automatic and cryptographically deletable (no manual exception without security approval).

Regarding the rollback clause: 30 days notice plus an explicit rollback right is risky for our downstream commitments. We recommend: 1) accept a notice period (30 days) but swap the unilateral rollback right for a documented staged rollback plan (canary + automatic fallback) that we can execute. Otherwise, we expose customers and our infra to sudden dependency flips.

I attached Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx with specifics. If Product can confirm the staged canary approach is implementable within Helios' timeline, Security can sign off with the mitigation language we propose in proposed-rollback-clause.txt.

-Olga
Head of Security Engineering

---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 11:42:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (legal suggested language)

Rishi — adding Legal's take. We prefer as little product work as possible but want a clean risk transfer. Suggested compromise in proposed-rollback-clause.txt: Helios gets 30 calendar days' notice of any subprocessor change that materially affects data handling; Redwood will provide a documented rollback plan and 7-day remediation window during which Helios can request a rollback into the prior subprocessor configuration. Rollback execution requires coordination and may be subject to technical feasibility; if impossible, Redwood will provide a documented remediation/compensation plan.

If Product & Security accept the staged rollback + auto-expiry telemetry controls, Legal is comfortable with countersigning. If Product rejects the staged approach, Legal's fallback is to propose a shorter notice plus stricter indemnities (undesirable).

-Ben
Sr. Contracts Counsel

---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 08:05:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (product assessment)

Rishi / all — Product assessment and roadmap impact:

- Telemetry retention (7d): feasible. We already batch KV cache snapshot uploads to S3 and purge within 14 days in dev/test; switching to an enforced 7-day TTL is a modest infra change (work: ~2 engineer-weeks with an ops playbook). Impact to Evaluate/Optimize: small — we will need to surface only aggregated metrics for longer-term trending; per-request traces older than 7 days will be unavailable.

- Staged rollback plan: implementable but not trivial. Requires: per-subprocessor build of canary routing, automated fallback hooks, and an expanded runbook for rollback. Estimated effort: initial implementation across Dedicated/Hosted path ~6-8 engineer-weeks, with follow-up QA. For Helios' timeline (they want contract in 3 weeks), we can offer a timeboxed exception: accept the staged rollback language if we commit to delivering the automation within 90 days and provide manual rollback assistance during that period under defined SLAs.

Product recommendation: Accept telemetry carveout with Security controls (scope + auto-expiry + access audit). For rollback, accept conditional approval: Product will sign off if Legal inserts the 90-day implementation commitment clause and Helios accepts a manual rollback support window (48-72 hours) until automation is complete. If Helios insists on immediate automated rollback rights, we should negotiate indemnities or push to executive review.

-Priya
Director, Product Management

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 09:47:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (consolidated ask + next steps)

Sarah — thanks for the redline and patience. Summary of our internal position and suggested path:

1) Telemetry carveout: ACCEPT with Security controls (pseudonymization, access-limited, auto-expiry at 7 days).
2) Rollback: CONDITIONALLY ACCEPT. Legal & Product propose: 30-day notice, 90-day delivery commitment for automated staged rollback + manual rollback support (48-72 hours) during implementation. Security will require the staged canary/fallback plan as part of the 90-day delivery.

If Helios accepts those two bullets, Legal will prepare a counter-redline and we can route for signature. If Helios cannot accept the 90-day remediation window, escalate to execs for decision (we'll need to evaluate indemnity or carveouts).

Next steps: Ben will push the counter-redline to DocuSign (link below). Sarah — can Helios confirm acceptance of the 90-day implementation path by EOD Friday? If yes, we'll proceed.

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/helios/dpa-v3
HubSpot: https://hubspot.example/HL-98231

Thanks everyone — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

[Attachment stubs included in thread: Helios_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf (pdf), Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx (xlsx), proposed-rollback-clause.txt (txt)]
