For Helios' timeline (they want contract in 3 weeks), we can offer a timeboxed exception: accept the staged rollback language if we commit to delivering the automation within 90 days and provide manual rollback assistance during that period under defined SLAs.

Product recommendation: Accept telemetry carveout with Security controls (scope + auto-expiry + access audit). For rollback, accept conditional approval: Product will sign off if Legal inserts the 90-day implementation commitment clause and Helios accepts a manual rollback support window (48-72 hours) until automation is complete. If Helios insists on immediate automated rollback rights, we should negotiate indemnities or push to executive review.

-Priya
Director, Product Management

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 09:47:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (consolidated ask + next steps)

Sarah — thanks for the redline and patience. Summary of our internal position and suggested path:

1) Telemetry carveout: ACCEPT with Security controls (pseudonymization, access-limited, auto-expiry at 7 days).
2) Rollback: CONDITIONALLY ACCEPT. Legal & Product propose: 30-day notice, 90-day delivery commitment for automated staged rollback + manual rollback support (48-72 hours) during implementation. Security will require the staged canary/fallback plan as part of the 90-day delivery.

If Helios accepts those two bullets, Legal will prepare a counter-redline and we can route for signature. If Helios cannot accept the 90-day remediation window, escalate to execs for decision (we'll need to evaluate indemnity or carveouts).

Next steps: Ben will push the counter-redline to DocuSign (link below). Sarah — can Helios confirm acceptance of the 90-day implementation path by EOD Friday? If yes, we'll proceed.

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/helios/dpa-v3
HubSpot: https://hubspot.example/HL-98231

Thanks everyone — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

[Attachment stubs included in thread: Helios_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf (pdf), Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx (xlsx), proposed-rollback-clause.txt (txt)]
text:
From: Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 15:22:00 -0500
Subject: DPA redline + runtime carveout request

Hi Rishi — thanks again for the call today. As discussed, Legal at Helios attached a clean redline of our DPA with two asks that are blocking procurement: 1) a carveout for ephemeral runtime telemetry used solely for model quality debugging (retention <= 7 days), and 2) an explicit rollback/rollback-notice clause if a subprocessor change introduces a data handling change.

We're flexible on language but need Product & Security to confirm it won't prevent your roadmap (esp. dedicated/private customers), or whether we need an exception path. Attaching our redline.

Sarah
Sr. Contracts Manager, Helios Health

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 16:03:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (routing to Product & Security)

Team — forwarding Helios' DPA redline. Two asks highlighted: 1) telemetry carveout (7d retention max) and 2) rollback-notice clause for subprocessor/model changes. Legal is okay with our counter if Product/Security confirm technical feasibility and acceptable risk.

Immediate questions for Product: does a 7-day retention window for runtime telemetry (KV cache snapshots + token-level traces used only for debugging) fit current pipelines? What would be the impact on observability and our Evaluate/Optimize features?

For Security: can we accept the proposed rollback-notice text (they want 30 days notice plus a rollback right) or do we need to require a staged canary with automatic fallback?

Please review the attached Helios_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf and Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx and reply with the concise decision (Accept / Accept with mitigations / Reject + reason). This is blocking the PO.

Thanks — Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:10:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (security assessment)

Rishi / team — quick assessment from Security: the telemetry carveout for debugging (7-day retention) is acceptable if we can ensure three controls:

- Scope restriction: telemetry must be pseudonymized and limited to runtime diagnostics (no full request payloads or PII).
- Access controls: telemetry must be accessible only to a named small group (SRE/ML-ops) with time-limited access tokens and audited access logs.
- Auto-expiry: retention enforcement must be automatic and cryptographically deletable (no manual exception without security approval).

Regarding the rollback clause: 30 days notice plus an explicit rollback right is risky for our downstream commitments. We recommend: 1) accept a notice period (30 days) but swap the unilateral rollback right for a documented staged rollback plan (canary + automatic fallback) that we can execute. Otherwise, we expose customers and our infra to sudden dependency flips.

I attached Telemetry_SA-Notes.xlsx with specifics. If Product can confirm the staged canary approach is implementable within Helios' timeline, Security can sign off with the mitigation language we propose in proposed-rollback-clause.txt.

-Olga
Head of Security Engineering

---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 11:42:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (legal suggested language)

Rishi — adding Legal's take. We prefer as little product work as possible but want a clean risk transfer. Suggested compromise in proposed-rollback-clause.txt: Helios gets 30 calendar days' notice of any subprocessor change that materially affects data handling; Redwood will provide a documented rollback plan and 7-day remediation window during which Helios can request a rollback into the prior subprocessor configuration. Rollback execution requires coordination and may be subject to technical feasibility; if impossible, Redwood will provide a documented remediation/compensation plan.

If Product & Security accept the staged rollback + auto-expiry telemetry controls, Legal is comfortable with countersigning. If Product rejects the staged approach, Legal's fallback is to propose a shorter notice plus stricter indemnities (undesirable).

-Ben
Sr. Contracts Counsel

---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sarah Lowe <s.lowe@helioshealth.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 08:05:00 -0500
Subject: RE: DPA redline + runtime carveout request (product assessment)

Rishi / all — Product assessment and roadmap impact:

- Telemetry retention (7d): feasible. We already batch KV cache snapshot uploads to S3 and purge within 14 days in dev/test; switching to an enforced 7-day TTL is a modest infra change (work: ~2 engineer-weeks with an ops playbook). Impact to Evaluate/Optimize: small — we will need to surface only aggregated metrics for longer-term trending; per-request traces older than 7 days will be unavailable.

- Staged rollback plan: implementable but not trivial. Requires: per-subprocessor build of canary routing, automated fallback hooks, and an expanded runbook for rollback. Estimated effort: initial implementation across Dedicated/Hosted path ~6-8 engineer-weeks, with follow-up QA. For Helios' timeline (they want contract in 3 weeks), we can offer a timeboxed exception: accept the staged rollback language if we commit to delivering the automation within 90 days and provide manual rollback assistance during that period under defined SLAs.

Product recommendation: Accept telemetry carveout with Security controls (scope + auto-expiry + access audit). For rollback, accept conditional approval: Product will sign off if Legal inserts the 90-day implementation commitment clause and Helios accepts a manual rollback support window (48-72 hours) until automation is complete. If Helios insists on immediate automated rollback rights, we should negotiate indemnities or push to executive review.

-Priya
Director, Product Management

Sarah — thanks for the redline and patience. Summary of our internal position and suggested path:

1) Telemetry carveout: ACCEPT with Security controls (pseudonymization, access-limited, auto-expiry at 7 days).
2) Rollback: CONDITIONALLY ACCEPT. Legal & Product propose: 30-day notice, 90-day delivery commitment for automated staged rollback + manual rollback support (48-72 hours) during implementation. Security will require the staged canary/fallback plan as part of the 90-day delivery.

If Helios accepts those two bullets, Legal will prepare a counter-redline and we can route for signature. If Helios cannot accept the 90-day remediation window, escalate to execs for decision (we'll need to evaluate indemnity or carveouts).

source.subject: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
source.body:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 7:42 AM PST
Subject: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
Attachments: Acme_streaming_escalation_summary_2026-02-07.pdf, Acme_customer_email_draft_v2.txt

Team — escalating Acme AI (enterprise) streaming timeouts/disconnects in us-east. They’re requesting (a) an exec-owned written update they can forward internally, and (b) clarity on potential credits given the customer impact.

I need approvals by 10:00am PT on:
1) Customer-facing language (attached draft) — specifically what we can state re: cause + “resolved” wording.
2) Whether we should proactively offer any goodwill / service credits vs. wait for their formal request + post-incident report.

Summary (details in PDF; ticket: SUP-1842):
- Customer: Acme AI (Corp)
- Symptom: SSE streaming responses resetting mid-generation (connection resets / truncated streams). Non-streaming less affected.
- Scope: us-east primarily; intermittent. Most visible on long generations.
- Current status: Mitigated overnight. Still monitoring; no new spike since ~00:10 PT.
- Mitigation: routing adjustments + gateway config rollback; recommended client-side timeout/retry guidance shared with Acme.

Customer asks / context:
- They have an internal exec review Monday and want a statement today.
- They asked for “credits for the incident window” on the call (Rachel has notes).

Proposed customer email language (high-level):
- Acknowledge impact + timeline
- Confirm mitigation applied
- Provide temporary client-side mitigation guidance
- Commit to follow-up RCA within 5 business days
- Avoid promising “fixed permanently” until we confirm longer soak

Please reply-all with:
- “Approved” / edits on the attached customer email
- Credits guidance (ok to offer X%, or wait)

Thanks,
Jasmine

—
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success & Support
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Adding SRE technical color for customer-safe wording.

What happened (customer-safe):
- We saw an elevated rate of mid-stream disconnects for streaming requests in us-east, concentrated on longer-lived connections.
- Non-streaming requests had normal success rates; this was primarily a streaming transport stability issue.

Most likely cause (keep high level):
- An interaction between a gateway config change and connection handling under elevated concurrency in us-east led to increased connection resets for long-lived streaming responses.

What we did:
- Rolled back the relevant gateway config and applied a conservative keepalive/connection-handling adjustment.
- Confirmed streaming reset rate returned to baseline in us-east post-mitigation.

Confidence / cautions on wording:
- I’d avoid “root cause fully resolved” in the email. Better: “Mitigation applied; metrics back to baseline; monitoring closely.”
- We can commit to an RCA with preventative follow-ups.

If Acme asks for immediate guidance:
- Recommend setting an explicit client-side read timeout appropriate for their max generation length, and implementing retry with backoff on network disconnects.
- If they can, temporarily switch to HTTP/1.1 for streaming clients behind aggressive proxies (we’ve seen fewer resets).

Rafael



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:21 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

+1 to Rafael’s guidance.

On the comms draft:
- Approve if we remove/soften any “we identified the root cause” phrasing. I suggest:
  - “We identified a likely contributing factor in gateway connection handling under load and applied a rollback + configuration adjustment.”
  - “Streaming stability metrics have returned to baseline; we’re continuing to monitor.”
- Keep the “RCA within 5 business days” commitment.

On credits:
- Recommend we do not proactively promise credits in the same note as mitigation.
- Suggest language: “If you’d like to request service credits per your agreement, we can evaluate once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment.”

Reason: avoids creating a precedent / commitment before we have the full incident accounting and contract interpretation.

Sean



From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Customer context + what they’re asking for:

- Morgan Lee (Acme Eng) is the point; they looped IT Ops because their internal dashboards lit up as “app instability.” They want a note they can forward to their VP Eng.
- They explicitly asked: “Are we going to get credits? We had a customer-visible incident.”
- They also asked whether they should pin traffic away from us-east for now.

My suggestions for the email:
- Include one sentence recommending temporary routing away from us-east *only if* they can do so without violating their internal requirements. Otherwise, we keep the guidance focused on retries/timeouts.
- Re: credits: agree with Sean — don’t promise. But we should reassure we’ll respond quickly if they submit a request.
- Update cadence: we told them we’d send a written update this morning and another check-in by EOD if anything changes. That’s aligned with what they expect.

Also: please keep wording very explicit that the workaround guidance is temporary and we’ll follow up with long-term prevention.

Rachel



From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Adding Sales POV.

Acme is in the middle of expanding usage this quarter. If we go too “legalistic” on credits, we may take a hit in the expansion motion.

Question: are we comfortable with a *goodwill* credit (separate from contractual service credits) once stabilized, e.g., a one-time usage credit applied to next invoice, pending Jasmine approval?

I’m aligned with not committing in writing today. But if Morgan pushes on the call, I’d like a clear internal decision tree:
- If they ask verbally: do we say “we’ll evaluate and get back by X date” (what date)?
- Who signs off (Jasmine vs. Finance)?

Avery



From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 10:19 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Thanks all — incorporating edits now.

Decision / approvals:
1) Comms language: approved with Sean + Rafael’s edits.
- We will NOT state “root cause confirmed” or “permanently fixed.”
- We WILL state: mitigation applied, streaming stability metrics back to baseline, continued monitoring, RCA to follow.
- We WILL include temporary mitigation guidance (timeouts/retry; optional HTTP/1.1 note) and keep it explicitly “temporary.”

2) Credits:
- We will not proactively offer credits in today’s email.
- Verbal guidance if pressed: “We take this seriously and will evaluate service credits per your agreement once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment. We’ll get back to you by Wednesday, Feb 11.”
- Any goodwill credit beyond contractual service credits requires my approval; loop me + Avery.

Rachel: please send the finalized email to Morgan once I drop it into this thread (I’ll forward the final text in ~15 min). Rafael/Sean: thank you — please keep an eye on us-east streaming metrics through the afternoon and post in SUP-1842 if anything moves.

Jasmine

—
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success & Support
Redwood Inference

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 10:19 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Thanks all — incorporating edits now.

Decision / approvals:
1) Comms language: approved with Sean + Rafael’s edits.
- We will NOT state “root cause confirmed” or “permanently fixed.”
- We WILL state: mitigation applied, streaming stability metrics back to baseline, continued monitoring, RCA to follow.
- We WILL include temporary mitigation guidance (timeouts/retry; optional HTTP/1.1 note) and keep it explicitly “temporary.”

2) Credits:
- We will not proactively offer credits in today’s email.
- Verbal guidance if pressed: “We take this seriously and will evaluate service credits per your agreement once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment. We’ll get back to you by Wednesday, Feb 11.”
- Any goodwill credit beyond contractual service credits requires my approval; loop me + Avery.

Rachel: please send the finalized email to Morgan once I drop it into this thread (I’ll forward the final text in ~15 min). Rafael/Sean: thank you — please keep an eye on us-east streaming metrics through the afternoon and post in SUP-1842 if anything moves.

Jasmine

—
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success & Support
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 7:42 AM PST
Subject: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
Attachments: Acme_streaming_escalation_summary_2026-02-07.pdf, Acme_customer_email_draft_v2.txt

Team — escalating Acme AI (enterprise) streaming timeouts/disconnects in us-east. They’re requesting (a) an exec-owned written update they can forward internally, and (b) clarity on potential credits given the customer impact.

I need approvals by 10:00am PT on:
1) Customer-facing language (attached draft) — specifically what we can state re: cause + “resolved” wording.
2) Whether we should proactively offer any goodwill / service credits vs. wait for their formal request + post-incident report.

Summary (details in PDF; ticket: SUP-1842):
- Customer: Acme AI (Corp)
- Symptom: SSE streaming responses resetting mid-generation (connection resets / truncated streams). Non-streaming less affected.
- Scope: us-east primarily; intermittent. Most visible on long generations.
- Current status: Mitigated overnight. Still monitoring; no new spike since ~00:10 PT.
- Mitigation: routing adjustments + gateway config rollback; recommended client-side timeout/retry guidance shared with Acme.

Customer asks / context:
- They have an internal exec review Monday and want a statement today.
- They asked for “credits for the incident window” on the call (Rachel has notes).

Proposed customer email language (high-level):
- Acknowledge impact + timeline
- Confirm mitigation applied
- Provide temporary client-side mitigation guidance
- Commit to follow-up RCA within 5 business days
- Avoid promising “fixed permanently” until we confirm longer soak

Please reply-all with:
- “Approved” / edits on the attached customer email
- Credits guidance (ok to offer X%, or wait)

Thanks,
Jasmine

—
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success & Support
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Adding SRE technical color for customer-safe wording.

What happened (customer-safe):
- We saw an elevated rate of mid-stream disconnects for streaming requests in us-east, concentrated on longer-lived connections.
- Non-streaming requests had normal success rates; this was primarily a streaming transport stability issue.

Most likely cause (keep high level):
- An interaction between a gateway config change and connection handling under elevated concurrency in us-east led to increased connection resets for long-lived streaming responses.

What we did:
- Rolled back the relevant gateway config and applied a conservative keepalive/connection-handling adjustment.
- Confirmed streaming reset rate returned to baseline in us-east post-mitigation.

Confidence / cautions on wording:
- I’d avoid “root cause fully resolved” in the email. Better: “Mitigation applied; metrics back to baseline; monitoring closely.”
- We can commit to an RCA with preventative follow-ups.

If Acme asks for immediate guidance:
- Recommend setting an explicit client-side read timeout appropriate for their max generation length, and implementing retry with backoff on network disconnects.
- If they can, temporarily switch to HTTP/1.1 for streaming clients behind aggressive proxies (we’ve seen fewer resets).

Rafael



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:21 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

+1 to Rafael’s guidance.

On the comms draft:
- Approve if we remove/soften any “we identified the root cause” phrasing. I suggest:
  - “We identified a likely contributing factor in gateway connection handling under load and applied a rollback + configuration adjustment.”
  - “Streaming stability metrics have returned to baseline; we’re continuing to monitor.”
- Keep the “RCA within 5 business days” commitment.

On credits:
- Recommend we do not proactively promise credits in the same note as mitigation.
- Suggest language: “If you’d like to request service credits per your agreement, we can evaluate once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment.”

Reason: avoids creating a precedent / commitment before we have the full incident accounting and contract interpretation.

Sean



From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 8:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Customer context + what they’re asking for:

- Morgan Lee (Acme Eng) is the point; they looped IT Ops because their internal dashboards lit up as “app instability.” They want a note they can forward to their VP Eng.
- They explicitly asked: “Are we going to get credits? We had a customer-visible incident.”
- They also asked whether they should pin traffic away from us-east for now.

My suggestions for the email:
- Include one sentence recommending temporary routing away from us-east *only if* they can do so without violating their internal requirements. Otherwise, we keep the guidance focused on retries/timeouts.
- Re: credits: agree with Sean — don’t promise. But we should reassure we’ll respond quickly if they submit a request.
- Update cadence: we told them we’d send a written update this morning and another check-in by EOD if anything changes. That’s aligned with what they expect.

Also: please keep wording very explicit that the workaround guidance is temporary and we’ll follow up with long-term prevention.

Rachel



From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Adding Sales POV.

Acme is in the middle of expanding usage this quarter. If we go too “legalistic” on credits, we may take a hit in the expansion motion.

Question: are we comfortable with a *goodwill* credit (separate from contractual service credits) once stabilized, e.g., a one-time usage credit applied to next invoice, pending Jasmine approval?

I’m aligned with not committing in writing today. But if Morgan pushes on the call, I’d like a clear internal decision tree:
- If they ask verbally: do we say “we’ll evaluate and get back by X date” (what date)?
- Who signs off (Jasmine vs. Finance)?

Avery



From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>

source.subject: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
source.body:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Hi all —

We’re ready for cross-functional review on the refreshed Support escalation playbook (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). Goal is to publish the Confluence pages early next week and start using the new severity + SLA language immediately for new escalations.

Docs to review:
- Escalation Playbook Refresh v0.9 (attached)
- Sign-off checklist (attached)
- Working notes (Drive): shared_drives/customer-success/escalation-playbook-refresh-working-notes

Requested sign-off by: Thu 2/5 EOD PT

What I need from each group:
1) SRE (Sean/Rafael)
   - Confirm paging thresholds + escalation triggers are consistent with on-call + SLO alerting
   - Confirm Slack bridge / #incidents handoff rules
   - Confirm we are not creating “backdoor paging” paths

2) Platform (Priya)
   - Confirm ownership boundaries + primary/backup contacts for: SSO, Audit Logs, Quota/Rate Limits, Routing/Fallback, Private installer
   - Confirm after-hours escalation expectations for Private/Dedicated

3) CS leadership (Jasmine/Rachel)
   - Confirm SLA language aligns with tiering + contract expectations
   - Confirm update cadence wording (especially enterprise) and credits/escalation triggers

Sign-off checklist (what I’m looking for in replies):
- [ ] Severity rubric: P0–P4 mapping to Sev + examples make sense
- [ ] Tiered SLAs: first response + update cadence + escalation thresholds are correct
- [ ] Escalation matrix: who/when/how to page is correct + backup coverage
- [ ] Slack bridge convention: naming + required context + who owns customer updates
- [ ] Jira guidance: required fields + SLA timers + linking conventions are workable
- [ ] Exec escalation: rules + summary template are accurate

How to respond:
- Please reply-all with either “Approved” or “Blocker” + the specific section/page. If “Blocker,” include the proposed replacement wording.

Thanks — this will unblock the known-issues sprint workflows + reduce the ping-pong during incidents.

Dev Patel
Support Ops Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- Escalation_Playbook_Refresh_v0.9.pdf
- Signoff_Checklist_Escalation_Playbook_v1.xlsx


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Reviewed. Mostly good — one blocker + a couple nits.

BLOCKER:
- SLA table currently implies we page SRE for “P1 enterprise” by default even if impact is isolated to a single tenant with an available workaround.
  - Proposed wording change: make paging trigger “customer-impacting outage/degradation meeting Sev1+ thresholds” rather than “customer tier.” Tier should influence update cadence + comms, not paging.
  - Specifically: in Escalation Triggers, replace “Enterprise P1 => page SRE” with “Page SRE when: (a) elevated error rate/latency affecting multiple customers or a region, OR (b) one customer but no workaround and business-critical workflow blocked.”

Nits:
- Please add “request IDs + region + model + approximate start time + recent deploy (if known)” as required fields in the page template. That’s the minimum for on-call.
- Bridge naming: can we standardize on “sup-<ticket>-<region>-<shortdesc>” so it’s searchable and tied to Jira?

If you incorporate the blocker wording, I’m good to approve.

Sean
SRE


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 1:04 PM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Thanks Dev.

Platform ownership looks correct with two changes:
- Quota/Rate Limits: primary = Control Plane on-call (Platform), backup = SRE if it’s correlated with broader degradation. Please avoid directing Support to ping individual engineers.
- Audit Logs: primary = Platform (data pipeline), but escalation path should include Security/Compliance only if customer is requesting formal attestation / evidence (otherwise it’s a Platform+SRE operational issue).

Other notes:
- Private installer issues: if a customer is mid-upgrade and stuck, we should trigger a Platform page once “rollback not possible” is confirmed (your checklist mentions this; just make it a bold callout).

Not a blocker. If you incorporate the ownership text above, I’m approved.

Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Reviewed from CS + enterprise SLA perspective.

Edits requested (not blockers):
- “Every 30 minutes” updates for enterprise P0: please clarify that cadence is during active mitigation window; if we’re “Waiting on Customer” we should explicitly state we will resume updates when new info is available, but still keep a time-based check-in (e.g., every 2 hours) to avoid silence.
- Credits guidance: please add “credits decisions require CS leadership approval” (Jasmine or me) and should not be promised in the initial acknowledgement.

Otherwise approved.

Rachel Kim
Enterprise Customer Success


---

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:44 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Approved with Rachel’s wording change on update cadence + credits.

One additional request: in the comms templates, add a single sentence that sets expectations on what we can share (“we’ll share mitigation steps and status; we may not be able to share full root cause immediately”).

Thanks for pushing this through.

Jasmine Liu
VP Customer Success & Support


---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 11:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Thanks all — incorporated changes and closing out sign-off.

Updates made (v1.0 draft in Confluence now):
- Paging triggers updated per Sean: tier influences comms cadence; paging based on Sev thresholds / lack of workaround. (Also added required context: request IDs, region, model, start time, recent deploy.)
- Bridge naming standardized to: sup-<SUP#>-<region>-<shortdesc>
- Platform ownership updated per Priya (quota/rate limits + audit logs + private installer bold callout)
- Enterprise comms cadence clarified per Rachel (active mitigation vs Waiting on Customer) + added explicit “credits require CS leadership approval; do not promise”
- Added comms template line per Jasmine re: what we can/can’t share during active incident

Next steps:
- I’m kicking off INT-332/INT-333 updates today so workflows match the playbook (bridge template + Jira required fields/SLA reminders).
- Target publish date remains Mon 2/9 (with internal announcement).

If anyone has final blockers after the edits, please reply by end of day. Otherwise I’ll treat this as final approval.

Dev

- Platform ownership updated per Priya (quota/rate limits + audit logs + private installer bold callout)
- Enterprise comms cadence clarified per Rachel (active mitigation vs Waiting on Customer) + added explicit “credits require CS leadership approval; do not promise”
- Added comms template line per Jasmine re: what we can/can’t share during active incident

Next steps:
- I’m kicking off INT-332/INT-333 updates today so workflows match the playbook (bridge template + Jira required fields/SLA reminders).
- Target publish date remains Mon 2/9 (with internal announcement).

If anyone has final blockers after the edits, please reply by end of day. Otherwise I’ll treat this as final approval.

Dev
text:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Hi all —

We’re ready for cross-functional review on the refreshed Support escalation playbook (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). Goal is to publish the Confluence pages early next week and start using the new severity + SLA language immediately for new escalations.

Docs to review:
- Escalation Playbook Refresh v0.9 (attached)
- Sign-off checklist (attached)
- Working notes (Drive): shared_drives/customer-success/escalation-playbook-refresh-working-notes

Requested sign-off by: Thu 2/5 EOD PT

What I need from each group:
1) SRE (Sean/Rafael)
   - Confirm paging thresholds + escalation triggers are consistent with on-call + SLO alerting
   - Confirm Slack bridge / #incidents handoff rules
   - Confirm we are not creating “backdoor paging” paths

2) Platform (Priya)
   - Confirm ownership boundaries + primary/backup contacts for: SSO, Audit Logs, Quota/Rate Limits, Routing/Fallback, Private installer
   - Confirm after-hours escalation expectations for Private/Dedicated

3) CS leadership (Jasmine/Rachel)
   - Confirm SLA language aligns with tiering + contract expectations
   - Confirm update cadence wording (especially enterprise) and credits/escalation triggers

Sign-off checklist (what I’m looking for in replies):
- [ ] Severity rubric: P0–P4 mapping to Sev + examples make sense
- [ ] Tiered SLAs: first response + update cadence + escalation thresholds are correct
- [ ] Escalation matrix: who/when/how to page is correct + backup coverage
- [ ] Slack bridge convention: naming + required context + who owns customer updates
- [ ] Jira guidance: required fields + SLA timers + linking conventions are workable
- [ ] Exec escalation: rules + summary template are accurate

How to respond:
- Please reply-all with either “Approved” or “Blocker” + the specific section/page. If “Blocker,” include the proposed replacement wording.

Thanks — this will unblock the known-issues sprint workflows + reduce the ping-pong during incidents.

Dev Patel
Support Ops Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- Escalation_Playbook_Refresh_v0.9.pdf
- Signoff_Checklist_Escalation_Playbook_v1.xlsx


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Reviewed. Mostly good — one blocker + a couple nits.

BLOCKER:
- SLA table currently implies we page SRE for “P1 enterprise” by default even if impact is isolated to a single tenant with an available workaround.
  - Proposed wording change: make paging trigger “customer-impacting outage/degradation meeting Sev1+ thresholds” rather than “customer tier.” Tier should influence update cadence + comms, not paging.
  - Specifically: in Escalation Triggers, replace “Enterprise P1 => page SRE” with “Page SRE when: (a) elevated error rate/latency affecting multiple customers or a region, OR (b) one customer but no workaround and business-critical workflow blocked.”

Nits:
- Please add “request IDs + region + model + approximate start time + recent deploy (if known)” as required fields in the page template. That’s the minimum for on-call.
- Bridge naming: can we standardize on “sup-<ticket>-<region>-<shortdesc>” so it’s searchable and tied to Jira?

If you incorporate the blocker wording, I’m good to approve.

Sean
SRE


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 1:04 PM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Thanks Dev.

Platform ownership looks correct with two changes:
- Quota/Rate Limits: primary = Control Plane on-call (Platform), backup = SRE if it’s correlated with broader degradation. Please avoid directing Support to ping individual engineers.
- Audit Logs: primary = Platform (data pipeline), but escalation path should include Security/Compliance only if customer is requesting formal attestation / evidence (otherwise it’s a Platform+SRE operational issue).

Other notes:
- Private installer issues: if a customer is mid-upgrade and stuck, we should trigger a Platform page once “rollback not possible” is confirmed (your checklist mentions this; just make it a bold callout).

Not a blocker. If you incorporate the ownership text above, I’m approved.

Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Reviewed from CS + enterprise SLA perspective.

Edits requested (not blockers):
- “Every 30 minutes” updates for enterprise P0: please clarify that cadence is during active mitigation window; if we’re “Waiting on Customer” we should explicitly state we will resume updates when new info is available, but still keep a time-based check-in (e.g., every 2 hours) to avoid silence.
- Credits guidance: please add “credits decisions require CS leadership approval” (Jasmine or me) and should not be promised in the initial acknowledgement.

Otherwise approved.

Rachel Kim
Enterprise Customer Success


---

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:44 AM PST
Subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5

Approved with Rachel’s wording change on update cadence + credits.

One additional request: in the comms templates, add a single sentence that sets expectations on what we can share (“we’ll share mitigation steps and status; we may not be able to share full root cause immediately”).

Thanks for pushing this through.

Jasmine Liu
VP Customer Success & Support


---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>

Next steps:

source.subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
Attachments: 2026-01-15-controlled-failover-metrics-snapshot.pdf, gameday-action-items-2026-01-16.csv

Team — sharing a concise leadership readout from Thursday’s controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west) and what we’re doing next.

TL;DR
- The core automation path worked end-to-end (signal → controller decision → routing policy flip → traffic shift → stabilization).
- Customer impact was limited but not zero: brief elevated 5xx/timeouts for a small slice of Hosted traffic + streaming disconnects for some clients that don’t handle redirects cleanly.
- We found several “must-fix before wider rollout” items (anti-flap tuning, dashboard annotations, streaming reconnect guidance, and capacity headroom guardrails).

What we tested
- Scenario: planned impairment simulation in us-east and controlled failover to eu-west for a scoped set of routes/models and a limited tenant allowlist.
- Objective: validate (1) detection + decisioning speed, (2) safe automation/rollback behavior, (3) observability + on-call usability, and (4) Dedicated protection (no unexpected pool drain).

Results against success criteria (high level)
- Detection + failover time:
  - First corroborated impairment signal observed: ~T+75s from impairment start.
  - Policy flip executed: ~T+2m40s.
  - Traffic settled in eu-west: ~T+4–5m (client retry/backoff dominated tail).
- Error/latency impact:
  - Hosted API: short spike in 5xx/timeouts during the shift window; elevated tail latency in eu-west for ~12–18 minutes as retries + warm-up converged.
  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:
  - No uncontrolled oscillation, but we did see an automated rollback trigger earlier than intended due to hold-down/threshold interaction (details below).

Customer impact / support signals
- One customer reported transient 5xx/timeouts during the planned window (SUP-25310).
- Streaming disconnects were reported by a couple customers whose clients did not gracefully reconnect on redirect (SUP-25322).
- Follow-on ticket on elevated tail latency in eu-west immediately post-shift (SUP-25341) — correlates with headroom + retry storm.

What went well
- On-call coordination and go/no-go discipline were strong (checklist completion and rollback readiness were clear).
- Region health aggregation + policy engine evaluation produced explainable decisions (we could point to which signals tripped).
- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps
- We are NOT expanding automation scope until the above items are closed and we pass a smaller-blast-radius rehearsal.
- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

Action items (short list; full tracker linked)
- Add automation-driven dashboard annotations + incident timeline hooks (Owner: Sean / Observability; target: end of Jan)
- Retune rollback/hold-down thresholds and add “stabilization grace” logic (Owner: Rafael + Routing; target: end of Jan)
- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

Thanks to everyone who helped make the test controlled and safe — we learned the right things without a large blast radius.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

This is the right framing.

Two adds:
- Let’s treat “dashboard annotations” as a hard gate for the next test. We lost time during the live window correlating graphs to events.
- Support comms: I’d prefer we keep proactive notifications limited to the same cohort (largest Hosted + Dedicated accounts + anyone with data residency questions) until streaming reconnect guidance is fully out.

I’ll sync with Rafael on gating criteria and get a proposed Feb window on the calendar once ENG-2426 is green.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Thanks, Ava.

A couple questions before I approve another production test window:
1) Were there any indications we violated (or came close to violating) the multi-region availability SLO budget during the test window?
2) What is our plan to ensure the next test doesn’t create a retry amplification event in the target region? (I want this explicitly called out in go/no-go.)

If we can answer those clearly and keep blast radius limited, I’m supportive of early Feb.

— Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Answers inline.

1) SLO/error budget proximity
- We did not materially burn the multi-region availability SLO budget for the month.
- The spike was brief and scoped; the rolling burn-rate alerts triggered as expected, but did not cross paging thresholds for sustained windows.
- We’ll include the exact numbers in the postmortem once the annotation gap is fixed (so we can line up the windows precisely), but this was within the planned “acceptable test impact” bounds.

2) Preventing retry amplification in the target region (explicit go/no-go)
We’re adding three concrete gates before we run Game Day #2:
- Capacity/headroom gate: require minimum eu-west headroom for the exact route/model allowlist (not just aggregate regional headroom). Irene’s team is helping define thresholds.
- Gateway/backoff gate: validate retry budgets and Retry-After behavior in staging under redirect (Priya’s team + Peter’s circuit breaker changes), with a target “max retry amplification” metric.
- Synthetic load rehearsal: replay a shaped load pattern with perf canaries enabled and confirm p99 stabilization time is within target before touching prod.

If those three are green, we’ll propose an early Feb date with an even smaller allowlist than 1/15.

I’ll also make sure the go/no-go doc includes an explicit rollback trigger tied to retry amplification (not just error burn).

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference
