Results against success criteria (high level)
- Detection + failover time:
  - First corroborated impairment signal observed: ~T+75s from impairment start.
  - Policy flip executed: ~T+2m40s.
  - Traffic settled in eu-west: ~T+4–5m (client retry/backoff dominated tail).
- Error/latency impact:
  - Hosted API: short spike in 5xx/timeouts during the shift window; elevated tail latency in eu-west for ~12–18 minutes as retries + warm-up converged.
  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:
  - No uncontrolled oscillation, but we did see an automated rollback trigger earlier than intended due to hold-down/threshold interaction (details below).

Customer impact / support signals
- One customer reported transient 5xx/timeouts during the planned window (SUP-25310).
- Streaming disconnects were reported by a couple customers whose clients did not gracefully reconnect on redirect (SUP-25322).
- Follow-on ticket on elevated tail latency in eu-west immediately post-shift (SUP-25341) — correlates with headroom + retry storm.

What went well
- On-call coordination and go/no-go discipline were strong (checklist completion and rollback readiness were clear).
- Region health aggregation + policy engine evaluation produced explainable decisions (we could point to which signals tripped).
- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps
- We are NOT expanding automation scope until the above items are closed and we pass a smaller-blast-radius rehearsal.
- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

Action items (short list; full tracker linked)
- Add automation-driven dashboard annotations + incident timeline hooks (Owner: Sean / Observability; target: end of Jan)
- Retune rollback/hold-down thresholds and add “stabilization grace” logic (Owner: Rafael + Routing; target: end of Jan)
- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

Thanks to everyone who helped make the test controlled and safe — we learned the right things without a large blast radius.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference

This is the right framing.

Two adds:
- Let’s treat “dashboard annotations” as a hard gate for the next test. We lost time during the live window correlating graphs to events.
- Support comms: I’d prefer we keep proactive notifications limited to the same cohort (largest Hosted + Dedicated accounts + anyone with data residency questions) until streaming reconnect guidance is fully out.

Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Thanks, Ava.

A couple questions before I approve another production test window:
1) Were there any indications we violated (or came close to violating) the multi-region availability SLO budget during the test window?
2) What is our plan to ensure the next test doesn’t create a retry amplification event in the target region? (I want this explicitly called out in go/no-go.)

If we can answer those clearly and keep blast radius limited, I’m supportive of early Feb.

— Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Answers inline.

1) SLO/error budget proximity
- We did not materially burn the multi-region availability SLO budget for the month.
- The spike was brief and scoped; the rolling burn-rate alerts triggered as expected, but did not cross paging thresholds for sustained windows.
- We’ll include the exact numbers in the postmortem once the annotation gap is fixed (so we can line up the windows precisely), but this was within the planned “acceptable test impact” bounds.

2) Preventing retry amplification in the target region (explicit go/no-go)
We’re adding three concrete gates before we run Game Day #2:
- Capacity/headroom gate: require minimum eu-west headroom for the exact route/model allowlist (not just aggregate regional headroom). Irene’s team is helping define thresholds.
- Gateway/backoff gate: validate retry budgets and Retry-After behavior in staging under redirect (Priya’s team + Peter’s circuit breaker changes), with a target “max retry amplification” metric.
- Synthetic load rehearsal: replay a shaped load pattern with perf canaries enabled and confirm p99 stabilization time is within target before touching prod.

If those three are green, we’ll propose an early Feb date with an even smaller allowlist than 1/15.

I’ll also make sure the go/no-go doc includes an explicit rollback trigger tied to retry amplification (not just error burn).

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference

Team — sharing a concise leadership readout from Thursday’s controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west) and what we’re doing next.

TL;DR
- The core automation path worked end-to-end (signal → controller decision → routing policy flip → traffic shift → stabilization).
- Customer impact was limited but not zero: brief elevated 5xx/timeouts for a small slice of Hosted traffic + streaming disconnects for some clients that don’t handle redirects cleanly.
- We found several “must-fix before wider rollout” items (anti-flap tuning, dashboard annotations, streaming reconnect guidance, and capacity headroom guardrails).

What we tested
- Scenario: planned impairment simulation in us-east and controlled failover to eu-west for a scoped set of routes/models and a limited tenant allowlist.

- Error/latency impact:

  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:

- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps

- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@novalabs.com>
Date: 2026-01-12T15:04:00-08:00
Subject: Novalabs MAP & POC onramp — API keys, org setup, allowlist, peering

Hi Emily,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — appreciated the product questions from your infra and security folks. Per our conversation, I wanted to kick off a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) for the POC onramp so we have clear owners, dates, and the minimal items required for staging. Goal: have a sandbox-ready environment and API keys by the end of the week so your team can run the first eval load next week.

Proposed MAP items (high level):
1) Org & IAM seed
   - Redwood creates Novalabs org container (read-only view for your security) + initial service accounts.
   - Owner (Redwood): Ben Carter. Owner (Novalabs): Emily Zhou.
2) API keys & scopes
   - Short-lived API keys for evaluation, limited to text-generation and embeddings scopes.
   - Deliverable: keys, expiration, and sample cURL.
3) Network allowlist
   - IP ranges for our hosted endpoints; optional VPC peering for throughput-sensitive paths.
   - Owner (Novalabs): provide firewall contact and CIDR ranges.
4) VPC peering (optional but recommended for heavy token throughput)
   - Exchange contact details, ASN, and preferred peering window.

Can you confirm:
- Security contact (name + email) who will own allowlist/VPC peering?
- Whether short-lived keys are OK for the initial eval?
- Any blackout dates next week we should avoid for peering windows?

I attached a lightweight MAP draft (novalabs-map-v2.pdf) — it includes a recommended 5-day close timeline and sample ticket owners. If that looks good I’ll loop in Neha (security ops) and Priya (solutions engineering) to finalize the steps.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com
Attachment: novalabs-map-v2.pdf (MAP draft)
From: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@novalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-01-12T18:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Novalabs MAP & POC onramp — API keys, org setup, allowlist, peering

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 3:04 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the demo yesterday — appreciated the product questions from your infra and security folks.
> Per our conversation, I wanted to kick off a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) for the POC onramp so we have clear owners, dates, and the minimal items required for staging.

Hi Ben — thanks for the clear summary and the draft. This aligns with our internal goals. Quick answers from my side:
- Security contact: Jordan Patel (jordan.patel@novalabs.com) — will own allowlist and coordinate with firewall team.
- Short-lived keys: acceptable for eval, but we need read-only logging to be enabled on your side for the first 72 hours (audit record).
- Blackout windows: none next week; we can do peering maintenance any day except Wednesday morning (Jan 14, 09:00–12:00 PST).

I attached the team's public IP blocks we plan to use for the first phase (see inline network list). Also: for VPC peering, I will introduce our cloud network engineer in the next reply.

Thanks —
Emily
Emily Zhou
Director, Platform Engineering — Novalabs
Email: emily.zhou@novalabs.com
Attachment: novalabs-ipblocks.txt (listed inline below)

---
Novalabs phase-1 egress (sample):
- 34.201.12.0/24
- 34.201.13.0/24
- 34.201.14.0/24
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@novalabs.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-01-13T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Novalabs MAP & POC onramp — API keys, org setup, allowlist, peering

Emily — great, thanks. Looping in Neha (security ops) and Priya (solutions) to pick up the remaining items. Neha — can you confirm the logging controls we can enable for short-lived keys and where Novalabs will find audit exports? Priya — I’ve attached a MAP compact with proposed owners/dates and minimal rollout steps; please review and flag any gaps.

Proposed short timeline (editable):
- Day 0 (today): Confirm contacts and IP blocks (Emily/Jordan) — done.
- Day 1: Redwood creates Novalabs org container + seed service accounts (Ben).
- Day 2: Issue eval API keys (scoped + expiring 7 days) and post sample requests (Priya).
- Day 3: Novalabs applies allowlist; smoke test generation (Emily/Jordan).
- Day 4: Optional VPC peering window (network teams schedule — prefer Jan 15 evening PST).
- Day 5: First load-run and notes; capture cost/latency baseline.

Deliverables attached: novalabs-map-v2.pdf (MAP compact) — includes ticket template and rollback steps for the peering window.

Neha — short checklist for you:
- Confirm that audit exports (S3 path) will include token usage and request ids for cross-reference.
- Confirm whether we need additional redaction for request bodies before export.

Thanks all — I’ll wait for Jordan’s peering contact and Neha’s logging note before issuing keys.

—
Ben
Senior Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference
Attachment: novalabs-map-v2.pdf (MAP compact)
From: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@novalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-01-15T09:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Novalabs MAP & POC onramp — API keys, org setup, allowlist, peering

Thanks team — quick update after syncing with our network engineer (CC: Marco Li). Confirmations:
- Peering contact: Marco Li (marco.li@novalabs.com). He can be reached for ASN/CIDR exchange and is available Jan 15 after 17:00 PST.
- Our confirmed egress blocks (for firewall allowlist): 34.201.12.0/24, 34.201.13.0/24, 34.201.14.0/24.
- Logging: Jordan confirms we can accept audit exports to an S3 staging account; he will provide bucket + IAM role next.

We’re happy to proceed on the proposed timeline. Please go ahead and create the org container and seed the service accounts; once Neha confirms audit exports we’ll apply the allowlist and schedule the peering window this evening.

Thanks —
Emily
Director, Platform Engineering — Novalabs
CC: Marco Li <marco.li@novalabs.com>, Jordan Patel <jordan.patel@novalabs.com>
-- end of thread --

Ben

source.subject: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@clearglass.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Nov 2025 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Hi Samir / Tanya,

Thanks again for the call earlier — quick recap and proposed next steps so the team has a single reference. We focused on the streaming use case for invoice extraction (avg sequence ~1.2k tokens) and the customer-facing chat assistant. Key SLO targets you asked us to qualify against:

- P95 latency: 250ms per response for single-turn chat (max 2k tokens)
- Tail latency requirement for streaming (first token <= 120ms, steady-state <= 40ms per chunk)
- Sustained throughput: 150 reqs/sec, with burst to 400 reqs/sec for 2-3 min windows
- Concurrency: 500 concurrent users (avg 1 request every 10s)

From our side, primary levers we'll evaluate to meet these targets: continuous batching, KV/prefix caching (hot prompts), model variants (smaller decoder for chat vs higher-accuracy generator for extraction), and FP16/8-bit quantization profiles.

Proposed immediate next steps (action items):
1) ClearGlass: share representative request trace samples + a small prompt set (5–10 examples) and a peak-hour traffic CSV — Tanya to send by EOD Friday.
2) Redwood: run a two-tier qualification: a) latency-focused baseline (no batching, real-time path) b) cost-optimized path (continuous batching + caching + int8 quant) and report delta in p95/p99 and tokens/sec. Deliverable: short matrix with recommended model variant + expected cost uplift/reduction. Target delivery: Tue 11/11.
3) Schedule a focused ops session to validate streaming chunking (server-side vs client-driven). Let's pencil Nov 12 10am PT — Jordan to send invite.

I've attached a draft SLO matrix and a short note on the perf options we're planning to test.

If any of the targets above are off, flag them and we'll adjust.

Thanks,

Jordan Blake
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: redwood_perf_options.pdf (summary of test vectors and config options)
Attachment: clearglass_slo_matrix.xlsx (template)
From: Samir Patel <samir@clearglass.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Tanya Lee <tanya.ops@clearglass.io>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 7 Nov 2025 09:17:00 -0800
Subject: Re: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Jordan — thanks for the clear recap. A couple quick clarifications before Tanya uploads traces:

- P95 250ms is an aspirational target for chat. For invoice extraction the team can accept up to 400ms p95 as long as throughput holds. Can you call that out in the matrix?
- For streaming, we do need the first-token latency tight (<=120ms) otherwise the UX feels sluggish.

Tanya will drop a ZIP with raw rpc traces and a small synthetic load profile by EOD Friday (she's on PTO Mon).

One ask from procurement: if Redwood recommends an int8 quant profile, can you sketch the accuracy delta we should expect versus FP16 for our extractor model? We need something we can show to our ML team.

Appreciate the fast turnaround.

Samir
Dir., Platform Engineering
ClearGlass

On Thu, Nov 6, 2025 at 2:03 PM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@clearglass.io>
Cc: Tanya Lee <tanya.ops@clearglass.io>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 9 Nov 2025 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Samir — great questions, and thanks for flagging the invoice-extraction tolerance. Short answers and what we'll include in the deliverable: 

- We'll split the deliverable matrix into two rows per workload: chat (250ms p95 target) and extraction (400ms p95 target).
- For int8 quant: our prior Bench (similar encoder-decoder extraction models) shows ~0.5–1.5% drop in exact-match / extraction F1 when moving FP16 -> int8 with Redwood's calibrated quant config; that gap narrows when we use per-channel calibration and a small finetune pass. We'll run a quick accuracy sweep (3 prompts, 50 samples) and include numbers in the Tue deliverable.
- Streaming first-token: we'll run two server-side chunking strategies (initial chunk size tradeoffs) and a client-driven fallback. Expect a p50/p95 table for first-token latency across both strategies.

Action updates:
- Tanya uploaded traces to the Drive folder you shared — thanks! (link below)
- Redwood will deliver the performance matrix and a short recommendation doc by Tue 11/11 COB PT. That will include:
  * latency p50/p95/p99 for both no-batch and continuous-batch paths
  * throughput (reqs/sec) and tokens/sec estimates at target concurrency levels
  * cost delta (% change in $/1000 tokens) for FP16 vs int8 vs mixed-precision + model-variant suggestion
  * fallback policies (model thinning and regional routing) to keep a soft SLO under load

One quick scheduling note: our ops experiment will need access to a small reserved pool for the load test (1–2 GPU nodes). Ben — can you confirm availability Nov 12–13?

Thanks,

Jordan

Related links:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1abcDEF (Tanya traces)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@clearglass.io>, Tanya Lee <tanya.ops@clearglass.io>
Date: Mon, 10 Nov 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Looping in procurement/context from our side and next commercial steps. Samir — thanks for the clarity on tolerance windows. A couple notes from Redwood commercial/op perspective: 

- For a sustained 150 reqs/sec and a burst to 400, one practical path is start with a small Dedicated pilot (1 month reserved capacity) to guarantee headroom during qualification. That removes noisy-neighbor risk for your peak tests.
- Pricing snapshot: for the pilot configuration we'll test (mixed FP16 + int8 fallback), estimated incremental cost is ~18–22% vs baseline FP16-only serving at your current token volumes. We'll provide a finer breakdown after the perf run.
- If ClearGlass signs for a quarterly pilot, we can include a one-time onboarding credit (up to $5k) tied to the joint qualification success criteria (p95 targets met and no functional regressions).

Next steps from commercial: I'll prepare a short one-page pilot term sheet once Jordan posts the perf matrix. Jordan — once we have numbers, can you and Ben confirm the node sizing so I can draft a pricing row?

Priya Natarajan
Senior Sales Ops
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: clearglass_slo_matrix.xlsx (updated with invoice/extraction tolerance row)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@clearglass.io>, Tanya Lee <tanya.ops@clearglass.io>
Date: Mon, 10 Nov 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Looping in procurement/context from our side and next commercial steps. Samir — thanks for the clarity on tolerance windows. A couple notes from Redwood commercial/op perspective: 

- For a sustained 150 reqs/sec and a burst to 400, one practical path is start with a small Dedicated pilot (1 month reserved capacity) to guarantee headroom during qualification. That removes noisy-neighbor risk for your peak tests.
- Pricing snapshot: for the pilot configuration we'll test (mixed FP16 + int8 fallback), estimated incremental cost is ~18–22% vs baseline FP16-only serving at your current token volumes. We'll provide a finer breakdown after the perf run.
- If ClearGlass signs for a quarterly pilot, we can include a one-time onboarding credit (up to $5k) tied to the joint qualification success criteria (p95 targets met and no functional regressions).

Next steps from commercial: I'll prepare a short one-page pilot term sheet once Jordan posts the perf matrix. Jordan — once we have numbers, can you and Ben confirm the node sizing so I can draft a pricing row?

Priya Natarajan
Senior Sales Ops
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: clearglass_slo_matrix.xlsx (updated with invoice/extraction tolerance row)

source.subject: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility
related_links: Linear: ENG-4821, ENG-4822, ENG-4823, ENG-4824, ENG-4827, ENG-4829
source.body:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Priya / Neha,

We’re kicking off the Provision-to-Ready automation sprint for Dedicated capacity + shared fleet bring-up. I want to align on (a) what “success” means in exec terms, and (b) a lightweight weekly reporting cadence so we don’t have to reconstruct this ad hoc during rollout.

Why now (exec framing)
- Dedicated expansions have been gated by manual steps and inconsistent node readiness. Time-to-ready is a top contributor to “capacity available vs capacity allocated” deltas.
- This sprint is intended to make bring-up repeatable across regions/GPU types via: golden images + automated orchestration + validated readiness gates.

Proposal: success metrics (what I’ll report weekly)
1) Time-to-ready (TTReady) for new nodes
   - P50 and P95 (overall + segmented by region + GPU type)
   - Definition: allocated -> Ready (with clear source-of-truth timestamps)
2) Provision-to-ready success rate
   - % nodes reaching Ready without human intervention within 24h
3) Validation gate health
   - Failure rate by step (GPU health/driver parity/kube join/network/storage/NCCL smoke/perf canary)
   - Quarantine count + top quarantine reasons
4) Rollout coverage
   - Regions/GPU types enabled behind flags, plus current constraints

Cadence
- Weekly update email every Friday EOD PT (one screen of bullets + a link to dashboard)
- Mid-week ping only if we hit stop conditions or need approval for staged rollout expansion

Ask
- Please confirm (or tweak) the metrics above and who should be on the weekly CC for exec visibility.
- If you have a strong preference on target thresholds (e.g., TTReady P95), call it out; otherwise we’ll report deltas from baseline and lock targets in week 1 once measurement is fully consistent.

Attaching a draft one-pager + baseline snapshot.

Attachments:
- Provision-to-Ready Sprint One-Pager v1.pdf
- Provision-to-Ready Baseline Metrics (Sep 2025).xlsx

Thanks,
Irene
—
Irene Choi
Director, Engineering Program Management
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Aligned on the framing and the reporting approach.

Two additions I’d like to see captured explicitly in the weekly update:
- Control-plane “readiness state machine” adoption: % of new nodes flowing through Allocated -> Provisioning -> Validating -> Ready via the new service (vs legacy/manual). This is the leading indicator that the platform integration is real.
- Safety/rollback: any time we have to flip the kill switch or roll back an image channel, call it out (even if customer impact is avoided). We should treat those as learning moments.

On targets: I’d rather we set them after Week 1 once the timestamps are defensible. The only non-negotiable is we don’t ship gates that create silent capacity loss (i.e., Ready is accurate, Quarantine is actionable).

Priya
—
Priya Natarajan
VP, Platform Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

+1 to Priya.

A couple of exec-level clarifications I’ll want baked into the weekly note:
- What does “Ready” mean operationally? (e.g., eligible to join serving pool vs only joined cluster). If we change that definition mid-sprint, flag it.
- Customer impact indicator: is Dedicated expansion currently blocked, at risk, or on-track? (simple R/Y/G is fine)

Target-wise: directionally, we should be able to say “we reduced time-to-ready by X%” and “we reduced manual touches by Y%” by end of sprint. Don’t over-fit to absolute numbers in week one.

Re: CC list, keep it tight. I’d suggest just me + Priya + Sean; add others only when we’re asking for a go/no-go.

Neha


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 11:16 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Got it. I’ll incorporate the additions and keep the CC tight (Priya/Neha/Sean + me; Vanessa only if there’s an approval ask or if we’re calling out a major operational decision).

Re: “Ready” definition — proposal for the weekly note (and we’ll pin this in the sprint page):
- Ready = node has:
  1) joined the cluster
  2) passed post-flight validations (GPU health + driver/toolkit parity + basic network/storage checks + NCCL smoke)
  3) passed a lightweight runtime/perf canary
  4) been marked Ready in the control-plane readiness service
  5) is eligible to be added to serving pools (subject to capacity policy)

If/when we decouple “Ready” from “eligible to serve,” I’ll call it out explicitly.

Weekly email template (one screen)
- Summary: R/Y/G for Dedicated expansion readiness
- TTReady: P50/P95 + delta vs baseline
- Manual touches: touches/node + top causes
- State machine adoption: % via new path
- Top 3 failure modes: step + failure rate + what we’re doing
- Safety events: kill switch / rollback / quarantine spike
- Next week: scope + decision points (if any)

I’ll also include links to:
- Sprint Confluence page
- Grafana dashboard
- Key Linear epics/tickets

If you’re OK with this, I’ll send the first weekly on Fri 10/3 with baseline + Week 1 plan, then start reporting actual metrics once we have the canonical timestamps finalized.

Irene


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 12:08 PM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

SRE POV: the template is good.

One suggestion: when you report TTReady, please include a “data quality” line for the first two weeks (e.g., whether allocated timestamp comes from cloud API vs internal allocator). We’ve had past confusion where the metric moved because the definition changed.

Also, for alerting/noise management during rollout, it would be helpful to track:
- flake rate on validations (NCCL + perf canary)
- quarantine escape hatch usage (operator overrides)

No further asks. Keep the CC list tight as discussed.

Sean
—
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 1:38 PM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Makes sense — I’ll add a “Metric definition / data quality” line item until we’re confident the allocated->ready timestamps are stable.

Plan:
- I’ll send the first update tomorrow (Fri 10/3) using the template + baseline + Week 1 measurement decisions.
- Subsequent weekly updates on Fridays EOD PT.
- Any kill switch / rollback / capacity-at-risk event will trigger a same-day heads up email (separately from the weekly).

Thanks all.

Irene

source.subject: Draft MAP + evaluation checklist and risk diligence
messages:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Alex Torres <atorres@brightair.ai>, Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@brightair.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Sep 2025 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: Draft MAP + evaluation checklist (BrightAir)

Hi Alex / Sophie,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — really enjoyed the conversation and the use cases you outlined for inference at scale. source.subject: Change request: mid-cycle capacity reduction & proration
messages:
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudMetrics Billing <finance@cloudmetrics.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Ben / Marissa,

We need to make a mid-month change to our Dedicated plan. Effective 2025-10-01 we want to reduce reserved capacity from 8 GPUs to 6 GPUs (we're pausing two machines as part of a workload consolidation). Can you confirm how Redwood handles proration for mid-cycle capacity reductions and whether the credit will show as a separate line on the next invoice?

Also: CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN 12-3456789). Please confirm whether any VAT / tax adjustments apply for this credit and whether you need a new PO or revised PO number from our end. I'm attaching our original PO and the current invoice for reference.

Requested by our finance team — month-end close is 10/05, so if possible we need the credit to appear on the October invoice.

Attachments: purchase_order_CM-789.pdf (PDF, 45KB), cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

Thanks,
Olivia Chen
Head of Cloud Engineering
CloudMetrics, Inc.
olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com
+1 (415) 555-0123
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 11:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the details and the PO. Looping in Priya (billing) and Rishi (finance) to confirm exact credit timing and tax handling. Quick notes from my side:

- We can do a mid-cycle proration for the 2 GPUs you are removing. Redwood prorates on a per-GPU-day basis, then applies the credit to your next invoice.
- If you need the credit to post on the October invoice (closing 10/05), please confirm the effective date as 2025-10-01 and provide the PO number you want referenced on the credit memo (I see PO CM-789 attached).
- As a ballpark: current monthly cost for 8 GPUs = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs (25% reduction) effective 10/01 yields ~25% of a one-month value prorated for the unused days in October. I ran a quick calc and attached a spreadsheet — estimated credit: $2,580 (rounded). See proration_calc_0925.xlsx.

Priya/Rishi: please confirm exact rounding rules, whether we post as a credit memo or negative invoice line, and VAT/tax steps. Olivia: if you want the credit earlier than the next invoice, we can issue a one-off credit memo but that will still appear as a credit on our accounting and should be visible via your billing page link.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Sep 2025 08:15:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the heads up and the PO. A few clarifications and what we'll do next: 

1) Policy / approach:
   - Mid-cycle reductions are prorated on a per-GPU-day basis and result in a credit on the next posted invoice (we do not reissue the original invoice).
   - Credits appear as a separate credit memo line ("Prorated capacity adjustment") and will show on the next invoice due to you.

2) Timing:
   - You confirmed effective date 2025-10-01: that works. The proration window is 2025-10-01 through 2025-10-31 (30 days) but we compute unused GPU-days from that effective date relative to your billing period (2025-10-01 to 2025-10-31). The credit will post on the invoice generated 2025-10-25 (your monthly invoice for Oct). It will be reflected in the payment due on 2025-11-05.

3) Amounts / rounding:
   - We calculate at the line-item level. Example (based on posted rate): 8 GPUs @ $4,000/GPU-month = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs = $8,000/month; for 31 days in Oct the unused portion is 31 days * (2 GPUs * daily rate). Attached is the detailed calc (proration_calc_0925.xlsx). Final credit will be rounded to nearest dollar.

4) Tax/VAT handling:
   - Since CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN provided), we will not apply EU VAT. Redwood will apply US sales tax only where required by jurisdiction; per our records CloudMetrics billing address is CA, so sales tax may apply. If you prefer a zero-rated tax treatment, please send a written resale/exemption certificate.

5) Required info from CloudMetrics to finalize:
   - Confirm effective date (REPLY: yes/no)
   - Confirm PO number to reference on the credit memo (we will use CM-789 if that is OK)
   - If you need the credit earlier than the standard next invoice, confirm and we can request expedited processing (subject to internal approvals).

I attached a draft "adjusted invoice" stub showing the credit line that will be inserted (cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf).

Regards,
Priya Natarajan
Billing & Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

title: Change request: mid-cycle capacity reduction & proration
preview:
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudMetrics Billing <finance@cloudmetrics.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Ben / Marissa,

We need to make a mid-month change to our Dedicated plan. Effective 2025-10-01 we want to reduce reserved capacity from 8 GPUs to 6 GPUs (we're pausing two machines as part of a workload consolidation). Can you confirm how Redwood handles proration for mid-cycle capacity reductions and whether the credit will show as a separate line on the next invoice?

Also: CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN 12-3456789). Please confirm whether any VAT / tax adjustments apply for this credit and whether you need a new PO or revised PO number from our end. I'm attaching our original PO and the current invoice for reference.

Requested by our finance team — month-end close is 10/05, so if possible we need the credit to appear on the October invoice.

Attachments: purchase_order_CM-789.pdf (PDF, 45KB), cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

Thanks,
Olivia Chen
Head of Cloud Engineering
CloudMetrics, Inc.
olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com
+1 (415) 555-0123


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 11:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the details and the PO. Looping in Priya (billing) and Rishi (finance) to confirm exact credit timing and tax handling. Quick notes from my side:

- We can do a mid-cycle proration for the 2 GPUs you are removing. Redwood prorates on a per-GPU-day basis, then applies the credit to your next invoice.
- If you need the credit to post on the October invoice (closing 10/05), please confirm the effective date as 2025-10-01 and provide the PO number you want referenced on the credit memo (I see PO CM-789 attached).
- As a ballpark: current monthly cost for 8 GPUs = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs (25% reduction) effective 10/01 yields ~25% of a one-month value prorated for the unused days in October. I ran a quick calc and attached a spreadsheet — estimated credit: $2,580 (rounded). See proration_calc_0925.xlsx.

Priya/Rishi: please confirm exact rounding rules, whether we post as a credit memo or negative invoice line, and VAT/tax steps. Olivia: if you want the credit earlier than the next invoice, we can issue a one-off credit memo but that will still appear as a credit on our accounting and should be visible via your billing page link.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Sep 2025 08:15:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the heads up and the PO. A few clarifications and what we'll do next: 

1) Policy / approach:
   - Mid-cycle reductions are prorated on a per-GPU-day basis and result in a credit on the next posted invoice (we do not reissue the original invoice).
   - Credits appear as a separate credit memo line ("Prorated capacity adjustment") and will show on the next invoice due to you.

2) Timing:
   - You confirmed effective date 2025-10-01: that works. The proration window is 2025-10-01 through 2025-10-31 (30 days) but we compute unused GPU-days from that effective date relative to your billing period (2025-10-01 to 2025-10-31). The credit will post on the invoice generated 2025-10-25 (your monthly invoice for Oct). It will be reflected in the payment due on 2025-11-05.

3) Amounts / rounding:
   - We calculate at the line-item level. Example (based on posted rate): 8 GPUs @ $4,000/GPU-month = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs = $8,000/month; for 31 days in Oct the unused portion is 31 days * (2 GPUs * daily rate). Attached is the detailed calc (proration_calc_0925.xlsx). Final credit will be rounded to nearest dollar.

4) Tax/VAT handling:
   - Since CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN provided), we will not apply EU VAT. Redwood will apply US sales tax only where required by jurisdiction; per our records CloudMetrics billing address is CA, so sales tax may apply. If you prefer a zero-rated tax treatment, please send a written resale/exemption certificate.

5) Required info from CloudMetrics to finalize:
   - Confirm effective date (REPLY: yes/no)
   - Confirm PO number to reference on the credit memo (we will use CM-789 if that is OK)
   - If you need the credit earlier than the standard next invoice, confirm and we can request expedited processing (subject to internal approvals).

I attached a draft "adjusted invoice" stub showing the credit line that will be inserted (cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf).

Regards,
Priya Natarajan
Billing & Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

source.subject: Renewal request: SOC 2 Type II artifacts for procurement gating and audit-period clarification
messages:
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@greenlinehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SOC 2 Type II for renewal / expansion PO gating

Hi Chloe,

We're preparing the renewal + expansion PO (Greenline Health / Redwood expansion) and procurement needs the most recent SOC 2 Type II report, plus confirmation on whether a bridge letter will be required. Our renewal is scheduled for 2025-10-01 and finance is asking for clear coverage dates to avoid a hold on the PO.

Could you confirm: 
- latest Type II report date and the audit period covered (start/end),
- whether Redwood provides a bridge/interim attestation if the report coverage doesn’t include our renewal date, and
- an estimate for when we can get artifacts (PDF + attestation summary) so our legal/procurement team can attach them to the PO?

We appreciate a quick turnaround—procurement has a close of business target next Thursday.

Thanks,
Alicia Gomez
Sr. Procurement Manager
Greenline Health
[Attachment: greenline_po_template.xlsx]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@greenlinehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II for renewal / expansion PO gating

Hi Alicia — thanks for the heads up. Looping in Priya from InfoSec for the authoritative coverage details and our standard bridge letter process; Ben (AM) is on copy to track the procurement milestone on the deal.

Priya — can you confirm the exact audit period for the latest Type II and whether we should produce a bridge letter for Greenline’s 2025-10-01 renewal window? If yes, I’ll flag this to Legal for signature and send the artifacts to Alicia.

Goal: get definitive answer and attachments to Greenline no later than Thu 9/18 so procurement can proceed.

-Chloe
Chloe Martin
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: PO_received_from_Greenline_20250915.pdf]

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@greenlinehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II for renewal / expansion PO gating

Hi all — quick update from InfoSec: 

1) Latest report: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024.pdf (Type II, period covered: 2024-08-01 through 2025-07-31). I have attached a one-page attestation summary (Redwood_Attestation_Summary_2024.xlsx) that procurement teams often accept alongside the full report.

2) Audit-period note: the current Type II window ends 2025-07-31. Greenline’s renewal on 2025-10-01 falls outside that window. For procurement gating we offer a standard bridge/interim attestation that confirms there have been no material changes in controls since the last report. The bridge is not a full audit but is commonly accepted by procurement and legal as temporary coverage until the next report is available.

3) Turnaround and attachments: 
- I can prepare the bridge-letter draft (SOC2_bridge_letter_template.pdf) and the attestation within 48 business hours.
- Legal will need to sign the bridge letter; once signed we can provide PDF and DocuSign-ready copy.

Next steps: Chloe/Ben, please confirm that Greenline will accept a bridge letter + attestation summary alongside the Type II PDF to unblock the PO. Alicia, if procurement needs a formal Rider/PO clause, let us know and we’ll involve Legal directly.

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Manager, Information Security | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024.pdf]

On Mon, Sep 15, 2025 at 10:05 AM Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Alicia — thanks for the heads up. Looping in Priya from InfoSec for the authoritative coverage details and our standard bridge letter process; Ben (AM) is on copy to track the procurement milestone on the deal.
>
> Priya — can you confirm the exact audit period for the latest Type II and whether we should produce a bridge letter for Greenline’s 2025-10-01 renewal window? If yes, I’ll flag this to Legal for signature and send the artifacts to Alicia.
>
> -Chloe

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@greenlinehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II for renewal / expansion PO gating

Chloe / Priya — thanks for the quick follow-up and the attachments. Greenline procurement is fine accepting the full Type II report plus an interim bridge letter confirming no material control changes; that should be sufficient to clear the PO by our internal deadline.

Please route the signed bridge letter to procurement@greenlinehealth.com and upload the attestation summary and PDF to our DocuSign envelope: https://docusign.com/Signing/Envelope12345. If Legal needs a specific procurement clause to be referenced, I’ll share it by EOD Friday.

Also, Ben — can you confirm the billing contact and PO reference we should expect so we can align timelines on invoicing once the PO is released?

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Alicia
Sr. Procurement Manager
Greenline Health

messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Hugo Martinez <hugo@solishealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Quick TrustRadius quote + donation option

Hi Hugo,

Thanks again for the time last week — the Solis Health pilot results were really encouraging. We have a small favor to ask: would you be open to posting a short review on TrustRadius? A 2–3 sentence quote about performance and reliability would be really helpful for our evaluation page.

Two quick details to make it easy:
- Link: https://www.trustradius.com/products/redwood-inference/reviews
- Incentives: we usually offer a $25 Amazon e-gift card, but if the team prefers we can instead make a $25 donation to a charity of your choice (we can issue a donation receipt).

If you’re comfortable, permission to use the quote on our website/case study would also be appreciated — I can send a one-click consent form. I’m cc’ing Ava from Customer Success for any technical details and Priya from Sales Ops to coordinate the reward.

No rush — if you’re up for it, a short note by the end of next week would be great.

Thanks again,

Jonas Weber
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132
Redwood Inference

Attachments: trustradius-quick-instructions.pdf (one-page guide with sample phrasing and screenshots)
From: Hugo Martinez <hugo@solishealth.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Solis Health Procurement <procurement@solishealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 09:05:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick TrustRadius quote + donation option

Jonas — thanks for the note and congrats to the team on the pilot results. Happy to leave a short review. Procurement asked that any vendor gift cards go through our procurement mailbox for tracking. If you can send the e-gift card to procurement@solishealth.com that works.

Also, the donation option sounds fine — if you want to route the donation instead, please indicate which charity options you support and we’ll confirm internally.

I’ll draft a quick 2–3 line quote and post it this week.

Best,
Hugo Martinez
VP of Platform — Solis Health

Charity options we support (examples):
- Doctors Without Borders
- Local Health Foundation (San Francisco)
- Girls Who Code

To streamline permissions for using the quote on our site, here’s a one-click consent (DocuSign): https://app.docusign.com/link/ABC123XYZ — it’s just to confirm usage rights, nothing legal-heavy. I’ve attached the quick instructions again for TrustRadius posting.

Thanks, and cc’ing Priya to confirm issuance once you tell us whether procurement or donation is preferred.

—
Jonas Weber
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: trustradius-quick-instructions.pdf

To: Hugo Martinez <hugo@solishealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>

Subject: Quick follow-up — TrustRadius post and reward routing

Hi Hugo — hope you’re well. Just a quick follow-up on the TrustRadius review — wanted to check if you had a chance to post the quote, and whether procurement wants the e-gift card or you prefer the donation option.

If you’d like me to drop the e-gift card to procurement@solishealth.com I can do that today; if donation, tell me which charity from the list (or another of your choice) and we’ll get a receipt over.

Thanks again for helping with this — really appreciate the time.

Best,
Jonas

source.subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness
messages:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement and billing ops: 

Summary from the call
- LedgerWave is excited about Redwood for the KYC/transaction enrichment pipeline.
- Procurement leans toward using a PO process for all 3rd-party SaaS purchases. They still evaluate marketplace options (AWS Marketplace) depending on discounts and SSO integration ease.

Open items for billing/procurement (we need confirmation from you):
1) Commercial path: PO (preferred) vs. card vs. marketplace — which will your procurement team route for this engagement?
2) Invoice requirements: billing address, VAT/tax ID, company legal name exactly as it should appear on invoices.
3) Net terms: do you require Net 30/45? Any internal approvals needed for Net terms?
4) Invoice format: PDF invoice (email), e-invoice/EDI, or custom fields required?
5) Remittance and purchase order workflow — do you need invoices to reference a PO number for payment to be processed?
6) Billing contact name + email for our Finance team to reach out.

Proposed next steps
- If you can attach your PO template and any billing-spec doc, I’ll loop in our Billing Ops (Karthik) to confirm we can produce invoices in the requested format.
- If procurement wants Marketplace, we can pull together a short comparison of expected differences (discounts, billing cadence, invoice fields).
- I’ll share a draft SOW/initial invoice example once we lock the path.

Would love your responses on the numbered items above — if easier, feel free to attach your PO template and billing requirements and I’ll coordinate internally.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: none in this message
From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, John Hayes <john.hayes@ledgerwave.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 17:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Priya —

Thanks for the clear recap. Answers inline below and I attached the PO template + billing checklist for your team.

1) Commercial path: Procurement prefers PO-based purchases for anything over $10k. For smaller pilots they will accept company card, but for rollout we expect PO. They are open to AWS Marketplace if we can get the same billing detail and our legal team signs the marketplace terms.

2) Invoice requirements:
- Legal name: LedgerWave, Inc.
- Billing address: 2308 Harbor Drive, Suite 400, Boston, MA 02110
- VAT/Tax ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we are US entity, no VAT).

3) Net terms: We prefer Net 30. For pilots they may pay on receipt if we provide PO + invoice with correct GL codes.

4) Invoice format: PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com is fine. They also accept a structured XLS with line-level GL codes (I attached a sample spreadsheet). No EDI required at this stage.

5) Yes — invoice must reference the PO number. Payments won't be scheduled without a PO on the invoice.

6) Billing ops contact: John Hayes, Billing Ops Manager — john.hayes@ledgerwave.com, +1 (617) 555-1010.

Attached: LedgerWave_PO_template.pdf, LedgerWave_billing_requirements.xlsx

Let me know if you need anything else to get the initial SOW/invoice example over to us. We are aiming to have procurement sign-off within 2–3 weeks.

Best,
Elena
Director, Strategic Partnerships | LedgerWave Inc.
elena@ledgerwave.com
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 09:38:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Folks — forwarding LedgerWave’s reply and the attached PO + billing checklist. Key asks for Billing Ops:
- Generate a sample invoice (PDF) that references a placeholder PO and uses LedgerWave’s legal name/address and GL line structure as per the XLS.
- Confirm Karthik whether we can produce an XLS invoice variant with line-level GL codes for AP.
- Confirm whether we can offer Net 30 in an initial pilot and what approvals are needed.

LedgerWave (cc’d procurement & John Hayes) wants PO on invoice and will accept PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com.

Can you please take point on producing the sample invoice and we’ll send an SOW draft tied to that invoice amount?

Thanks — Priya

> On Sep 15, 2025, at 17:02, Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com> wrote:
> [quoted original message and attachments]
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

title: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement and billing ops: 

Summary from the call
- LedgerWave is excited about Redwood for the KYC/transaction enrichment pipeline.
- Procurement leans toward using a PO process for all 3rd-party SaaS purchases. They still evaluate marketplace options (AWS Marketplace) depending on discounts and SSO integration ease.

Open items for billing/procurement (we need confirmation from you):
1) Commercial path: PO (preferred) vs. card vs. marketplace — which will your procurement team route for this engagement?
2) Invoice requirements: billing address, VAT/tax ID, company legal name exactly as it should appear on invoices.
3) Net terms: do you require Net 30/45? Any internal approvals needed for Net terms?
4) Invoice format: PDF invoice (email), e-invoice/EDI, or custom fields required?
5) Remittance and purchase order workflow — do you need invoices to reference a PO number for payment to be processed?
6) Billing contact name + email for our Finance team to reach out.

Proposed next steps
- If you can attach your PO template and any billing-spec doc, I’ll loop in our Billing Ops (Karthik) to confirm we can produce invoices in the requested format.
- If procurement wants Marketplace, we can pull together a short comparison of expected differences (discounts, billing cadence, invoice fields).
- I’ll share a draft SOW/initial invoice example once we lock the path.

Would love your responses on the numbered items above — if easier, feel free to attach your PO template and billing requirements and I’ll coordinate internally.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: none in this message


From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, John Hayes <john.hayes@ledgerwave.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 17:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Priya —

Thanks for the clear recap. Answers inline below and I attached the PO template + billing checklist for your team.

1) Commercial path: Procurement prefers PO-based purchases for anything over $10k. For smaller pilots they will accept company card, but for rollout we expect PO. They are open to AWS Marketplace if we can get the same billing detail and our legal team signs the marketplace terms.

2) Invoice requirements:
- Legal name: LedgerWave, Inc.
- Billing address: 2308 Harbor Drive, Suite 400, Boston, MA 02110
- VAT/Tax ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we are US entity, no VAT).

3) Net terms: We prefer Net 30. For pilots they may pay on receipt if we provide PO + invoice with correct GL codes.

4) Invoice format: PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com is fine. They also accept a structured XLS with line-level GL codes (I attached a sample spreadsheet). No EDI required at this stage.

5) Yes — invoice must reference the PO number. Payments won't be scheduled without a PO on the invoice.

6) Billing ops contact: John Hayes, Billing Ops Manager — john.hayes@ledgerwave.com, +1 (617) 555-1010.

Attached: LedgerWave_PO_template.pdf, LedgerWave_billing_requirements.xlsx

Let me know if you need anything else to get the initial SOW/invoice example over to us. We are aiming to have procurement sign-off within 2–3 weeks.

Best,
Elena
Director, Strategic Partnerships | LedgerWave Inc.
elena@ledgerwave.com

source.subject: Intern class 2026 — return-offers & new-grad headcount request
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Sep 2025 16:22:00 -0700
Subject: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Hi team,

Attached is the proposed grid for the 2026 intern class and the ask for two new grad (entry-level SWE) headcount slots tied to our most successful internship conversions. Summary:

- Returning interns recommended for offers: 7 total (4 SWE, 2 Infra, 1 PM). Names and recommendations are in the spreadsheet ("intern_offer_grid.xlsx").
- New-grad headcount request: 2 FTEs (SWE IC) to be added to Q1 2026 hiring plan if budget approved.
- Compensation: I'm proposing baseline intern offer bands that map to the comp_bands_2026.pdf. For SWE interns converted to new-grads, we'd place them at the lower half of the E1 band per prior practice, with expedited review if market forces require.

Asks:
1) Markus — can you review the comp bands and confirm we can use the mapping in comp_bands_2026.pdf for both interns and new-grad placements? If you recommend an uplift for remote EU candidates, please call that out.
2) Priya — please confirm budget availability for two additional headcount in Q1 and advise whether this should be backfilled from hiring reserves or require headcount reallocation. I attached the headcount request PDF stub for finance workflow.
3) Laura — would appreciate a quick verbal ok (or escalation) so we can move offers to recruiting and start counter windows by 9/22.

Notes: several of these interns were high-impact (shipping infra optimizations and RLHF evaluation tooling). If we lose more than two, we risk slowing a couple of roadmap items.

Attachments: intern_offer_grid.xlsx (workbook), comp_bands_2026.pdf (summary), headcount_request_form.pdf (finance form).

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Engineering Manager, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

---

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Evelyn — thanks for the write-up and the materials. Quick thoughts after a first pass:

- Comp bands: comp_bands_2026.pdf looks reasonable for US-based interns. For remote EU hires we should add a regional modifier (we've historically added a 10%-12% uplift for cost-of-living and local market parity). Recommend we flag those 2 interns who are EU-remote for a +10% adjustment to stipend/offer.
- SWE new-grad placement: placing converted interns in the lower half of E1 is fine, but I'd like a clause for early performance-based uplift at 6 months to avoid losing them in the offer season.
- Headcount ask: I support 2 FTEs if Priya confirms budget. We should include a delta against current headcount plan so People Ops doesn't double-allocate.

I've annotated intern_offer_grid.xlsx with suggested salary rows (see comments column). If we need to move faster, I can sign-off on the comp mapping today.

Markus
Director, Engineering
Redwood Inference

On Sun, Sep 14, 2025 at 4:22 PM Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com> wrote:
> Attached is the proposed grid for the 2026 intern class and the ask for two new grad headcount slots...

---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 13:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Thanks — I reviewed the spreadsheet and Markus's notes. Finance summary:

- Budget: We have limited discretionary headcount in Q1. There is capacity if we reallocate two hires from the contractor budget (saves ~1.4x on spend) or if Recruiting delays two non-critical hires slated for Q2.
- Request path: please complete the headcount_request_form.pdf and route to HRBP (cc: me). If approved, I'll tag the two new grad slots as "expedited conversion" to prioritize requisition creation.
- EU uplift: we can absorb a 10% regional uplift for the two EU-remote interns within current offer band assumptions; mark them as "regionally adjusted" on the spreadsheet.

Action items for you, Evelyn:
1) Finalize the intern selection list in intern_offer_grid.xlsx with the region column filled.
2) Fill out the headcount_request_form.pdf and include the current requisition IDs for any backfilled roles.

If you need a formal exception (e.g., >2 new grads or >$25k uplift/offer) you'll need Laura's approval.

Priya Natarajan
Finance, People & Talent Ops
Redwood Inference

---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Evelyn — approved.

- Approve offers for the 7 returning interns per the spreadsheet (apply Markus's EU uplift where noted).
- Approve 2 new-grad headcount as contingent on Priya routing the headcount_request_form through HRBP (Priya, please confirm once submitted).
- Evelyn: please instruct Recruiting to begin verbal offers today and follow the standard two-week acceptance window. If any candidate needs a faster turnaround or there's a counter, loop me in immediately.

We'll track the hires in the next hiring review (10/01). Great work getting the data together — makes this efficient.

Laura Bennett
VP, Engineering
Redwood Inference

P.S. Evelyn — once you have the finalized spreadsheet, please forward the offers to our campus partner for scheduling (Samantha Jenkins).
\n
