- Compensation: I'm proposing baseline intern offer bands that map to the comp_bands_2026.pdf. For SWE interns converted to new-grads, we'd place them at the lower half of the E1 band per prior practice, with expedited review if market forces require.

Asks:
1) Markus — can you review the comp bands and confirm we can use the mapping in comp_bands_2026.pdf for both interns and new-grad placements? If you recommend an uplift for remote EU candidates, please call that out.
2) Priya — please confirm budget availability for two additional headcount in Q1 and advise whether this should be backfilled from hiring reserves or require headcount reallocation. I attached the headcount request PDF stub for finance workflow.
3) Laura — would appreciate a quick verbal ok (or escalation) so we can move offers to recruiting and start counter windows by 9/22.

Notes: several of these interns were high-impact (shipping infra optimizations and RLHF evaluation tooling). If we lose more than two, we risk slowing a couple of roadmap items.

Attachments: intern_offer_grid.xlsx (workbook), comp_bands_2026.pdf (summary), headcount_request_form.pdf (finance form).

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Engineering Manager, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

---


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Evelyn — thanks for the write-up and the materials. Quick thoughts after a first pass:

- Comp bands: comp_bands_2026.pdf looks reasonable for US-based interns. For remote EU hires we should add a regional modifier (we've historically added a 10%-12% uplift for cost-of-living and local market parity). Recommend we flag those 2 interns who are EU-remote for a +10% adjustment to stipend/offer.
- SWE new-grad placement: placing converted interns in the lower half of E1 is fine, but I'd like a clause for early performance-based uplift at 6 months to avoid losing them in the offer season.
- Headcount ask: I support 2 FTEs if Priya confirms budget. We should include a delta against current headcount plan so People Ops doesn't double-allocate.

I've annotated intern_offer_grid.xlsx with suggested salary rows (see comments column). If we need to move faster, I can sign-off on the comp mapping today.

Markus
Director, Engineering
Redwood Inference

On Sun, Sep 14, 2025 at 4:22 PM Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com> wrote:
> Attached is the proposed grid for the 2026 intern class and the ask for two new grad headcount slots...

---


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 13:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Thanks — I reviewed the spreadsheet and Markus's notes. Finance summary:

- Budget: We have limited discretionary headcount in Q1. There is capacity if we reallocate two hires from the contractor budget (saves ~1.4x on spend) or if Recruiting delays two non-critical hires slated for Q2.
- Request path: please complete the headcount_request_form.pdf and route to HRBP (cc: me). If approved, I'll tag the two new grad slots as \"expedited conversion\" to prioritize requisition creation.
- EU uplift: we can absorb a 10% regional uplift for the two EU-remote interns within current offer band assumptions; mark them as \"regionally adjusted\" on the spreadsheet.

Action items for you, Evelyn:
1) Finalize the intern selection list in intern_offer_grid.xlsx with the region column filled.
2) Fill out the headcount_request_form.pdf and include the current requisition IDs for any backfilled roles.

If you need a formal exception (e.g., >2 new grads or >$25k uplift/offer) you'll need Laura's approval.

Priya Natarajan
Finance, People & Talent Ops
Redwood Inference

---


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood-inference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intern class 2026 — proposed return offers + headcount ask

Evelyn — approved.

- Approve offers for the 7 returning interns per the spreadsheet (apply Markus's EU uplift where noted).
- Approve 2 new-grad headcount as contingent on Priya routing the headcount_request_form through HRBP (Priya, please confirm once submitted).
- Evelyn: please instruct Recruiting to begin verbal offers today and follow the standard two-week acceptance window. If any candidate needs a faster turnaround or there's a counter, loop me in immediately.

We'll track the hires in the next hiring review (10/01). Great work getting the data together — makes this efficient.

Laura Bennett
VP, Engineering
Redwood Inference

P.S. Evelyn — once you have the finalized spreadsheet, please forward the offers to our campus partner for scheduling (Samantha Jenkins).

source.subject: SeedCloud discovery — inference SLOs, topology and next steps
messages:
From: Sara Kim <sara.kim@seedcloud.io>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-09-14T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: SeedCloud / Redwood — discovery follow-up

Thanks for the call today — really helpful to walk through the options. Quick recap from our side and a couple items for you: 

Pain points we called out:
- Tail latency spikes impacting interactive search (current p95 450-600ms on our 256-token tests)
- Bursty traffic around business-hours peaks (short, high-concurrency bursts ~300–400 req/s)
- Cost sensitivity on per-token billing for large models; want to avoid overprovisioning
- Model size constraints: ML team prefers to keep a single 70B-ish model if possible rather than many distillations

Goals for a POC:
- Demonstrate consistent p95 latency under 150ms for 256-token responses
- Verify streaming reliability and prefix reuse (customers often repeat prompts)
- Validate a deployment that fits our cost targets (target ~40% lower than current baseline)

We attached a small traffic sample (seedcloud-traffic-sample.csv) and our short evaluation prompt set. Next steps from SeedCloud: please send a proposed mutual action plan, a scoped POC runbook, and a short note on topology options (dedicated vs hosted). Also a calendar invite for the POC planning call would be great.

Thanks — Sara
Sara Kim
Head of Platform
SeedCloud

Attachment: seedcloud-traffic-sample.csv (CSV - 42 KB)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Kim <sara.kim@seedcloud.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-09-14T17:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SeedCloud / Redwood — discovery follow-up

Sara — thanks for the clear summary and the sample data. Below is our action-oriented recap and a technical sketch for the POC topology so your engineering team can react quickly.

Recap (high level)
- Target SLOs: p95 latency <= 150ms for 256-token responses; p99 as a stretch goal <= 300ms under baseline loads.
- Throughput profile: baseline 50–80 req/s, hourly bursts to 300–400 req/s lasting ~20–45 minutes.
- Model: primary requirement is ~70B weight family (you mentioned preferring a single model). We propose testing a quantized 70B variant (4-bit/8-bit) with fast attention kernels and KV caching.

Technical POC topology (proposed)
- Environment: Dedicated GPU pool (us-west) for primary traffic with autoscaling minimum of 3 instances (to keep cold-starts bounded) and burst capacity up to 12 instances.
- Fallback: Host smaller 13B variant in us-east for overflow/failover to keep user-facing latency acceptable if dedicated pool is saturated.
- KV/prefix caching: Enable prefix/KV caching for repeated prompts — expected >30% token savings on your sample workload.
- Batching and latency guardrails: Continuous batching with max-batch latency cap of 10ms, and per-request latency budgets to enforce p95 target.
- Streaming: We will enable token-streaming with an early-flush policy for interactive UX.
- Security: TLS in transit, optional customer-managed KMS for at-rest keys in Dedicated.

Measurement & success criteria
- 7-day POC: synthetic and replayed traffic (we'll use the sample you shared), measure p50/p95/p99, tokens/op cost, and tail latency under burst events.
- Success: p95 <=150ms on 256 tokens during baseline and at least 75% of burst windows staying <300ms. Cost delta report vs current baseline.

Deliverables we will send by EOD:
- Mutual Action Plan (attachment: mutual_action_plan.xlsx) — timelines, responsibilities, and milestones
- Draft POC scope (attachment: draft-poc-scope.md) — test harness, acceptance criteria, and roles
- Topology sketches and a short checklist for infra pre-reqs (networking, IP allowlists, test creds)

Questions for you now
1) For data residency: do you require EU-hosted infra for any of the dataset or logs? (You referenced EU customers earlier.)
2) Can you confirm an internal point-of-contact for the ML engineering runbook and for procurement?

I'll schedule a 30-minute POC kickoff next week — sending invites now.

— Priya
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference

Attachments: mutual_action_plan.xlsx (XLSX - 12 KB), draft-poc-scope.md (MD - 3 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Kim <sara.kim@seedcloud.io>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-09-15T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SeedCloud / Redwood — discovery follow-up

On Mon, Sep 14, 2025 at 5:10 PM Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Technical POC topology (proposed)
> - Environment: Dedicated GPU pool (us-west) for primary traffic with autoscaling minimum of 3 instances...

Sara — quick additions from the infra/security side: 
- We'll provide a filled SOC2 questionnaire and a short security-readiness pack (attachment: security-questionnaire.pdf).
- Encryption: At-rest encryption is on by default; for Dedicated we can integrate your KMS (bring your own key).
- Audit logs: we can stream redwood inference audit logs to your designated S3/GCS bucket for retention. Confirm retention window requirements.
- Network: VPC peering vs private egress options — we'll outline network flows in the POC doc.

Operational ask: if you need EU data residency for logs or models, call that out now; we will map Dedicated nodes to eu-west regions and surface any SLA implications.

I looped in Markus for the kernel/quantization notes — Markus, take a look at the traffic sample and priya's proposed autoscale numbers; I think we should bench the 4-bit config and prepare a fallback 8-bit path if quality regressions appear.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

Attachment: security-questionnaire.pdf (PDF - 48 KB)

From: Sara Kim <sara.kim@seedcloud.io>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@seedcloud.io>
Date: 2025-09-16T08:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SeedCloud / Redwood — discovery follow-up

Thanks Priya and Ben — this is exactly the level of detail we need. Confirming next steps from SeedCloud: 
- Review mutual_action_plan.xlsx and draft-poc-scope.md internally and return any edits by Friday 2025-09-19.
- We require EU residency for a subset of customer logs; please include an eu-west Dedicated node option in the topology doc and a short note on SLA impact.
- Proposed POC kickoff week: Oct 6 — please confirm availability for the Redwood engineering leads and supply test credentials and the synthetic load harness by Oct 1.

Attendees: Sara Kim (Head of Platform), Tom Alvarez (ML Eng Lead), Priya Patel (SRE) — sending contact emails in a separate calendar invite.

We got the security-questionnaire.pdf — thanks. We'll return the completed SOC2 questions to Ben next week. 

Looking forward to the POC — Sara

Attachment references: mutual_action_plan.xlsx, draft-poc-scope.md

--
SeedCloud internal note: scheduling requested for Oct 6 POC kickoff.

source.subject: Re: Lumen Legaltech — Terraform support for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max + predictive)
related_links: GitHub: redwood-terraform PR-612 (Expose scaling policy min/max in Dedicated modules)
Linear: ENG-22152 (Terraform support for dedicated scaling policy)
Jira: INT-9412 (Request enable autoscaling bounds feature flag) — referenced for pilot enablement
source.body:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Aug 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Lumen Legaltech — Terraform support for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max + predictive)

Vanessa — looping you in on a customer requirement that’s now gating Lumen’s Dedicated rollout.

Lumen Legaltech (enterprise) will only manage Dedicated config via IaC (Terraform). They specifically need the new scaling policy fields:
- min_replicas / max_replicas (hard bounds)
- predictive_mode toggle (on/off) + any required fields

They’re asking for:
1) When Terraform support will be available (even beta is fine)
2) Any interim workaround (API/Console is a non-starter for change management)

Do we have an ETA / PR in flight? Lauren, FYI in case this impacts their timeline.

Thanks,
Irene
—
Irene Choi
Enterprise Programs | Redwood Inference


---

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Aug 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM
Subject: Re: Lumen Legaltech — Terraform support for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max + predictive)

Yep — we have work in progress on the Terraform side.

Status:
- We opened ENG-22152 for Terraform provider/module support.
- A PR is in flight on redwood-terraform (PR-612) to expose min/max bounds on the Dedicated deployment resource.

source.subject: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)
source.body:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Neha — looping you in for an infra leadership review.

We’re finalizing semantics for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max). A recurring enterprise ask is “set min high enough to guarantee baseline throughput,” but we need a clear stance on what happens when region capacity can’t meet a customer’s min.

Questions we need to lock this week:
1) If a customer sets min to a level we can’t provision in-region (shortage), what behavior + comms do we want? (degraded state, alerts, CS guidance)
2) Should we enforce any guardrails at config time (e.g., recommended min range, approvals for very high mins, per-account caps)?
3) Capacity planning implications: do we need a new internal workflow to reserve headroom when mins increase?

Vanessa has a proposal draft for the autoscaler behavior. I attached a 1-pager with shortage scenarios.

Attachment: min-bounds-shortage-scenarios-v2.pdf (application/pdf)

Related: SUP-28452 (min not met during region shortage), ENG-22151 (shortage behavior).

Can you take a look and reply with your concerns / suggested policy? If helpful, we can do a 20 min sync today.

Thanks,
Lauren
Product Lead, Dedicated


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Thanks — read the 1-pager.

My main concern is that “min” will be interpreted contractually (even if we don’t intend it that way) and will cause escalations when regional supply is constrained. If we ship this without crisp semantics + surfacing, we’ll create a failure mode that looks like: customer sets min, Redwood “accepts” it, later we can’t fulfill, and the autoscaler thrashes / keeps trying.

What I’d like to see:
- Explicit degraded state when min cannot be met (and it should be sticky + rate-limited to avoid flapping).
- Clear separation between:
  a) configured min (customer intent)
  b) effective provisioned capacity (what we could actually allocate)
  c) reason codes (region shortage vs quota vs internal error).
- Comms + operational flow:
  - automatic alert to on-call + CS when a customer is below min for >N minutes
  - customer-facing message in Console (“We are currently unable to allocate the configured minimum due to regional capacity constraints; contact support”)
  - no silent fallback that hides the fact we’re violating the min intent.

On guardrails:
- I’m in favor of a “soft” warning for high mins and a “hard” admin approval for extremely high mins in a single region (i.e., values that materially affect fleet headroom). This can be internal-only at first.

Capacity planning:
- We should treat changes to min as a capacity event and pipe them into our planning dashboard. Otherwise, we’ll get surprised by multiple customers ratcheting mins.

Vanessa: can you confirm whether the current implementation will keep attempting scale-up to min every loop? If so, we need backoff to prevent thundering herd behavior during shortage.

Priya — would like your take on whether we want to promise anything around “min” in GA language, or make it clear it’s a best-effort control with shortage signaling.

-Neha
Infra / Reliability


---

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:31 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Confirming behavior + proposal.

Current autoscaler loop (pre-shortage work):
- Computes desired replicas from reactive signals (queue depth/saturation) + optional predictive bias
- Clamps desired into [min, max]
- Submits to fleet allocator

If the allocator can’t place GPUs (regional shortage), today it returns “insufficient capacity,” and we retry on the next loop. Without additional logic, yes, we’d keep trying each cycle.

What we’re planning under ENG-22151 / PR-48270:
- Introduce a distinct state when provisioned < min due to allocator shortage:
  - emit metric: dedicated_autoscaler_min_unmet{deployment_id, region, reason="insufficient_capacity"}
  - add exponential backoff on allocator retries (with a cap), to avoid stampeding the allocator
  - keep desired clamped to min (intent preserved) but prevent rapid repeated place attempts
- Surface “min unmet” in structured logs and the capacity signal feed to routing so routing can degrade gracefully.

Open question we need product/leadership decision on:
- Do we block saving config with very high min values unless fleet has headroom? That’s hard because headroom is dynamic. Alternative is to allow save but show warning + make “min unmet” explicit.

Also: the “min unmet” condition should not trigger scale-down logic; it should freeze scale-down decisions while unmet (we don’t want oscillation).

Happy to jump on a call.

-Vanessa
Fleet Autoscaling


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 11:18 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Agree with Neha’s framing: we need deterministic semantics + explicit signaling.

Decision direction for GA:
1) “Min” is a hard bound on autoscaler intent (we will not intentionally scale below it), but it is not a guarantee of instantaneous provisioning in a capacity shortage.
2) We must have first-class “min unmet” degraded state in Console and in alerts.
3) We should not block config saves based on current headroom (too volatile), but:
   - add warnings for high mins
   - add internal approval gate (behind an admin workflow) for unusually large min increases on a single deployment/region during pilot/early GA.
4) Capacity planning: log all min changes as events and get a weekly rollup of total committed mins per region (by tier) so infra planning is not blind.

Lauren: please ensure the release notes/docs do not imply “min == reserved capacity” unless it’s explicitly coupled with a contract SKU.

Vanessa: ensure allocator retry backoff and state-stickiness are in the canary criteria.

-Priya
Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

+1.

Two additional operational asks:
- Alerting thresholds should differentiate:
  a) min unmet due to shortage (page only if sustained + customer impact metrics show queue/latency)
  b) min unmet due to internal errors (page faster)

- CS comms template: “We are seeing regional capacity constraints; here are options” (temporary max increase in alternate region is out-of-scope now, but at least we can suggest raising max in-region if the issue is not shortage, or reducing model concurrency, etc.).

Lauren: can we ensure SUP-28452’s customer story is reflected in the CS playbook? This is exactly the failure mode that becomes a fire drill.

Vanessa: please include reason codes we can use in paging + dashboards (insufficient_capacity vs quota vs allocator_error).

-Neha


---

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 2:38 PM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

This is super helpful — thanks all.

Captured decisions / actions:
- Product semantics: min is hard intent bound; shortages -> explicit “min unmet” degraded state (no implied guarantee of provisioning).
- Eng (Vanessa): implement allocator backoff + sticky degraded state + reason codes; ensure signal is exported to routing.
- SRE/Obs: add alerting split by reason; wire into ENG-22145 scope.
- Docs/Release: avoid language that equates min to “reserved” unless paired with contract; add FAQ: “What happens if min cannot be met?”
- Capacity planning: we’ll create an event stream for min changes; weekly regional rollup to infra planning (pilot first).
- Guardrails: Console warning for high mins; internal approval gate (admin workflow) for unusually large min increases during pilot/early GA.

I’ll update the PRD + ADR notes and link to ENG-22151. Also will make sure SUP-28452 is incorporated into the CS escalation playbook.

If anything else comes up as you review PR-48270 / the canary plan, please flag.

-Lauren

title: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Neha — looping you in for an infra leadership review.

We’re finalizing semantics for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max). A recurring enterprise ask is “set min high enough to guarantee baseline throughput,” but we need a clear stance on what happens when region capacity can’t meet a customer’s min.

Questions we need to lock this week:
1) If a customer sets min to a level we can’t provision in-region (shortage), what behavior + comms do we want? (degraded state, alerts, CS guidance)
2) Should we enforce any guardrails at config time (e.g., recommended min range, approvals for very high mins, per-account caps)?
3) Capacity planning implications: do we need a new internal workflow to reserve headroom when mins increase?

Vanessa has a proposal draft for the autoscaler behavior. I attached a 1-pager with shortage scenarios.

Attachment: min-bounds-shortage-scenarios-v2.pdf (application/pdf)

Related: SUP-28452 (min not met during region shortage), ENG-22151 (shortage behavior).

Can you take a look and reply with your concerns / suggested policy? If helpful, we can do a 20 min sync today.

Thanks,
Lauren
Product Lead, Dedicated


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Thanks — read the 1-pager.

My main concern is that “min” will be interpreted contractually (even if we don’t intend it that way) and will cause escalations when regional supply is constrained. If we ship this without crisp semantics + surfacing, we’ll create a failure mode that looks like: customer sets min, Redwood “accepts” it, later we can’t fulfill, and the autoscaler thrashes / keeps trying.

What I’d like to see:
- Explicit degraded state when min cannot be met (and it should be sticky + rate-limited to avoid flapping).
- Clear separation between:
  a) configured min (customer intent)
  b) effective provisioned capacity (what we could actually allocate)
  c) reason codes (region shortage vs quota vs internal error).
- Comms + operational flow:
  - automatic alert to on-call + CS when a customer is below min for >N minutes
  - customer-facing message in Console (“We are currently unable to allocate the configured minimum due to regional capacity constraints; contact support”)
  - no silent fallback that hides the fact we’re violating the min intent.

On guardrails:
- I’m in favor of a “soft” warning for high mins and a “hard” admin approval for extremely high mins in a single region (i.e., values that materially affect fleet headroom). This can be internal-only at first.

Capacity planning:
- We should treat changes to min as a capacity event and pipe them into our planning dashboard. Otherwise, we’ll get surprised by multiple customers ratcheting mins.

Vanessa: can you confirm whether the current implementation will keep attempting scale-up to min every loop? If so, we need backoff to prevent thundering herd behavior during shortage.

Priya — would like your take on whether we want to promise anything around “min” in GA language, or make it clear it’s a best-effort control with shortage signaling.

-Neha
Infra / Reliability


---

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 10:31 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Confirming behavior + proposal.

Current autoscaler loop (pre-shortage work):
- Computes desired replicas from reactive signals (queue depth/saturation) + optional predictive bias
- Clamps desired into [min, max]
- Submits to fleet allocator

If the allocator can’t place GPUs (regional shortage), today it returns “insufficient capacity,” and we retry on the next loop. Without additional logic, yes, we’d keep trying each cycle.

What we’re planning under ENG-22151 / PR-48270:
- Introduce a distinct state when provisioned < min due to allocator shortage:
  - emit metric: dedicated_autoscaler_min_unmet{deployment_id, region, reason="insufficient_capacity"}
  - add exponential backoff on allocator retries (with a cap), to avoid stampeding the allocator
  - keep desired clamped to min (intent preserved) but prevent rapid repeated place attempts
- Surface “min unmet” in structured logs and the capacity signal feed to routing so routing can degrade gracefully.

Open question we need product/leadership decision on:
- Do we block saving config with very high min values unless fleet has headroom? That’s hard because headroom is dynamic. Alternative is to allow save but show warning + make “min unmet” explicit.

Also: the “min unmet” condition should not trigger scale-down logic; it should freeze scale-down decisions while unmet (we don’t want oscillation).

Happy to jump on a call.

-Vanessa
Fleet Autoscaling


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 11:18 AM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

Agree with Neha’s framing: we need deterministic semantics + explicit signaling.

Decision direction for GA:
1) “Min” is a hard bound on autoscaler intent (we will not intentionally scale below it), but it is not a guarantee of instantaneous provisioning in a capacity shortage.
2) We must have first-class “min unmet” degraded state in Console and in alerts.
3) We should not block config saves based on current headroom (too volatile), but:
   - add warnings for high mins
   - add internal approval gate (behind an admin workflow) for unusually large min increases on a single deployment/region during pilot/early GA.
4) Capacity planning: log all min changes as events and get a weekly rollup of total committed mins per region (by tier) so infra planning is not blind.

Lauren: please ensure the release notes/docs do not imply “min == reserved capacity” unless it’s explicitly coupled with a contract SKU.

Vanessa: ensure allocator retry backoff and state-stickiness are in the canary criteria.

-Priya
Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Region capacity risk review: customer min bounds (Dedicated autoscaling)

+1.

Two additional operational asks:
- Alerting thresholds should differentiate:
  a) min unmet due to shortage (page only if sustained + customer impact metrics show queue/latency)
  b) min unmet due to internal errors (page faster)

- CS comms template: “We are seeing regional capacity constraints; here are options” (temporary max increase in alternate region is out-of-scope now, but at least we can suggest raising max in-region if the issue is not shortage, or reducing model concurrency, etc.).

Lauren: can we ensure SUP-28452’s customer story is reflected in the CS playbook? This is exactly the failure mode that becomes a fire drill.

Vanessa: please include reason codes we can use in paging + dashboards (insufficient_capacity vs quota vs allocator_error).

-Neha


---

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 2:38 PM

Captured decisions / actions:
- Product semantics: min is hard intent bound; shortages -> explicit “min unmet” degraded state (no implied guarantee of provisioning).
- Eng (Vanessa): implement allocator backoff + sticky degraded state + reason codes; ensure signal is exported to routing.
- SRE/Obs: add alerting split by reason; wire into ENG-22145 scope.
- Docs/Release: avoid language that equates min to “reserved” unless paired with contract; add FAQ: “What happens if min cannot be met?”
- Capacity planning: we’ll create an event stream for min changes; weekly regional rollup to infra planning (pilot first).
- Guardrails: Console warning for high mins; internal approval gate (admin workflow) for unusually large min increases during pilot/early GA.

I’ll update the PRD + ADR notes and link to ENG-22151. Also will make sure SUP-28452 is incorporated into the CS escalation playbook.

If anything else comes up as you review PR-48270 / the canary plan, please flag.

-Lauren

source.subject: Service availability: request for credit eligibility and calculation (OrbitAI)
messages:
From: Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: OrbitAI Billing <billing@orbitai.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Service disruption 6/16 — request for SLA credit eligibility

Jordan —

Our platform (OrbitAI) experienced significant latency spikes and partial availability loss on June 16 between 02:18 and 06:41 UTC. We saw a sharp increase in failed inference calls and a 35–50% latency increase for critical endpoints. This impacted our production customers and triggered our contractual SLA review.

Per section 3.2 of our contract, we are requesting confirmation of eligibility for service credits and a preliminary quantification of the expected credit. Attached are our client-side logs and a short timeline (orbitai_service_logs_0616.txt). Please let us know what information you need from our side to move this forward.

We'd like an initial response today with expected next steps and an ETA for a calculation. OrbitAI would prefer credits applied as an invoice adjustment for July billing if possible.

Thanks,
Samara Cho
Head of Operations, OrbitAI
samara@orbitai.com
+1 415-555-0147

Attachments: orbitai_service_logs_0616.txt

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — request for SLA credit eligibility

Hi Samara —

Thank you for the quick note and the logs. I'm really sorry OrbitAI was impacted. We've opened an internal incident (INC-8472) and I've looped in Marcus (support) and Priya (billing) to assess eligibility and produce the credit calculation.

Plan / next steps:
- Marcus will verify the incident window and whether the impact meets the SLA definition of "service unavailability".
- Priya will prepare an estimated credit amount and advise on the mechanics (invoice adjustment vs credit note).
- I will share our incident report and the calculation spreadsheet once the first-pass numbers are ready.

Can you confirm whether OrbitAI wants the credit as an account adjustment (applied to July invoice) or as a credit note? We'll default to invoice adjustment unless you prefer otherwise.

I'll follow up with a preliminary eligibility note by EOD.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 09:12 AM Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com> wrote:
> Jordan —
>
> Our platform (OrbitAI) experienced significant latency spikes and partial availability loss on June 16 between 02:18 and 06:41 UTC...
>

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — technical verification (INC-8472)

Jordan / Evelyn —

I've finished a preliminary verification of the serving logs and routing traces for the impacted timeframe. Summary:
- Root cause: an autoscaler regression affecting a subset of shared routing pools; approximately 80 minutes of degraded throughput (higher tail latency) and a 2 hour, 3 minute window where some model-serving tasks retried and timed out.
- Measured full unavailability (>= 50% request failures) for a continuous 22-minute window from 03:49–04:11 UTC. Outside that window we saw elevated latency and increased error rates.
- Affected tenants: a subset of customers pinned to the affected routing pools — OrbitAI is confirmed as impacted based on their request signatures.

SLA assessment approach (per contract):
1) We measure total minutes of full unavailability for the month and calculate the percentage of uptime loss relative to total minutes in billing period.
2) Credit tiers: 99.9–99.5% = 10% credit, 99.4–99.0% = 25% credit, <99.0% = 50% credit of monthly committed fees (example in contract).

For OrbitAI this month, using our preliminary telemetry and their usage weight on the affected pools, I'm estimating the entitlement is in the 10–25% band. Exact minutes and accounting for partial degradation need spreadsheet work.

I've attached a timeline extract (downtime_timeline_INC8472.csv) and a quick calc I ran locally. I'll produce a formal spreadsheet for Priya to convert into an invoice adjustment.

Marcus
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference
Attachments: downtime_timeline_INC8472.csv

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — preliminary credit calculation and next steps

Samara —

Attached are the formal artifacts: Redwood's incident report (incident-report-INC-8472.pdf) and the credit calculation workbook (redwood_credit_calc_OrbitAI.xlsx). Summary of the outcome:

- Verified unavailability window (full failures): 03:49–04:11 UTC on 2025-06-16 -> 22 minutes.
- Weighting for partial degradation minutes (02:18–06:41 UTC) applied per contractual method — we prorated by request volume across the interval.
- Final calculation: entitlement equals a 12.5% credit of OrbitAI's June committed usage billing (per the committed capacity tier). The spreadsheet has the minute-by-minute breakdown and assumptions.

Mechanics & timeline:
- We can apply the credit as an invoice adjustment to your July invoice (preferred method), or issue a credit note if you require it on record.
- Once you confirm acceptance, Priya will finalize the accounting entry and we will apply it to July billing; you should see the adjustment reflected within 7–10 business days.
- If OrbitAI requests a written acceptance or a countersigned form for audit purposes, we can provide one — let us know the required format.

Please see the attached files. Happy to hop on a quick call if you'd like Marcus to walk through the technical minutes and assumptions.

Thanks and again sorry for the disruption.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: incident-report-INC-8472.pdf; redwood_credit_calc_OrbitAI.xlsx

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 11:05 AM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samara —
>
> Thank you for the quick note and the logs. I've opened an internal incident (INC-8472) and looped in Marcus and Priya...
>

From: Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: OrbitAI Billing <billing@orbitai.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-20T15:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — preliminary credit calculation and next steps

Jordan / Team —

Thanks for the rapid turnaround and for the clear breakdown. OrbitAI accepts the 12.5% credit as calculated and would prefer an invoice adjustment applied to our July invoice. No countersignature is required for our records, but please include the incident-report PDF alongside the invoice entry for our finance team.

One small ask: can you include the minute-by-minute CSV (you mentioned a timeline extract) in the invoice packet so our auditors can reconcile quickly?

Appreciate the help — we'll expect the adjustment in 7–10 business days as you outlined.

Regards,
Samara Cho
Head of Operations, OrbitAI

On Thu, Jun 19, 2025 at 10:22 AM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Samara —
>
> Attached are the formal artifacts: Redwood's incident report and the credit calculation workbook...
>

I'll follow up with a preliminary eligibility note by EOD.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 09:12 AM Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com> wrote:
> Jordan —
>
> Our platform (OrbitAI) experienced significant latency spikes and partial availability loss on June 16 between 02:18 and 06:41 UTC...

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-18T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — technical verification (INC-8472)

Jordan / Evelyn —

I've finished a preliminary verification of the serving logs and routing traces for the impacted timeframe. Summary:
- Root cause: an autoscaler regression affecting a subset of shared routing pools; approximately 80 minutes of degraded throughput (higher tail latency) and a 2 hour, 3 minute window where some model-serving tasks retried and timed out.
- Measured full unavailability (>= 50% request failures) for a continuous 22-minute window from 03:49–04:11 UTC. Outside that window we saw elevated latency and increased error rates.
- Affected tenants: a subset of customers pinned to the affected routing pools — OrbitAI is confirmed as impacted based on their request signatures.

SLA assessment approach (per contract):
1) We measure total minutes of full unavailability for the month and calculate the percentage of uptime loss relative to total minutes in billing period.
2) Credit tiers: 99.9–99.5% = 10% credit, 99.4–99.0% = 25% credit, <99.0% = 50% credit of monthly committed fees (example in contract).

For OrbitAI this month, using our preliminary telemetry and their usage weight on the affected pools, I'm estimating the entitlement is in the 10–25% band. Exact minutes and accounting for partial degradation need spreadsheet work.

I've attached a timeline extract (downtime_timeline_INC8472.csv) and a quick calc I ran locally. I'll produce a formal spreadsheet for Priya to convert into an invoice adjustment.

Marcus
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference
Attachments: downtime_timeline_INC8472.csv


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samara Cho <samara@orbitai.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-06-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Service disruption 6/16 — preliminary credit calculation and next steps

Samara —

Attached are the formal artifacts: Redwood's incident report (incident-report-INC-8472.pdf) and the credit calculation workbook (redwood_credit_calc_OrbitAI.xlsx). Summary of the outcome:

- Verified unavailability window (full failures): 03:49–04:11 UTC on 2025-06-16 -> 22 minutes.
- Weighting for partial degradation minutes (02:18–06:41 UTC) applied per contractual method — we prorated by request volume across the interval.
- Final calculation: entitlement equals a 12.5% credit of OrbitAI's June committed usage billing (per the committed capacity tier). The spreadsheet has the minute-by-minute breakdown and assumptions.

Mechanics & timeline:
- We can apply the credit as an invoice adjustment to your July invoice (preferred method), or issue a credit note if you require it on record.
- Once you confirm acceptance, Priya will finalize the accounting entry and we will apply it to July billing; you should see the adjustment reflected within 7–10 business days.
- If OrbitAI requests a written acceptance or a countersigned form for audit purposes, we can provide one — let us know the required format.

Please see the attached files. Happy to hop on a quick call if you'd like Marcus to walk through the technical minutes and assumptions.

Thanks and again sorry for the disruption.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: incident-report-INC-8472.pdf; redwood_credit_calc_OrbitAI.xlsx

source.subject: Request: Confirmation of Subprocessor Decommission + Evidence for EU Data Flows
messages:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@brightworks.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: security@brightworks.com
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 09:13:00 -0700
Subject: Confirmation of subprocessor decommission + evidence needed for EU data flows

Hi Jordan,

Hope you’re well. As part of our ongoing vendor risk reviews we need written confirmation that the third-party 'EdgeAccelerate' (vendor contact: Markus Schröder) will be decommissioned from production paths that handle EU personal data no later than July 31, 2025.

Specifically, can you please:
- Confirm decommission timeline and the fallback routing you will use for EU traffic.
- Provide the latest subprocessor inventory that shows EdgeAccelerate removal and the effective date.
- Share a simple data flow diagram (at the network/app layer) showing where EU tokens/logs may be stored and the export paths after the change.
- Indicate whether any exports to the US or other third countries will occur during the migration and provide mitigation controls.

We'd like this before our audit date on June 10. If you need a longer timeline please advise and we’ll evaluate. Marked urgent on our side because this impacts our internal DPA and SOC 2 scope.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Head of Procurement, BrightWorks Inc.
lena.ortiz@brightworks.com
+44 20 7946 0123

Attachments: none

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@brightworks.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Confirmation of subprocessor decommission + evidence needed for EU data flows

Hi Lena — thanks for the note and the clear checklist.

Short answer: we will retire EdgeAccelerate from EU production paths and route affected workloads to our in-house proxy pool and a verified EU CDN by 2025-07-31. I’ll coordinate to get you the inventory and a draft data flow diagram.

Next steps from our side:
- Priya (cc) will confirm the contractual effective date and whether a DPA addendum is required.
- Diego (cc) will pull the security annotations for the migration and confirm whether any limited log export to non-EU regions is required during the cutover.
- I’ll gather the subprocessor inventory and a dataflow sketch and send by June 4 at the latest. If that slips I’ll notify you with a proposed new date.

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachment: provisional_schedule.xlsx (stub)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@brightworks.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 08:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Confirmation of subprocessor decommission + evidence needed for EU data flows

Jordan / Lena —

I reviewed the request. A couple of notes to make BrightWorks' audit happy:
1) Provide a dated subprocessor inventory (.csv) showing current roster and an "effective_removal_date" column for EdgeAccelerate.
2) Attach the excerpt of the DPA clause that references subprocessor replacement/deprecation and the last notification date. If we need to produce a DPA addendum, we should state the effective date in the response.
3) For the data flow diagram please include: where ephemeral KV caches live, which monitoring/metrics pipelines receive logs, and whether any archival snapshots go to third countries.

Jordan, if you want I can draft the short legal paragraph confirming the effective removal date once Diego confirms there are no export exceptions during the cutover.

Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

Attachment: dpa_clause_reference.txt (stub)

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@brightworks.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 10:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Confirmation of subprocessor decommission + evidence needed for EU data flows

Lena / Jordan / Priya —

Quick security note: EdgeAccelerate is used only for edge caching in a subset of EU routes. For the planned migration we will:
- Drain active sessions and switch traffic to our EU-resident proxy pool (no user payloads are stored long-term on EdgeAccelerate).
- Turn off metric export to external analytics during the cutover window. Any temporary exports for troubleshooting will be restricted, logged, and time-limited; we will provide a checklist of those operations.

I’ve uploaded a high-level EU dataflow PDF and the redacted log retention matrix to the shared folder (link below).

Diego Fernandez
Security Ops, Redwood Inference
diego.fernandez@redwood.com

Uploaded: data_flow_eu_region_v2.pdf; log_retention_matrix_redacted.csv
Shared folder: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/brightworks/subprocessor-evidence-2025

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@brightworks.com>, security@brightworks.com
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Confirmation of subprocessor decommission + evidence needed for EU data flows

Lena —

Per our internal review (Priya + Diego cc'd), please find the requested items in the shared folder:
- subprocessor_inventory_Q2_2025.csv (shows EdgeAccelerate removal with effective_removal_date = 2025-07-31)
- data_flow_eu_region_v2.pdf (high-level network/app layer diagram)
- log_retention_matrix_redacted.csv (retention by subsystem; note: no long-term user payloads on EdgeAccelerate)
- dpa_clause_reference.txt (excerpt of relevant DPA language)

Summary of controls and timeline:
- Decommission target: 2025-07-31. After that date EdgeAccelerate will not receive EU production traffic.
- Fallback: EU requests will route to our EU proxy pool + VerifiedEU CDN (both EU data centers).
- Temporary exports: We may run one troubleshooting export to the US for a 4-hour window during the cutover if needed; this will be pre-authorized, logged, and limited to aggregated metrics.

Next steps:
- If BrightWorks needs a signed DPA addendum confirming the removal and export constraints, please share the addendum template. We can process via DocuSign; I’ve included a placeholder link below.
- If you want a short technical walkthrough, Diego and I can schedule a 30-minute call next week.

Thanks again for the heads up — let me know if you need more granular artifacts for audit.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: subprocessor_inventory_Q2_2025.csv, data_flow_eu_region_v2.pdf
DocuSign (placeholder): https://www.docusign.com/remote-sign/abc12345
Shared folder: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/brightworks/subprocessor-evidence-2025

Next steps from our side:
- Priya (cc) will confirm the contractual effective date and whether a DPA addendum is required.
- Diego (cc) will pull the security annotations for the migration and confirm whether any limited log export to non-EU regions is required during the cutover.
- I’ll gather the subprocessor inventory and a dataflow sketch and send by June 4 at the latest. If that slips I’ll notify you with a proposed new date.

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

source.subject: HealthCore: Tenant-scoped deletion & field-redaction options for PHI workloads
messages:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Avery / Diego,

Thanks for taking this. HealthCore is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will ingest clinical chat transcripts, structured vitals, and small document attachments that include PHI. Procurement asked me to capture a few specific requirements before we move to a security review: 

- Can Redwood support per-tenant retention policies (we need different windows per environment)?
- For PHI fields (patient name, MRN, SSN, clinical notes) we need a configurable maximum retention window and an automatic irreversible redaction option once the window expires.
- We require proof-of-deletion exports (auditable report) that we can store off-site for our auditors.
- Are there field-level redaction options (mask tokens / hashed pseudonymization) at ingest vs. deletion-time?
- Any limitations for attachments (PDFs/images) — can they also be redacted/removed on a per-tenant schedule?

We can share a filled questionnaire if that helps. Goal is to understand constraints (latency, costs, and whether this impacts availability SLAs).

Thanks — let me know if a quick call this week helps.

— Sarah
Sarah Kim
Director, Security & Compliance
HealthCore Clinics
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Sarah — appreciate the clarity here. High-level answers and proposed next steps below; attaching a short options brief (redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf) and a questionnaire template you can complete.

Short answers:
- Tenant-scoped retention: yes — per-tenant (org) policy with route-level overrides.
- Configurable windows: yes — arbitrary TTLs per field group (PHI, PII metadata) and per model/route.
- Irreversible redaction: we support irreversible field redaction (crypto-shredding of keys + deletion of token mappings) and a confirmable deletion workflow.
- Field-level options: both — we support ingest-time pseudonymization (hashing, reversible tokenization with KMS) and post-ingest deletion/redaction jobs. Ingest-time reduces footprint and risk but changes downstream analytics.
- Attachments: blobs are supported and can be deleted/zeroed out per schedule; we also offer OCR-based field extraction to allow selective redaction of text inside PDFs (additional processing step).

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Irreversible deletion for encrypted token mappings requires KMS key destruction or secure key-wrapping workflows; that has implications for legal holds and recoverability.
- Proof-of-deletion: we can export signed deletion manifests (timestamped, request-id, checksum-based) and delivery via secure S3 link or push to your SIEM.
- Availability: running aggressive background deletion/redaction jobs at scale can create IO spikes; we can schedule phased canary rollouts and rate-limit background work to preserve SLOs.

Next steps:
1) Can you share the high-level retention windows you expect for each data class (clinical notes, identifiers, attachments)?
2) If you prefer ingest-time pseudonymization vs. post-hoc deletion, tell us which workflows you want for the pilot.
3) We can schedule a 30-min tech call with Diego and Marcus (SRE/product) to walk process and show the deletion manifest format.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Notes on legal / compliance implications for HealthCore ask

Avery / Diego / Marcus — quick internal alignment before we reply with a formal proposal. Key points from legal/compliance perspective: 

1) Retention granularity: support per-tenant and per-route TTLs. We should propose default templates: 
   - PHI (identifiers, clinical notes): 90 days (pilot), configurable up to client-requested maximum;
   - PII-metadata (session IDs, usage logs w/o content): 365 days;
   - Attachments: configurable, default 180 days.

2) Redaction semantics: recommend offering two primary modes to customer: 
   - Ingest-time irreversible redaction: redact fields before they are stored; no keys retained. Use when client needs minimal retention risk.
   - Tokenize-with-reversible-keys: store tokens and KMS-wrapped mappings for cases where client requires later re-identification for support. Provide clear UI toggle and flag for legal_holds.

3) Proof-of-deletion: require support for signed manifests including: deletion_request_id, deleted_object_ids, checksums before/after, deletion_timestamp, operator_id, and canonical audit_hash. We can produce a PKI-signed manifest (service key) that HealthCore can verify. This satisfies auditors.

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Thanks — that level of detail helps. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Some of our records (state-level requirements) require a minimum retention window of 7 years for certain records. Can the per-field policy be set to a minimum (e.g., 7 years) if we request that?
- We will likely want ingest-time tokenization for identifiers but keep clinical notes for 90 days then delete. Is Redwood able to support hybrid policies per-field in the same record?
- For proof-of-deletion, we prefer a push to our SIEM (Syslog/TLS) and an encrypted S3 drop for manifests — can you confirm both delivery methods exist?

I've attached the initial completed questionnaire (healthcore-data-retention-questionnaire.xlsx). If helpful, we can take the tech call Thursday morning PT.

— Sarah
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>, HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.

Proposed next steps:
1) Tech call Thursday 10:00 AM PT — Diego/Mike (SRE) to demo deletion manifest and show ingestion pipeline options.
2) We will produce a short appendix in the SOW laying out: template TTLs, redaction modes (ingest/post), manifest schema, and delivery options.

If you confirm the Thursday slot I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

On Tue, May 20, 2025 at 10:22 AM Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com> wrote:
> Thanks — Thursday works for us. Looking forward to the demo and the manifest sample.

Thanks everyone — will follow up with the invite.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>, HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.

Proposed next steps:
1) Tech call Thursday 10:00 AM PT — Diego/Mike (SRE) to demo deletion manifest and show ingestion pipeline options.
2) We will produce a short appendix in the SOW laying out: template TTLs, redaction modes (ingest/post), manifest schema, and delivery options.

If you confirm the Thursday slot I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

On Tue, May 20, 2025 at 10:22 AM Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com> wrote:
> Thanks — Thursday works for us. Looking forward to the demo and the manifest sample.

Thanks everyone — will follow up with the invite.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Linda Zhao <linda.zhao@brightfin.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: MAP: POC network & access checklist — API keys, IP allowlist, VPC peering

Linda —

As discussed on the call, I drafted a short mutual action plan (attached) to align on the POC runbook and close timeline. High level asks for the POC environment we’ll include in the MAP: 

- Redwood: provide POC API keys scoped to a BrightFin evaluation org, temporary quota for load testing, and a test model variant.
- BrightFin: org provisioning (one evaluation org), IP allowlist (CIDR ranges), and VPC peering where applicable.
- Shared: schedule a 1-hour onboarding/kickoff to validate connectivity and permissions, plus a security checkpoint for SOC2 evidence requests.

Target timeline: 2 weeks to have full infra and keys in place, then a 1-week evaluation window. I’ve attached Draft_MAP_BrightFin_v1.pdf and a quick action-item CSV.

Please confirm owners for the items below so we can lock the MAP today:
- API key issuance (Redwood): Ben
- Org setup (BrightFin): Linda / Cloud Ops
- IP allowlist (BrightFin): Cloud Ops
- VPC peering (BrightFin & Redwood infra): Cloud Ops + Redwood Infra (Priya)
- Security evidence (BrightFin request): Marissa to capture

If you’re good with this, I’ll convert the draft into a signed MAP and add milestone dates.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

Attachment: Draft_MAP_BrightFin_v1.pdf (map draft, 3 pages)

From: Linda Zhao <linda.zhao@brightfin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightFin Cloud Ops <cloudops@brightfin.com>
Date: 2025-05-14T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: POC network & access checklist — API keys, IP allowlist, VPC peering

Ben —

Thanks for putting this together. High level looks good. A couple of clarifications before we finalize: 

1) For VPC peering: our infra team needs the exact CIDR you expect to route to/from and the peering contact. Is Priya the right Redwood contact?
2) IP allowlist: we prefer to manage allowlist at the load-balancer level. Can you provide the list of Redwood egress IPs (or a short-lived range) so Cloud Ops can add them?
3) API keys: can they be scoped to a test namespace and set to expire automatically after the evaluation?

We can commit to org provisioning within 48 hours once we have the CIDR and peering details. Kickoff next week looks fine — Wed or Thu afternoons are best.

-Linda
Linda Zhao
Sr. Cloud Ops Manager, BrightFin
linda.zhao@brightfin.com

> Linda —
> 
> As discussed on the call, I drafted a short mutual action plan (attached) to align on the POC runbook and close timeline. High level asks for the POC environment we’ll include in the MAP:...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Linda Zhao <linda.zhao@brightfin.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, BrightFin Cloud Ops <cloudops@brightfin.com>
Date: 2025-05-15T08:46:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: POC network & access checklist — API keys, IP allowlist, VPC peering

Linda — thanks for the quick reply. Yes, Priya will be the internal contact for peering and network details. I’ve put together a more detailed action item list inline below and attached a CSV with owners/dates. We recommend the following defaults for the POC to keep things simple: 

- API keys: created for an "eval" org, scoped to read/write generative endpoints, auto-expire at 30 days unless extended. Owner: Ben (Redwood).
- Org setup: BrightFin creates an evaluation org and a test team. Owner: BrightFin Cloud Ops (Linda).
- IP allowlist: BrightFin to add Redwood outbound IPs. We can provide either a short list of IPs or a CIDR range. Owner: BrightFin Cloud Ops.
- VPC peering: standard peering flow — exchange VPC IDs, CIDRs, and route table updates. Priya (Redwood Infra) will send a peering template and BGP/route recommendations. Owner: Priya Natarajan (Redwood).
- Security checkpoint: Marissa to collect SOC2 evidence items (audit logs, data retention policy, processing agreements) and route to your security team. Owner: Marissa Cole (Redwood).

Proposed milestone dates (target, adjustable):
- Day 0 (today): sign MAP and assign owners
- Day 2: BrightFin creates eval org + initial IP allowlist entry
- Day 5: Redwood issues API keys and provides network egress IP list
- Day 7–10: VPC peering configured and validated (connectivity test)
- Day 14: POC ready for full workload run

I attached MAP_action_items.csv with the above split out to help with tracking in HubSpot/Drive. If that looks good I’ll convert to a formal MAP doc and add dates.

Thanks — looping Priya and Marissa for network and security workstreams.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP_action_items.csv (3 rows)

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>

SOC2-evidence (example set to include in MAP):
- Audit log sample (7 days) — Redwood to provide export/sample
- Data retention & deletion policy (doc) — Redwood public policy link
- Access control summary (who has keys/roles) — short table

Diego: if you can confirm availability to provide the audit sample and point to the retention doc, I’ll add "Security evidence provided" as a MAP milestone with a 5-day SLA from when we receive the formal request.

Marissa Cole

source.subject: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
source.body:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
Attachments: PLG-to-Sales-Signal-Routing_Kickoff-Notes_2025-05-01.docx; Signal-taxonomy-and-thresholds_working-notes_v0.3.xlsx

Team —

Kicking off the “PLG-to-sales signal routing + playbook” project. We’re seeing more expansions initiate from self-serve usage, but we’re too inconsistent about catching the right moments (or we catch too many and spam).

Outcome we want: a privacy-aware, reliable signal pipeline that routes *sales-worthy* signals into HubSpot with the right owner + the right next step, and a playbook so AEs/SEs/CSMs follow up consistently.

Phase 0 (next 1–2 weeks): align on signal definitions + success metrics + privacy guardrails.

Goals (MVP):
- Define canonical signals: threshold (scale/reliability) + intent (Dedicated/Private/security).
- Daily/weekly window aggregation + dedupe + scoring into a “PLG Signal” object.
- Route qualified signals into HubSpot as company timeline notes + tasks.
- Deterministic ownership rules: preserve existing owner; else region/tier mapping; else round-robin.
- Pilot NA team with a limited cohort.
- Instrument outcomes: contacted → meeting → eval started → opp created (Dedicated/Private).

Non-goals for initial phase:
- No fully automated outbound without human review for early cohorts.
- Not building an in-house sequencing tool (start with HubSpot tasks/templates).

Asks (owners + dates):
1) Signal definitions + cut lines (Product/Growth/Sales):
   - Owner: Nikhil + Ben + Stephanie
   - Due: Fri 5/9
   - Output: “v1 signal taxonomy + thresholds” with expected weekly volume (and suppression rules).

2) Data pipeline MVP (Eng):
   - Owner: Logan + Priya
   - Due: MVP plan by Wed 5/7; implementation estimate after schema is final
   - Requirements: idempotent writes, dedupe keys, backfills, and audit logs.

3) HubSpot object + routing workflows (BizOps/SalesOps/MarketingOps):
   - Owner: David + Isabelle + Tyler
   - Due: initial spec by Fri 5/9

4) Privacy + data handling (Security/Legal):
   - Owner: Aisha
   - Due: guardrails + review checklist by Fri 5/9

5) Playbook + cadence (Sales/SE/CS):

- Noise controls are a requirement, not a “nice to have.” We will set a pilot cap per AE/week and suppression windows (Sales + Marketing).

Attachments: PLG-to-Sales-Signal-Routing_Kickoff-Notes_2025-05-01.docx; Signal-taxonomy-and-thresholds_working-notes_v0.3.xlsx

Team —

Kicking off the “PLG-to-sales signal routing + playbook” project. We’re seeing more expansions initiate from self-serve usage, but we’re too inconsistent about catching the right moments (or we catch too many and spam).

Outcome we want: a privacy-aware, reliable signal pipeline that routes *sales-worthy* signals into HubSpot with the right owner + the right next step, and a playbook so AEs/SEs/CSMs follow up consistently.

Phase 0 (next 1–2 weeks): align on signal definitions + success metrics + privacy guardrails.

Goals (MVP):
- Define canonical signals: threshold (scale/reliability) + intent (Dedicated/Private/security).
- Daily/weekly window aggregation + dedupe + scoring into a “PLG Signal” object.
- Route qualified signals into HubSpot as company timeline notes + tasks.
- Deterministic ownership rules: preserve existing owner; else region/tier mapping; else round-robin.
- Pilot NA team with a limited cohort.
- Instrument outcomes: contacted → meeting → eval started → opp created (Dedicated/Private).

Non-goals for initial phase:
- No fully automated outbound without human review for early cohorts.
- Not building an in-house sequencing tool (start with HubSpot tasks/templates).

Asks (owners + dates):
1) Signal definitions + cut lines (Product/Growth/Sales):
   - Owner: Nikhil + Ben + Stephanie
   - Due: Fri 5/9
   - Output: “v1 signal taxonomy + thresholds” with expected weekly volume (and suppression rules).

2) Data pipeline MVP (Eng):
   - Owner: Logan + Priya
   - Due: MVP plan by Wed 5/7; implementation estimate after schema is final
   - Requirements: idempotent writes, dedupe keys, backfills, and audit logs.

3) HubSpot object + routing workflows (BizOps/SalesOps/MarketingOps):
   - Owner: David + Isabelle + Tyler
   - Due: initial spec by Fri 5/9

4) Privacy + data handling (Security/Legal):
   - Owner: Aisha
   - Due: guardrails + review checklist by Fri 5/9

5) Playbook + cadence (Sales/SE/CS):
   - Owner: Stephanie + Markus + Jasmine (Ben for PLG messaging)
   - Due: draft by Fri 5/16 (so it’s ready for pilot)

Target pilot start: week of Mon 5/19.

Please reply-all with (a) confirmation you can own the above, (b) any blockers, and (c) “what would make this fail” risks we should address in Phase 0.

Thanks,
Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference
