5) Playbook + cadence (Sales/SE/CS):
   - Owner: Stephanie + Markus + Jasmine (Ben for PLG messaging)
   - Due: draft by Fri 5/16 (so it’s ready for pilot)

Target pilot start: week of Mon 5/19.

Please reply-all with (a) confirmation you can own the above, (b) any blockers, and (c) “what would make this fail” risks we should address in Phase 0.

Thanks,
Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference


---

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on Sales owner for NA pilot. Avery (pilot AE rep) and I can commit time for weekly quality review.

Two items I’d like us to nail early:
1) Noise controls — AEs will ignore this if we generate “busywork tasks.” We should set an explicit cap per AE per week for the pilot (even if we miss some).
2) What counts as “reliability signal” — repeated 429s and sustained latency spikes should probably be treated differently. For 429s, we need to know if they’re self-inflicted (bad retry loops) vs true capacity limits.

Nikhil/Ben: can we add a “confidence” + “why this triggered” snippet directly on the HubSpot task?

-Stephanie

Stephanie Nguyen
VP Sales, North America
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

+1 to suppression/noise control.

Marketing ask: we need explicit rules for when an AE task suppresses (or pauses) lifecycle emails, so we don’t double-touch and confuse customers.

Proposal for pilot:
- If an account is routed to AE task: pause “Dedicated/Private nurture” for 14 days.
- If Marketing has already sent a nurture touch in the last 3 business days: delay AE task creation by 2 days (unless it’s a compliance/security intent signal).

Tyler/I can own the workflow side if David confirms what fields / lists we’ll have available.

Camila

Camila Reyes
VP Marketing
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed from Eng sponsorship side.

A couple constraints to factor into Phase 0:
- We can reliably compute daily aggregates (usage/spend/error rates) from the analytics dataset with ~12–24h latency. Anything “real-time” will require additional infra and oncall.
- HubSpot API rate limits will become a problem if we do per-event writes. We should route only aggregated “qualified signals” and batch updates.

Logan and I will draft the MVP plan by Wed 5/7 as requested. Key design points we’ll cover:
- idempotency + dedupe key (account_id + signal_type + window_start)
- backfill job + replays for late telemetry
- audit log for what was sent to HubSpot (payload hash + timestamp)

Priya

Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed: David + Isabelle + Tyler will own HubSpot setup.

For the spec by 5/9, we’ll propose:
- Company properties:
  - last_plg_signal_type
  - last_plg_signal_at
  - plg_signal_score (0–100)
  - plg_signal_summary (short text, e.g., “7d spend +62%, 11x 429s, viewed Dedicated docs”)
  - plg_signal_suppressed_until
  - plg_signal_owner_rule (existing_owner/region/round_robin)
- Timeline note template: standardized “Why this triggered” block.
- Task template(s): “PLG signal follow-up (pilot)” with fields for persona + recommended next step.

One question for Product/Eng: do we want a custom object (“PLG Signal”) or store as properties + timeline notes? Custom object is cleaner but adds complexity + permission surface area. For pilot, I’d prefer properties + timeline note + task.

David

David Kim
Business Systems (HubSpot)
Redwood Inference


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 1:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on security/privacy guardrails.

Baseline requirements for Phase 0 approval:
- PII minimization: no end-user content, prompts, completions, or raw request payloads in HubSpot. No IP addresses.
- Aggregate-only metrics are OK (token volume, spend, error counts, doc page view categories), but keep “doc view” signals to coarse categories (e.g., “Security FAQ viewed” vs exact URL query params).
- Retention: define retention in HubSpot for signal fields and timeline notes (and how to support deletion requests).
- Access control: only GTM roles should see signal fields; we should avoid exposing to broad portal users.

Also: any “security intent signal” needs careful messaging. The playbook should avoid implying we’re monitoring anything beyond product usage + public docs interactions.

I’ll send a checklist by 5/9; looping in Marcus Lin for security engineering review if we end up doing custom objects.

-Aisha

Dr. Aisha Rahman
Security & Privacy
Redwood Inference


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, May 2, 2025 at 8:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Thanks all — quick recap and decisions so we can move.

Decisions (pilot):
- We will start with *aggregated qualified signals only* (daily/weekly windows) and avoid per-event HubSpot writes.
- For HubSpot, we’ll do properties + timeline note + task for pilot (no custom object). Revisit custom object after we validate signal volume + usefulness.
- Noise controls are a requirement, not a “nice to have.” We will set a pilot cap per AE/week and suppression windows (Sales + Marketing).

Owner confirmations:
- Signal definitions + thresholds: Nikhil / Ben / Stephanie (due 5/9)
- Eng MVP plan: Priya / Logan (plan due 5/7)
- HubSpot spec: David / Isabelle / Tyler (due 5/9)
- Privacy guardrails: Aisha (due 5/9)
- Playbook draft: Stephanie / Markus / Jasmine / Ben (due 5/16)

Open questions to close in Phase 0:
1) Confidence + “why this triggered” copy — agree on a standard snippet AEs can trust.
2) 429 interpretation — distinguish customer misconfiguration vs platform capacity; route differently.
3) Marketing suppression rules — finalize the exact timing + exceptions (Camila’s proposal is a good starting point).

Next meeting: 30 min working session Tue 5/6 @ 11:00am PT (calendar invite coming) to finalize the v1 list of signals and expected weekly volumes.

Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference

source.subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
source.body:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
Attachments: Route_Experiments_GA_Scope_2025-03-20.pdf

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)
- SRE: I need a committed block for alert review + game day + on-call readiness signoff (week of 3/31).
- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart
VP Product, Redwood Inference


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on scope as written.

Eng cut line proposal (Console 2025.04)
- Keep: results API basic deltas + guardrail banners; audit trail view; RBAC gates on all mutations.
- Keep: assignment determinism + “missing/low-entropy sticky key” warnings (we saw too much skew without this).
- Keep: query cost controls (aggregation-backed charts only; no raw high-cardinality drilldowns at GA).

Scope we should explicitly de-prioritize to protect schedule
- Anything resembling in-Console statistical significance. We should close the loop on the exploratory branch and ensure no UI hooks imply it.
- “Quality” UX beyond showing the eval score stream if present (no deep analysis). If we’re tight, we can display quality as a single normalized score trend per variant and ship richer breakdown later.

Risk callouts
- Results query load: we need the aggregation jobs stable + cached endpoints. If we miss that, Console page performance will regress under larger beta.
- Attribution completeness for streaming + retries: we’re dependent on the last fixes + validation in staging.

If you want a hard GA gate, I’d suggest: (1) attribution drop <0.5%, (2) propagation lag p99 within SLO, (3) no P1 alerts for 7 days during beta expansion.

-Dylan
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Aligned.

SRE coverage / asks
- We can staff a 2-hour alert/runbook review early next week + 90 min game day slot the week of 3/31.
- For GA go/no-go: we’ll need (a) propagation lag alerting enabled, (b) attribution drop-rate alert tuned (the earlier version was noisy), (c) the hard-stop kill switch verified end-to-end.

Strong +1 to keeping hard-stop disabled by default at GA. False positives here would be high-severity, so we should treat enabling it as a per-account opt-in until we have more history.

If product wants a single “break glass” story, we should document:
- who can stop experiments in emergency,
- how quickly config propagates,
- what customers will see when we soft-stop.

Sean
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed from Observability.

For GA, I’m signing off with these constraints (non-negotiable unless we re-review):
- Required metric dimensions: route_id (normalized template), experiment_id, variant_id.
- region allowed; model_id only where already present + sampled expansion (no “always-on” model_id tagging on all metrics).
- No user_id/customer_id in metrics labels. Stickiness keys must never be emitted as labels.
- Traces can include experiment_id/variant_id as span attrs, but the “export set” needs to stay consistent with our existing OTel conventions.

If we do the above, I’m comfortable widening beta and moving to GA enable-by-default after the initial region-by-region ramp.

Priya
Observability, Redwood Inference


---

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 2:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed.

Data quality plan (GA gating)
- Attribution drop-rate: target <0.5% overall and <1.0% for streaming traffic specifically. We’ll report both.
- The streaming/retry attribution bug is addressed; we’re validating in staging with synthetic replay this week.

Backfill
- We can backfill 7 days of rollups post-schema change. Beyond that, retention + cost make it not worth it for GA; we’ll document the cutoff clearly in Console (e.g., “variant metrics available from <date>”).

Propagation lag
- We’re instrumenting p50/p95/p99 propagation lag by region; Sean’s team will help set final alert thresholds after game day.

I’ll send a short note to Marissa/Dylan with the exact wording for the Console tooltip on “Why are my results missing?” so Support has something consistent.

Logan
Telemetry & Analytics Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on product side.

Comms / expectations we’ll set for GA
- “Experiment Results” = operational deltas + guardrails; not a statistical decision engine.
- We’ll add explicit copy in the results view: “No significance calculation. Use deltas + your own analysis.”
- We’ll call out the backfill cutoff/date in docs + in-product.

Beta → GA plan
- Week 1: GA flag on for existing beta accounts + 2 additional design partners; region ramp with daily check-ins.
- Week 2: widen to self-serve orgs with admin role + no custom SLA constraints.
- Then: enable by default once we have 7 clean days on the key gates Sean/Dylan listed.

Also: I’m aligning with Security on audit export wording (what’s included, retention expectations). That’s the #1 thing Helio/Northbridge keep asking about in the security reviews.

Marissa
Product, Console & Rollouts

- We can staff a 2-hour alert/runbook review early next week + 90 min game day slot the week of 3/31.

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed from Observability.

For GA, I’m signing off with these constraints (non-negotiable unless we re-review):
- Required metric dimensions: route_id (normalized template), experiment_id, variant_id.
- region allowed; model_id only where already present + sampled expansion (no “always-on” model_id tagging on all metrics).
- No user_id/customer_id in metrics labels. Stickiness keys must never be emitted as labels.
- Traces can include experiment_id/variant_id as span attrs, but the “export set” needs to stay consistent with our existing OTel conventions.

If we do the above, I’m comfortable widening beta and moving to GA enable-by-default after the initial region-by-region ramp.

Priya
Observability, Redwood Inference


---

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 2:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed.

Data quality plan (GA gating)
- Attribution drop-rate: target <0.5% overall and <1.0% for streaming traffic specifically. We’ll report both.
- The streaming/retry attribution bug is addressed; we’re validating in staging with synthetic replay this week.

Backfill
- We can backfill 7 days of rollups post-schema change. Beyond that, retention + cost make it not worth it for GA; we’ll document the cutoff clearly in Console (e.g., “variant metrics available from <date>”).

Propagation lag
- We’re instrumenting p50/p95/p99 propagation lag by region; Sean’s team will help set final alert thresholds after game day.

I’ll send a short note to Marissa/Dylan with the exact wording for the Console tooltip on “Why are my results missing?” so Support has something consistent.

Logan
Telemetry & Analytics Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on product side.

Comms / expectations we’ll set for GA
- “Experiment Results” = operational deltas + guardrails; not a statistical decision engine.
- We’ll add explicit copy in the results view: “No significance calculation. Use deltas + your own analysis.”
- We’ll call out the backfill cutoff/date in docs + in-product.

Beta → GA plan
- Week 1: GA flag on for existing beta accounts + 2 additional design partners; region ramp with daily check-ins.
- Week 2: widen to self-serve orgs with admin role + no custom SLA constraints.
- Then: enable by default once we have 7 clean days on the key gates Sean/Dylan listed.

Also: I’m aligning with Security on audit export wording (what’s included, retention expectations). That’s the #1 thing Helio/Northbridge keep asking about in the security reviews.

Marissa
Product, Console & Rollouts
text:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
Attachments: Route_Experiments_GA_Scope_2025-03-20.pdf

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)
- SRE: I need a committed block for alert review + game day + on-call readiness signoff (week of 3/31).
- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart
VP Product, Redwood Inference


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on scope as written.

Eng cut line proposal (Console 2025.04)
- Keep: results API basic deltas + guardrail banners; audit trail view; RBAC gates on all mutations.
- Keep: assignment determinism + “missing/low-entropy sticky key” warnings (we saw too much skew without this).
- Keep: query cost controls (aggregation-backed charts only; no raw high-cardinality drilldowns at GA).

Scope we should explicitly de-prioritize to protect schedule
- Anything resembling in-Console statistical significance. We should close the loop on the exploratory branch and ensure no UI hooks imply it.
- “Quality” UX beyond showing the eval score stream if present (no deep analysis). If we’re tight, we can display quality as a single normalized score trend per variant and ship richer breakdown later.

Risk callouts
- Results query load: we need the aggregation jobs stable + cached endpoints. If we miss that, Console page performance will regress under larger beta.
- Attribution completeness for streaming + retries: we’re dependent on the last fixes + validation in staging.

If you want a hard GA gate, I’d suggest: (1) attribution drop <0.5%, (2) propagation lag p99 within SLO, (3) no P1 alerts for 7 days during beta expansion.

-Dylan
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Aligned.

SRE coverage / asks
- We can staff a 2-hour alert/runbook review early next week + 90 min game day slot the week of 3/31.
- For GA go/no-go: we’ll need (a) propagation lag alerting enabled, (b) attribution drop-rate alert tuned (the earlier version was noisy), (c) the hard-stop kill switch verified end-to-end.

Strong +1 to keeping hard-stop disabled by default at GA. False positives here would be high-severity, so we should treat enabling it as a per-account opt-in until we have more history.

If product wants a single “break glass” story, we should document:
- who can stop experiments in emergency,
- how quickly config propagates,
- what customers will see when we soft-stop.

Sean
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


---

Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>

source.subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Priya — quick exec update on “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” with current milestone status + top risks/mitigations. Lauren/Marissa/Connor looping in for visibility.

Where we are (as of Mar 18)
- Control plane plan model + migrations: DONE (behind FF)
  - State machine: draft → running → held → aborted → completed
  - Promote/abort idempotency + audit hooks in place
- Routing primitives (stage-based split/partition selection): DONE for single-region; multi-region rules in final review
- Telemetry tagging for stage_id: DONE; mitigations for metrics cardinality landed (see hotfix)
- Gate evaluation v1 (policy engine): DONE for latency/error/saturation; “missing data” semantics default fail-closed w/ override
- Console: timeline + evidence endpoints DONE; UI work in progress (design ready, implementation ongoing)

Top risks & mitigations

1) Capacity fragmentation / effective throughput drop during early stages
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- On smaller reservations, a 1% / 10% stage can create a partition that’s too small to absorb burst + streaming concurrency → queue depth spikes, 429s, and noisy gate failures.
- We saw a variant of this in beta-like testing (similar to SUP-28522): canary partition reduced effective capacity and caused unexpected 429s.

Mitigations in flight:
- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Thanks Ava.

A couple quick questions so I can relay cleanly:
1) For the “spillover” rule to prevent starvation — are we comfortable that it won’t confuse customers (“I thought 10% was canary”) and that audit/evidence remains deterministic?
2) On telemetry lag → hold: what’s the auto-timeout? I don’t want pools sitting in held state for days without anyone noticing.
3) Any specific beta design partners already confirmed / readiness level?

-Priya


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Answers inline.

1) Spillover rule
- We’re scoping spillover as an SRE safety valve, not a silent behavior change.
- Determinism/auditability:
  - Routing decision remains deterministic given (stage, policy version, partition saturation state).
  - We will emit a stage-level annotation + metric when spillover is active (so gate evidence + audit trails can explain “why baseline absorbed some traffic”).
- Customer messaging:
  - Console will continue to show the configured stage (e.g., 10%) but will surface a warning banner if spillover is triggered (“Canary partition saturated; overflow enabled to protect availability”).
  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava

  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Priya — quick exec update on “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” with current milestone status + top risks/mitigations. Lauren/Marissa/Connor looping in for visibility.

Where we are (as of Mar 18)
- Control plane plan model + migrations: DONE (behind FF)
  - State machine: draft → running → held → aborted → completed
  - Promote/abort idempotency + audit hooks in place
- Routing primitives (stage-based split/partition selection): DONE for single-region; multi-region rules in final review
- Telemetry tagging for stage_id: DONE; mitigations for metrics cardinality landed (see hotfix)
- Gate evaluation v1 (policy engine): DONE for latency/error/saturation; “missing data” semantics default fail-closed w/ override
- Console: timeline + evidence endpoints DONE; UI work in progress (design ready, implementation ongoing)

Top risks & mitigations

1) Capacity fragmentation / effective throughput drop during early stages
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- On smaller reservations, a 1% / 10% stage can create a partition that’s too small to absorb burst + streaming concurrency → queue depth spikes, 429s, and noisy gate failures.
- We saw a variant of this in beta-like testing (similar to SUP-28522): canary partition reduced effective capacity and caused unexpected 429s.

Mitigations in flight:
- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Thanks Ava.

A couple quick questions so I can relay cleanly:
1) For the “spillover” rule to prevent starvation — are we comfortable that it won’t confuse customers (“I thought 10% was canary”) and that audit/evidence remains deterministic?
2) On telemetry lag → hold: what’s the auto-timeout? I don’t want pools sitting in held state for days without anyone noticing.
3) Any specific beta design partners already confirmed / readiness level?

-Priya


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Answers inline.

1) Spillover rule
- We’re scoping spillover as an SRE safety valve, not a silent behavior change.
- Determinism/auditability:
  - Routing decision remains deterministic given (stage, policy version, partition saturation state).
  - We will emit a stage-level annotation + metric when spillover is active (so gate evidence + audit trails can explain “why baseline absorbed some traffic”).
- Customer messaging:
  - Console will continue to show the configured stage (e.g., 10%) but will surface a warning banner if spillover is triggered (“Canary partition saturated; overflow enabled to protect availability”).
  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava

source.subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

1) Cardinality policy is non-negotiable:
   - allowlist labels only; no “free-form route name”, no tenant_id, no request_id
   - route_id/model_version_id must be normalized (single source of truth from control plane)
   - prompt-length/token-count must be bucketed (per standards)

2) Ingestion needs an explicit “drop detection” signal.
   We need to know when guards are dropping series so dashboards don’t show false “0 hits”. Please make that part of ENG-4823/4825 acceptance.

Logan/Nadia will own schema enforcement; Caleb will implement the guardrails.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:29 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Reliability.

A few guardrails:
- Please treat this as an operational change. Rollout needs canary gates + rollback plan (Connor + Sean to sign off before prod enablement).
- I want “telemetry pipeline health” on the same dashboard page as cache hit-rate. When data is missing, we need a clear indicator.
- Any p99 regression >1% at canary should block rollout unless explicitly approved by Runtime + SRE.

If we hit these gates, I’m comfortable proceeding.

Neha Kapoor
VP Infrastructure & Reliability


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 11:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

+1 go from SRE.

Specific SRE asks (so we don’t page ourselves to death):
- Alerts must be “guardrail” and avoid paging on workload mix shifts. Start with ticket-level alerts and promote to page only after we see stable baselines.
- Add an alert on “hit-rate appears to be zero because metrics are missing” (drop detection + missing-tag).
- Runbook must include:
  - how to validate join keys (route_id/model_version_id)
  - how to distinguish real regression vs telemetry failure
  - rollback steps and who owns each step

Allison will drive dashboards + runbook; I’ll approve alert policy before prod.

Sean
Director, Site Reliability Engineering


---

From: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Thanks all.

From Product side: +1 to proceed. The minimal requirements for the “recommendations MVP” slice are:
- Output is per route + model_version + region (at minimum) and includes a plain-English “why” with suggested levers.
- Must include caveats when confidence is low (e.g., insufficient sample, high workload volatility).
- We should explicitly avoid any recommendation that implies logging prompt content.

If we can land a first internal report + 1–2 example recommendations validated by CS, that’s a win for this sprint.

Paula
Sr PM, Telemetry Requirements / Rollouts


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 2:06 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Approved — thank you.

Decisions captured:
- Proceed with the sprint starting tomorrow.
- Must ship behind flags; staging -> canary -> prod only with gates.
- Cardinality policy enforced via allowlists + ingestion guards; add explicit drop-detection metrics.
- Perf gates: p95 <0.5% regression at default sampling; p99 >1% at canary blocks unless Runtime+SRE approve.
- Alerts start ticket-level; page only after baseline stabilization.
- Runbook + telemetry pipeline health indicator are required deliverables.

Talia: please set up a daily 15-min leadership checkpoint this week only (Mon–Fri) to keep decision latency low.

Connor/Allison/Logan/Omar: please reply-all by EOD with the concrete rollout timeline (staging date, canary start, target prod date) + explicit rollback owner.

Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference

A few guardrails:

- I want “telemetry pipeline health” on the same dashboard page as cache hit-rate. When data is missing, we need a clear indicator.
- Any p99 regression >1% at canary should block rollout unless explicitly approved by Runtime + SRE.

If we hit these gates, I’m comfortable proceeding.

Neha Kapoor
VP Infrastructure & Reliability

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

1) Cardinality policy is non-negotiable:
   - allowlist labels only; no “free-form route name”, no tenant_id, no request_id
   - route_id/model_version_id must be normalized (single source of truth from control plane)
   - prompt-length/token-count must be bucketed (per standards)

2) Ingestion needs an explicit “drop detection” signal.
   We need to know when guards are dropping series so dashboards don’t show false “0 hits”. Please make that part of ENG-4823/4825 acceptance.

Logan/Nadia will own schema enforcement; Caleb will implement the guardrails.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform
