source.subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
source.body:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 6:48 PM PT
Subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Beta_Enablement_Plan_v0.3.pdf; RolloutPlan_Migration_Checklist_v0.2.txt

All — sharing the proposed release plan for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts in prod for beta accounts/pools.

High-level goal: land schema + control plane + routing + telemetry stage tags safely (feature flagged), then enable in a single internal Dedicated pool as a prod canary, then expand to 1–2 beta customers.

Proposed sequence (prod):

0) Preconditions (before any prod enablement)
- Confirm the following are in the release train (or already deployed):
  - DB model + migrations: PR-28411
  - Promote/abort + audit hooks: PR-28458
  - Policy engine gate eval v1 (missing data behavior + caching): PR-28476
  - Routing split-by-stage: PR-28503
  - Stage tags in metrics/traces: PR-28521

1) Deploy (flags OFF) — Mon 3/3 AM PT
- Deploy control plane + routing changes with staged rollout feature flags disabled by default.
- Run additive DB migrations (no destructive changes).
- Run backfill job for any existing “pool rollouts” config to DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan rows (where applicable). This is idempotent.

2) Canary enablement — Mon 3/3 PM PT
- Enable on 1 internal Dedicated pool (single-region if possible; if not, one region at a time).
- Only allow manual gates for first 24h (operators promote stages; gate evidence still computed/displayed).
- Watch dashboard: p95/p99, error rate, 429/drop, saturation/headroom, OOM, GPU health.

3) Beta enablement (limited) — Wed 3/5
- Enable for 1 design partner account + 1 internal “high QPS” pool.
- Keep “force-promote/hold” admin-only.
- Require SRE sign-off before enabling multi-region pools.

Feature flags (current naming proposal; can adjust if needed)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled (master switch)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_allow_accountlist (scoping gate)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_stage_tags_enabled (telemetry labeling)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_auto_gate_eval_enabled (auto-pass/auto-fail)

Canary success criteria (first 24h)
- No sustained increase in 429/drop attributable to partitioning (queue depth stable).
- No cardinality regressions in metrics (telemetry cost stable; label allowlist holding).
- Promote/abort actions idempotent under retries (no duplicate stage transitions).

Rollback plan (fast)
- Flip OFF dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled + stage_tags_enabled (immediate effect on routing + telemetry labeling).
- Abort any active rollout plans (API supports abort; routing reverts to baseline “stable” partition).
- No DB rollback planned (additive schema). If needed, we can disable codepaths and leave rows unused.

Open items / asks
- Priya: confirm OK with “additive migration + no down-migration” posture for beta.
- Rafael: confirm alerting/oncall routing for gate failures during canary (page vs ticket).
- Lauren/Marissa: confirm beta account list + whether customer admins see “auto gate eval” initially or we keep manual-only for beta week 1.

Attachment has more detail on sequencing + command snippets.

Thanks,
Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Looks good overall.

Two questions/concerns from the platform side:
1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions (old control plane still running) for at least one deploy window. If the new tables are required for reads, we should have a “dual-read” or “read-old-if-missing” behavior.

2) Promotion idempotency: for promote/abort we should be using idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency (etag / version) so the Console can retry safely. I want to avoid “double promote” when there’s an API timeout.

If both are already addressed in PR-28458, then 👍.

Also: for rollback, please explicitly call out what happens to in-flight streams (pinned vs reroute). Even if we “don’t reroute,” we need to make sure we aren’t killing connections.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Engineering Sponsor, Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 9:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

+1 on the plan.

From SRE side, I want to make sure the canary runbook is explicit on:

- Gate eval behavior under missing/late telemetry. Are we failing closed (i.e., gate = UNKNOWN => block promotion) or failing open? My preference is fail-closed with an admin override, but we need it documented.

- Alert routing during canary:
  - If we see 429/queue depth spike that’s clearly tied to partitioning, that should page Fleet/SRE immediately.
  - If a gate fails (latency/error) but traffic is low (1%), that’s a ticket unless customer impact is obvious.

- “Disable telemetry stage tags” needs to be its own rollback lever (cardinality risk). If we have to shut tags off, does the Console gate evidence panel degrade gracefully?

If you can add a short section to the checklist for: what to capture for post-incident review (links to dashboards, rollout plan ID, stage ID, timing), that will help.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 2:17 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Thanks — replies inline.

On Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions

Confirmed.
- Schema is additive (new tables + indexes). No existing tables modified.
- Read paths remain compatible if the rollout plan rows don’t exist yet: control plane treats “no plan” as “no staged rollout enabled for pool.”
- Backfill job can run after deploy; it only creates plans where a pool has explicitly opted-in via the new flag.

> 2) Promotion idempotency

PR-28458 includes:
- Idempotency key header supported on promote/abort.
- Optimistic concurrency on plan_version (reject if stale; Console retries after refresh).

> for rollback, what happens to in-flight streams

Current behavior: in-flight streaming requests are pinned to the selected partition at request start; disabling flags affects new requests only. No connection termination as part of rollback.

Rafael — on gate behavior + alerts:
- Missing/late telemetry: we are FAIL-CLOSED for auto-gate eval (gate status = UNKNOWN blocks auto-promotion). Manual promotion is still possible with admin override, which emits an audit event + requires reason.
- Stage tags rollback: yes, stage_tags_enabled is a separate lever. Console should show “Evidence unavailable (telemetry tags disabled)” rather than an empty panel.

I’ll update the checklist doc to include:
- What to capture (plan_id, stage_id, region, timestamps, dashboard links, last 50 routing decisions sample if available).
- Paging thresholds for 429/queue spike during canary.

Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 6:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Confirming beta list proposal (assuming canary is clean by EOD Mon):

1) Internal Dedicated pool: “dedicated-dogfood-us-east” (high-ish QPS, single region)
2) Design partner #1: Lattice AI (Dedicated; asked specifically for phased upgrades + audit trail)

For customer-facing exposure:
- Week 1 beta: keep auto gate eval OFF; manual-only promotion with gate evidence shown.
- Week 2: enable auto gate eval on Lattice AI after we validate telemetry lag behavior in the wild.

Marissa — we should make sure Console copy is clear that “UNKNOWN” means “insufficient data,” and that promotion requires an override.

Connor, please add a comms bullet to the plan: CS/TAM instructions for beta (who to contact if a gate fails, etc.).

Lauren

—
Lauren Bishop
Product Lead, Dedicated
Redwood Inference


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Ack on the beta list + sequencing.

Updates I made this morning (re-attaching updated checklist text in the same filename; Gmail won’t diff, sorry):
- Added explicit “Mixed-version safety” notes + backfill ordering.
- Added “Telemetry lag / UNKNOWN gate” operator guidance.
- Added paging thresholds for canary:
  - Page if 429 rate > baseline +0.5% sustained 5m OR queue depth p95 doubles vs baseline
  - Ticket for isolated gate failures at 1% stage unless correlated with customer impact
- Added “CS/TAM comms” section: who to ping + how to explain hold/abort/override semantics to customer admins.

Next step: I’ll schedule a 20-min go/no-go tomorrow 10:30am PT (after we get first canary metrics from today). Invite coming separately.

Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

title: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 6:48 PM PT
Subject: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback
Attachments: Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Beta_Enablement_Plan_v0.3.pdf; RolloutPlan_Migration_Checklist_v0.2.txt

All — sharing the proposed release plan for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts in prod for beta accounts/pools.

High-level goal: land schema + control plane + routing + telemetry stage tags safely (feature flagged), then enable in a single internal Dedicated pool as a prod canary, then expand to 1–2 beta customers.

Proposed sequence (prod):

0) Preconditions (before any prod enablement)
- Confirm the following are in the release train (or already deployed):
  - DB model + migrations: PR-28411
  - Promote/abort + audit hooks: PR-28458
  - Policy engine gate eval v1 (missing data behavior + caching): PR-28476
  - Routing split-by-stage: PR-28503
  - Stage tags in metrics/traces: PR-28521

1) Deploy (flags OFF) — Mon 3/3 AM PT
- Deploy control plane + routing changes with staged rollout feature flags disabled by default.
- Run additive DB migrations (no destructive changes).
- Run backfill job for any existing “pool rollouts” config to DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan rows (where applicable). This is idempotent.

2) Canary enablement — Mon 3/3 PM PT
- Enable on 1 internal Dedicated pool (single-region if possible; if not, one region at a time).
- Only allow manual gates for first 24h (operators promote stages; gate evidence still computed/displayed).
- Watch dashboard: p95/p99, error rate, 429/drop, saturation/headroom, OOM, GPU health.

3) Beta enablement (limited) — Wed 3/5
- Enable for 1 design partner account + 1 internal “high QPS” pool.
- Keep “force-promote/hold” admin-only.
- Require SRE sign-off before enabling multi-region pools.

Feature flags (current naming proposal; can adjust if needed)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled (master switch)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_allow_accountlist (scoping gate)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_stage_tags_enabled (telemetry labeling)
- dedicated_staged_rollouts_auto_gate_eval_enabled (auto-pass/auto-fail)

Canary success criteria (first 24h)
- No sustained increase in 429/drop attributable to partitioning (queue depth stable).
- No cardinality regressions in metrics (telemetry cost stable; label allowlist holding).
- Promote/abort actions idempotent under retries (no duplicate stage transitions).

Rollback plan (fast)
- Flip OFF dedicated_staged_rollouts_enabled + stage_tags_enabled (immediate effect on routing + telemetry labeling).
- Abort any active rollout plans (API supports abort; routing reverts to baseline “stable” partition).
- No DB rollback planned (additive schema). If needed, we can disable codepaths and leave rows unused.

Open items / asks
- Priya: confirm OK with “additive migration + no down-migration” posture for beta.
- Rafael: confirm alerting/oncall routing for gate failures during canary (page vs ticket).
- Lauren/Marissa: confirm beta account list + whether customer admins see “auto gate eval” initially or we keep manual-only for beta week 1.

Attachment has more detail on sequencing + command snippets.

Thanks,
Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Looks good overall.

Two questions/concerns from the platform side:
1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions (old control plane still running) for at least one deploy window. If the new tables are required for reads, we should have a “dual-read” or “read-old-if-missing” behavior.

2) Promotion idempotency: for promote/abort we should be using idempotency keys + optimistic concurrency (etag / version) so the Console can retry safely. I want to avoid “double promote” when there’s an API timeout.

If both are already addressed in PR-28458, then 👍.

Also: for rollback, please explicitly call out what happens to in-flight streams (pinned vs reroute). Even if we “don’t reroute,” we need to make sure we aren’t killing connections.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Engineering Sponsor, Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 9:04 AM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

+1 on the plan.

From SRE side, I want to make sure the canary runbook is explicit on:

- Gate eval behavior under missing/late telemetry. Are we failing closed (i.e., gate = UNKNOWN => block promotion) or failing open? My preference is fail-closed with an admin override, but we need it documented.

- Alert routing during canary:
  - If we see 429/queue depth spike that’s clearly tied to partitioning, that should page Fleet/SRE immediately.
  - If a gate fails (latency/error) but traffic is low (1%), that’s a ticket unless customer impact is obvious.

- “Disable telemetry stage tags” needs to be its own rollback lever (cardinality risk). If we have to shut tags off, does the Console gate evidence panel degrade gracefully?

If you can add a short section to the checklist for: what to capture for post-incident review (links to dashboards, rollout plan ID, stage ID, timing), that will help.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE Execution Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Mar 2, 2025 at 2:17 PM PT
Subject: Re: Release plan: beta enablement (Dedicated staged rollouts) — migrations, feature flags, canary + rollback

Thanks — replies inline.

On Sat, Mar 1, 2025 at 7:26 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 1) Migration/backfill: confirm it’s truly additive and safe to run with mixed versions

Confirmed.
- Schema is additive (new tables + indexes). No existing tables modified.
- Read paths remain compatible if the rollout plan rows don’t exist yet: control plane treats “no plan” as “no staged rollout enabled for pool.”
- Backfill job can run after deploy; it only creates plans where a pool has explicitly opted-in via the new flag.

> 2) Promotion idempotency

PR-28458 includes:
- Idempotency key header supported on promote/abort.
- Optimistic concurrency on plan_version (reject if stale; Console retries after refresh).

> for rollback, what happens to in-flight streams

Current behavior: in-flight streaming requests are pinned to the selected partition at request start; disabling flags affects new requests only. No connection termination as part of rollback.

Rafael — on gate behavior + alerts:
- Missing/late telemetry: we are FAIL-CLOSED for auto-gate eval (gate status = UNKNOWN blocks auto-promotion). Manual promotion is still possible with admin override, which emits an audit event + requires reason.
- Stage tags rollback: yes, stage_tags_enabled is a separate lever. Console should show “Evidence unavailable (telemetry tags disabled)” rather than an empty panel.

I’ll update the checklist doc to include:
- What to capture (plan_id, stage_id, region, timestamps, dashboard links, last 50 routing decisions sample if available).
- Paging thresholds for 429/queue spike during canary.

Connor

—
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>

2) Design partner #1: Lattice AI (Dedicated; asked specifically for phased upgrades + audit trail)

- Week 1 beta: keep auto gate eval OFF; manual-only promotion with gate evidence shown.
- Week 2: enable auto gate eval on Lattice AI after we validate telemetry lag behavior in the wild.

source.subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification
source.body:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
Cc: NordBank Procurement <procurement@nordbank.example>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Hi Elin,

Confirming what we discussed: NordBank traffic will be served in EU (eu-west) under normal operations. We can support a *time-bounded exception* to allow failover serving in the US region if the EU region is degraded or during scheduled DR test windows.

Key point: we have payload logging disabled for your tenant, so we do not persist prompts/responses as logs. During failover, operational metrics (request ids, timings, error rates) may be processed in the failover region.

We’re drafting a short exception memo for your procurement file.

Thanks,
Camila

—
From: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 6:02 PM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Thanks. Please include: duration, approved regions, and confirmation about logging/retention. Procurement needs it signed off by your security/legal.

—
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, NordBank Procurement <procurement@nordbank.example>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 1:47 PM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Elin,

Attached is the exception memo (Redwood-Data-Residency-Exception-NordBank-2025-02-18.pdf). It documents scope (out-of-region serving only), duration (through 2025-05-31), and the controls (no payload logging for your tenant).

Regards,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

—
From: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 3:02 PM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Received, thank you. We’ll proceed with the next step in our review.

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
Cc: NordBank Procurement <procurement@nordbank.example>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Hi Elin,

Confirming what we discussed: NordBank traffic will be served in EU (eu-west) under normal operations. We can support a *time-bounded exception* to allow failover serving in the US region if the EU region is degraded or during scheduled DR test windows.

Key point: we have payload logging disabled for your tenant, so we do not persist prompts/responses as logs. During failover, operational metrics (request ids, timings, error rates) may be processed in the failover region.

We’re drafting a short exception memo for your procurement file.

Thanks,
Camila

—
From: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 6:02 PM

Thanks. Please include: duration, approved regions, and confirmation about logging/retention. Procurement needs it signed off by your security/legal.

—

Elin,

Attached is the exception memo (Redwood-Data-Residency-Exception-NordBank-2025-02-18.pdf). It documents scope (out-of-region serving only), duration (through 2025-05-31), and the controls (no payload logging for your tenant).

Regards,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

—

source.subject: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)
source.body:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:18 AM PT
Subject: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

Team — we need to lock the behavior for guardrails in beta/GA so Console copy + backend semantics match.

Question: when a guardrail triggers (e.g., p99 latency regression, error rate spike, cost/token blowup), do we automatically stop the experiment (“hard-stop”), or do we mark as degraded + notify and require operator action (“soft-stop”)?

Proposal (recommended):
1) Initial release (beta + GA): SOFT-STOP only
- System evaluates guardrails continuously and sets experiment.status = DEGRADED when triggered
- Console banner + notifications (email + Slack integration later) with “Stop experiment” CTA
- No routing behavior change automatically
- Audit event emitted with metric + threshold that tripped

2) Follow-on (post-GA, optional): HARD-STOP as an opt-in per experiment
- Behind an account-level feature flag + global kill switch
- Default off; requires explicit opt-in in the creation wizard
- If triggered, transitions experiment to STOPPING -> STOPPED, and routing reverts to control / pre-experiment policy

Rationale for soft-stop first:
- We don’t yet have enough operational confidence in thresholds and attribution stability to terminate traffic automatically
- False positives are costly (we’d be flipping customer production behavior based on noisy metrics)
- Stop semantics are not instantaneous (propagation lag, cache staleness) so we can’t promise “immediate stop” anyway
- We can still be “safe” via very loud degraded state + recommended actions + runbook links

Open items if we go soft-stop first:
- What language do we put in GA docs (“guardrails detect regressions” vs “guardrails prevent regressions”)
- What is the minimal “severe regression” policy we want to support later (e.g., p99 > X% for Y min)

Attachments:
- Route-Experiments-Guardrails-Decision-Memo-2025-02-12.pdf
- Guardrails-Thresholds-Proposal-v3.csv

If everyone’s aligned, I’ll update ENG-9028 scope and we’ll keep the hard-stop work as follow-on behind a flag.

— Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

+1 to soft-stop for beta/GA.

Two SRE-specific reasons to avoid hard-stop by default:
- We’ve already seen attribution drop in a few retry/streaming paths in staging. If attribution drops, guardrails can “flip” from OK -> BAD inaccurately (or vice versa). We shouldn’t take automated action until we’re confident the signal is stable.
- Hard-stop creates a new class of incident: customers asking why an experiment stopped “by itself” (and whether control plane is making business decisions). That will be hard to support while we’re still tuning alerts + dashboards.

If/when we do hard-stop:
- Must be gated by a global kill switch (SRE-owned)
- Must write the runbook before enabling
- Must expose “reason” in Console (which metric, window, threshold, and the underlying data freshness)

Sean
Sean Gallagher
Head of SRE
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

Aligned.

From a product/expectations standpoint, we should be explicit:
- “Guardrails detect regressions and alert operators” (GA)
- We should avoid promising “automatic rollback” in GA launch messaging.

Console UX:
- We’ll show a degraded status with recommended actions and link out to the runbook
- We’ll keep “Auto-stop” copy out of the wizard for GA unless the backend semantics are actually there

I’ll update the PRD language today once decision is final.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Product Lead, Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 11:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

+1 soft-stop first.

One more angle: metric freshness / propagation lag.
- If aggregation jobs are delayed (or configs propagate slowly), the guardrail evaluator can compare mismatched windows.
- In that world, auto-stop can create “yo-yo” behavior: stop, restart, stop again.

If we add hard-stop later, please require:
- minimum evaluation window + cool-down (no repeated stop/start churn)
- explicit kill switch
- minimum data quality checks (attribution drop rate under threshold)

Priya
Priya Natarajan
Observability & Reliability
Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 1:26 PM PT
Subject: Re: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

Evelyn — quick exec sign-off ask so we can lock GA semantics.

Decision proposal:
- Beta + GA: guardrails are SOFT-STOP (degraded state + notifications + operator stop action). No automatic stopping/rollback.
- Follow-on: optional HARD-STOP (opt-in per experiment) behind account flag + global kill switch; requires SRE runbook + data quality gating.

We’ll ensure the UI/Docs say “detect + alert” (not “automatic rollback”) for GA.

Can you reply with approval / any changes?

— Dylan


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 2:07 PM PT
Subject: Re: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

Approved as written.

Let’s ship soft-stop in GA, with clear degraded status + audit trail and strong operator guidance.

For hard-stop: I’m supportive, but only as an explicitly opt-in capability with an SRE-owned kill switch and after we’ve demonstrated stable attribution + low-noise thresholds with beta customers.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
VP Product
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Northstar Bank  confirmation on inference log retention
source.content:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Ellis <jellis@northstarbank.example>
Cc: Maya Chen <mchen@northstarbank.example>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Re: Northstar Bank  confirmation on inference log retention

Jordan  confirming what we can support for Dedicated in us-east:

- Prompts/completions (payload): not persisted (0 days retention)
- Request metadata (no payload text): 30 days retention

Well keep aggregated metrics (no request identifiers) for longer-term operational reporting.

If this matches your requirement, reply confirmed and well proceed with the config change and document the exception.

 Aisha

---
From: Jordan Ellis <jellis@northstarbank.example>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Northstar Bank  confirmation on inference log retention

Confirmed. Thanks for being explicit about payload vs metadata.

Jordan

---
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:18 AM
Subject: Re: Northstar Bank  confirmation on inference log retention

Please ensure this is reflected in the exception register and the INT ticket links this thread for evidence.

Priya

---
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 2:02 PM
Subject: Re: Northstar Bank  confirmation on inference log retention

Will do. Linked to INT-1842 and updating the register today.

source.subject: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review
source.body:
From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Team,

I need an exec-level update on RBAC v2 today for the Q1 enterprise readiness review.

Please reply-all with:
1) Current timeline (discovery/design/implementation/rollout) and what is at risk.
2) Top migration risks (Hosted + Dedicated + Private), including likelihood/impact.
3) Compliance implications: what we can credibly claim for SOC 2 / enterprise questionnaires once v2 ships, and what remains “in progress.”
4) Concrete actions (owner + date) to reduce risk, especially deny-by-default rollout + break-glass controls + auditability.

Keep it crisp (bullet points are fine). If there’s something you need from me (resourcing, prioritization, sequencing), ask directly.

Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 9:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Aisha + team — status + timeline as of this morning.

Timeline (current plan)
- Discovery & inventory (in progress; week 2/3)
  - Output: canonical permission inventory + endpoint coverage report + naming conventions
  - Tracking: Linear ENG-18241 (inventory) + shared coverage spreadsheet
- Design (starts next week; 3–5 weeks)
  - Output: RBAC v2 design overview + ADRs + audit log schema updates
  - Tracking: PM-642 (PRD + success metrics)
- Implementation (6–10 weeks, overlaps with design)
  - Shared authz library + gateway middleware + endpoint annotations + policy bundle + Console role mgmt MVP
- Rollout & validation (4–8 weeks)
  - Dogfood + pilot accounts (1 Private, 1 Dedicated) behind flags, then staged rollout

Key milestones (targets)
- Permission catalog v1 freeze for implementation: Feb 21
- RBAC v2 design sign-off: Mar 14
- First end-to-end (gateway -> control plane -> audit logging) in staging: Apr 11
- Pilot enablement start: late April / early May (dependent on inventory coverage)

What’s at risk
- Inventory completeness / coverage gates: we’re finding endpoints without consistent checks; needs quick remediation ownership.
- Console role management MVP scope: we need to keep MVP tight (templates + bindings + permission viewer/search) to avoid slipping.
- Private installer bootstrap details: scoping + initial admin bindings need alignment with customer IdP constraints.

Requested decisions from execs (Aisha/Priya)
- Confirm we will ship with deny-by-default on all annotated endpoints, with a staged flag plan (dual-read) to avoid outages.
- Confirm break-glass workflow requirement (time-bound elevation + alerting + post-use review) is non-negotiable for enterprise launch.

I can circulate a 1-pager with the above + owners by EOD.

Talia
Program Manager


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Adding migration risks + mitigations (security eng view). Top 6:

1) “Silent allow” gaps (missing annotations or bypass routes)
- Likelihood: Medium (based on early endpoint audit)
- Impact: High (least-privilege claims undermined)
- Mitigation:
  - Coverage gate in CI: fail if security-relevant endpoints are unannotated (owner: Ethan/Benji, target: Mar 7)
  - Regression tests for critical actions (owner: Leila, target: Mar 21)
  - Explicit hot-path review for streaming routes (we already saw one weak spot in prior work)

2) Deny spikes during migration (customer-facing 403s)
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High (outage-like symptoms + support load)
- Mitigation:
  - Dual-read mode + metrics comparison (RBAC v1 vs v2 decisions) (owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan, target: Mar 28)
  - “Why denied” admin guidance + denial reason codes (owner: Felix/Chloe + Naomi for copy, target: Apr 4)

3) Stale authz decisions due to caching
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium/High (privilege persistence after role change)
- Mitigation:
  - Short TTL + scoped cache keys + invalidate-on-binding-change hooks (owner: Jordan, target: Apr 4)
  - Metrics: cache hit rate + decision age + deny/allow drift (owner: Logan, target: Apr 11)

4) Scope model confusion (org vs project vs env vs cluster)
- Likelihood: High (design still in flux)
- Impact: Medium (overprivileged defaults or blocking workflows)
- Mitigation:
  - Keep v2 scope primitives limited (org + project + environment; cluster only for Private admin) (owner: Naomi + Ines, target: Mar 14)
  - Publish a single scope matrix for top workflows (routing policy edits, audit export, key mgmt) (owner: Naomi/Talia, target: Mar 14)

5) Break-glass misuse / lack of monitoring
- Likelihood: Low/Medium
- Impact: High
- Mitigation:
  - Time-bound elevation, approvals, alerting to SecOps, and mandatory post-use review (owner: Fiona + Marcus, target: Mar 21)

6) Audit log “authz decision” fields incomplete/inconsistent across services
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High for enterprise evidence + investigations
- Mitigation:
  - Schema update + exporter mapping (owner: Kevin, target: Apr 11)
  - Require decision context on deny + allow for sensitive actions (owner: Allison, target: Apr 18)

If helpful, I can turn the above into a risk register format with likelihood/impact scoring + contingency plans.

Marcus
Security Engineering Lead


---

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 11:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Compliance/evidence implications (what we can say vs what’s still “in progress”):

What RBAC v2 enables us to credibly claim (once rolled out to Hosted + Dedicated + Private control plane)
- Least privilege: fine-grained permissions tied to concrete product actions (key mgmt, role mgmt, routing policy edits, audit export, retention settings, private deployment admin ops).
- Deny-by-default enforcement: consistent middleware/interceptor behavior; no implicit “admin can do everything” shortcuts beyond explicit break-glass.
- Separation of duties: built-in role templates (e.g., Security Admin vs Deployment Operator vs Billing Admin vs Read-only Auditor) with explicit permission matrices.
- Auditability of access decisions: audit log entries include subject, action, scope, decision allow/deny, denial reason code, and policy version.
- Evidence artifacts: versioned permission catalog + policy bundle release artifacts + CI coverage reports + integration tests demonstrating enforcement.

What will remain “in progress” / not claimed for v2
- Customer-defined ABAC/policy scripting (out of scope)
- Perfectly granular data-plane action scoping for all runtime inference requests (we’re focusing on control plane + admin actions first)
- Long-tail legacy endpoints not classified as security-relevant until inventory completes (we’ll be explicit about phased coverage)

SOC 2 / questionnaire packaging (Olga/Daniel, please sanity check)
- We should have a standard evidence packet: design overview + ADRs, permission inventory, role template matrix, audit log schema example (allow + deny), break-glass SOP, test coverage summary.
- We should pre-write answers for “how do you ensure authorization is enforced?” and “how do you detect/handle unauthorized access attempts?” including monitoring/alerts.

Open question for Aisha/Priya
- Do we want to require audit log export permissions be separate from “read audit logs” (to prevent bulk export by default)? I recommend yes for regulated accounts.

Naomi
PM, Security & Compliance


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 1:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Agree with the plan and the non-negotiables (deny-by-default + break-glass controls + auditable decisions).

Resourcing / sequencing commitments from Platform:
- Gateway enforcement + control plane endpoint annotations are prioritized over new feature work through March.
- We will staff a “coverage strike team” for the endpoint audit remediation (Benji + Brian + service owners); goal is to eliminate high-risk unannotated admin endpoints before pilots.
- We’ll treat Private bootstrap/admin binding flows as a first-class deliverable (not an afterthought). Vivek’s team will get weekly checkpoints.

On Naomi’s question: yes, we should split “read audit logs” from “export audit logs” and have export default to Security Admin + Auditor only (not Deployment Operator).

If we run into a timeline slip, I’d rather cut Console role editor polish than compromise on enforcement coverage + logging.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 2:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Thanks — this is the right framing. Capturing action items here so we can track them cleanly.

Decisions (confirmed)
- Deny-by-default is required for all security-relevant control plane endpoints (staged via feature flags/dual-read as needed).
- Break-glass must be time-bound + monitored + reviewed.
- Separate permission for audit log export vs view.

Action items
1) Executive 1-pager (timeline + risks + mitigations)
- Owner: Talia
- Due: Feb 7 EOD

2) CI coverage gate for endpoint annotations (no unannotated sensitive endpoints)
- Owner: Ethan/Benji (with Marcus reviewing the initial scope)
- Due: Mar 7

3) Dual-read + comparison metrics dashboard (RBAC v1 vs v2 decision drift)
- Owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan + Logan for metrics
- Due: Mar 28

4) Break-glass SOP + alerting wired to SecOps + post-use review checklist
- Owner: Fiona + Marcus
- Due: Mar 21

5) Audit log schema updates + exporter mapping + example events (allow + deny)
- Owner: Kevin (schema/export) + Allison (standards/consistency)
- Due: Apr 11 (schema/export), Apr 18 (standards requirement)

6) Evidence packet outline for SOC 2 / enterprise questionnaires (what we’ll ship + what’s phased)
- Owner: Olga + Daniel + Naomi
- Due: Feb 21

Connor — please ensure release planning includes explicit rollback steps for deny spikes and clear comms runbook for pilots.

I’ll use this thread to brief leadership. If anything materially changes (timeline slip, critical security gap), escalate same-day.

Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference

---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Adding migration risks + mitigations (security eng view). Top 6:

1) “Silent allow” gaps (missing annotations or bypass routes)
- Likelihood: Medium (based on early endpoint audit)
- Impact: High (least-privilege claims undermined)
- Mitigation:
  - Coverage gate in CI: fail if security-relevant endpoints are unannotated (owner: Ethan/Benji, target: Mar 7)
  - Regression tests for critical actions (owner: Leila, target: Mar 21)
  - Explicit hot-path review for streaming routes (we already saw one weak spot in prior work)

2) Deny spikes during migration (customer-facing 403s)
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High (outage-like symptoms + support load)
- Mitigation:
  - Dual-read mode + metrics comparison (RBAC v1 vs v2 decisions) (owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan, target: Mar 28)
  - “Why denied” admin guidance + denial reason codes (owner: Felix/Chloe + Naomi for copy, target: Apr 4)

3) Stale authz decisions due to caching
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium/High (privilege persistence after role change)
- Mitigation:
  - Short TTL + scoped cache keys + invalidate-on-binding-change hooks (owner: Jordan, target: Apr 4)
  - Metrics: cache hit rate + decision age + deny/allow drift (owner: Logan, target: Apr 11)

4) Scope model confusion (org vs project vs env vs cluster)
- Likelihood: High (design still in flux)
- Impact: Medium (overprivileged defaults or blocking workflows)
- Mitigation:
  - Keep v2 scope primitives limited (org + project + environment; cluster only for Private admin) (owner: Naomi + Ines, target: Mar 14)
  - Publish a single scope matrix for top workflows (routing policy edits, audit export, key mgmt) (owner: Naomi/Talia, target: Mar 14)

5) Break-glass misuse / lack of monitoring
- Likelihood: Low/Medium
- Impact: High
- Mitigation:
  - Time-bound elevation, approvals, alerting to SecOps, and mandatory post-use review (owner: Fiona + Marcus, target: Mar 21)

6) Audit log “authz decision” fields incomplete/inconsistent across services
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High for enterprise evidence + investigations
- Mitigation:
  - Schema update + exporter mapping (owner: Kevin, target: Apr 11)
  - Require decision context on deny + allow for sensitive actions (owner: Allison, target: Apr 18)

If helpful, I can turn the above into a risk register format with likelihood/impact scoring + contingency plans.

Marcus
Security Engineering Lead


---

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 11:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Compliance/evidence implications (what we can say vs what’s still “in progress”):

What RBAC v2 enables us to credibly claim (once rolled out to Hosted + Dedicated + Private control plane)
- Least privilege: fine-grained permissions tied to concrete product actions (key mgmt, role mgmt, routing policy edits, audit export, retention settings, private deployment admin ops).
- Deny-by-default enforcement: consistent middleware/interceptor behavior; no implicit “admin can do everything” shortcuts beyond explicit break-glass.
- Separation of duties: built-in role templates (e.g., Security Admin vs Deployment Operator vs Billing Admin vs Read-only Auditor) with explicit permission matrices.
- Auditability of access decisions: audit log entries include subject, action, scope, decision allow/deny, denial reason code, and policy version.
- Evidence artifacts: versioned permission catalog + policy bundle release artifacts + CI coverage reports + integration tests demonstrating enforcement.

What will remain “in progress” / not claimed for v2
- Customer-defined ABAC/policy scripting (out of scope)
- Perfectly granular data-plane action scoping for all runtime inference requests (we’re focusing on control plane + admin actions first)
- Long-tail legacy endpoints not classified as security-relevant until inventory completes (we’ll be explicit about phased coverage)

SOC 2 / questionnaire packaging (Olga/Daniel, please sanity check)
- We should have a standard evidence packet: design overview + ADRs, permission inventory, role template matrix, audit log schema example (allow + deny), break-glass SOP, test coverage summary.
- We should pre-write answers for “how do you ensure authorization is enforced?” and “how do you detect/handle unauthorized access attempts?” including monitoring/alerts.

Open question for Aisha/Priya
- Do we want to require audit log export permissions be separate from “read audit logs” (to prevent bulk export by default)? I recommend yes for regulated accounts.

Naomi
PM, Security & Compliance


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 1:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Agree with the plan and the non-negotiables (deny-by-default + break-glass controls + auditable decisions).

Resourcing / sequencing commitments from Platform:
- Gateway enforcement + control plane endpoint annotations are prioritized over new feature work through March.
- We will staff a “coverage strike team” for the endpoint audit remediation (Benji + Brian + service owners); goal is to eliminate high-risk unannotated admin endpoints before pilots.
- We’ll treat Private bootstrap/admin binding flows as a first-class deliverable (not an afterthought). Vivek’s team will get weekly checkpoints.

On Naomi’s question: yes, we should split “read audit logs” from “export audit logs” and have export default to Security Admin + Auditor only (not Deployment Operator).

If we run into a timeline slip, I’d rather cut Console role editor polish than compromise on enforcement coverage + logging.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 2:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Thanks — this is the right framing. Capturing action items here so we can track them cleanly.

Decisions (confirmed)
- Deny-by-default is required for all security-relevant control plane endpoints (staged via feature flags/dual-read as needed).
- Break-glass must be time-bound + monitored + reviewed.
- Separate permission for audit log export vs view.

Action items
1) Executive 1-pager (timeline + risks + mitigations)
- Owner: Talia
- Due: Feb 7 EOD

2) CI coverage gate for endpoint annotations (no unannotated sensitive endpoints)
- Owner: Ethan/Benji (with Marcus reviewing the initial scope)
- Due: Mar 7

3) Dual-read + comparison metrics dashboard (RBAC v1 vs v2 decision drift)
- Owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan + Logan for metrics
- Due: Mar 28

4) Break-glass SOP + alerting wired to SecOps + post-use review checklist
- Owner: Fiona + Marcus
- Due: Mar 21

5) Audit log schema updates + exporter mapping + example events (allow + deny)
- Owner: Kevin (schema/export) + Allison (standards/consistency)
- Due: Apr 11 (schema/export), Apr 18 (standards requirement)

6) Evidence packet outline for SOC 2 / enterprise questionnaires (what we’ll ship + what’s phased)
- Owner: Olga + Daniel + Naomi
- Due: Feb 21

Connor — please ensure release planning includes explicit rollback steps for deny spikes and clear comms runbook for pilots.

I’ll use this thread to brief leadership. If anything materially changes (timeline slip, critical security gap), escalate same-day.

Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference

source.body:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor


---

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead


---

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 2:57 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Ava — summarizing requested edits and asking for final approval.

Edits to incorporate into v1.0 Beta Plan:
1) Scope locked to route-level enablement only (no tenant defaults), Hosted + Dedicated.
2) Profile refs must be explicit and versioned (no “latest” alias in Beta).
3) Platform requirements:
   - RBAC limited to route admins; confirmation modal + visible diff
   - Audit events for all changes incl. actor + old/new + outcome
   - Compatibility checks + deterministic fallback to baseline
4) Runtime requirements:
   - Deterministic selection per (model, variant, profile)
   - Global kill-switch with <5 min propagation target
   - Request logs/metrics tag quant_profile_id + applied/fallback/disabled
5) Applied ML requirements:
   - Only publish profiles that pass regression thresholds
   - Explicit customer messaging: outputs can change; run your evals
   - Partner-by-partner rollback owner + criteria
6) Release gates / rollout:
   - Must-pass gates + dashboards/alerts + runbook (per Connor’s list)
   - 0.1% -> 1% -> 10% staged ramp with explicit signoff
7) Design partners:
   - Acme AI + Northstar Search as initial two; FalconBank optional if security review completes without expanding scope.

If you approve this direction, Marissa will issue Beta Plan v1.0 today and we’ll proceed with partner onboarding + execution.

Aditya


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 4:48 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Approved with the edits captured below.

Please make sure the Beta Plan v1.0 is explicit on:
- allowlist-only enablement for Beta (no self-serve without review)
- we do not expand scope to tenant defaults or auto-recommendations during Beta
- “no alias” policy for profiles in Beta (explicit version pin)

Marissa: publish v1.0 and proceed.
Connor/Priya/Mateo/Maya: treat the launch gates as hard gates; no exceptions without bringing it back here.

Ava
Executive Sponsor
Redwood Inference

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor
